Leviticus23:1 The LORD spoke to Moses:
Leviticus23:2 “Speak to the Israelites and tell them: These are my appointed times
Leviticus23:3 “Work may be done for six days
Leviticus23:4 “These are the LORD’s appointed times
Leviticus23:5 The Passover to the LORD comes in the first month
Leviticus23:6 The Festival of Unleavened Bread to the LORD is on the fifteenth day of the same month. For seven days you must eat unleavened bread.
Leviticus23:7 On the first day you are to hold a sacred assembly; you are not to do any daily work.
Leviticus23:8 You are to present a fire offering to the LORD for seven days. On the seventh day there will be a sacred assembly; do not do any daily work.”
Leviticus23:9 The LORD spoke to Moses:
Leviticus23:10 “Speak to the Israelites and tell them: When you enter the land I am giving you and reap its harvest
Leviticus23:11 He will present the sheaf before the LORD so that you may be accepted; the priest is to present it on the day after the Sabbath.
Leviticus23:12 On the day you present the sheaf
Leviticus23:13 Its grain offering is to be four quarts of fine flour mixed with oil as a fire offering to the LORD
Leviticus23:14 You must not eat bread
Leviticus23:15 “You are to count seven complete weeks starting from the day after the Sabbath
Leviticus23:16 You are to count fifty days until the day after the seventh Sabbath and then present an offering of new grain to the LORD.
Leviticus23:17 Bring two loaves of bread from your settlements as a presentation offering
Leviticus23:18 You are to present with the bread seven unblemished male lambs a year old
Leviticus23:19 You are also to prepare one male goat as a sin offering
Leviticus23:20 The priest will present the lambs with the bread of firstfruits as a presentation offering before the LORD; the bread and the two lambs will be holy to the LORD for the priest.
Leviticus23:21 On that same day you are to make a proclamation and hold a sacred assembly. You are not to do any daily work. This is to be a permanent statute wherever you live throughout your generations.
Leviticus23:22 When you reap the harvest of your land
Leviticus23:23 The LORD spoke to Moses:
Leviticus23:24 “Tell the Israelites: In the seventh month
Leviticus23:25 You must not do any daily work
Leviticus23:26 The LORD again spoke to Moses:
Leviticus23:27 “The tenth day of this seventh month is the Day of Atonement. You are to hold a sacred assembly and practice self-denial; you are to present a fire offering to the LORD.
Leviticus23:28 On this particular day you are not to do any work
Leviticus23:29 If any person does not practice self-denial on this particular day
Leviticus23:30 I will destroy among his people anyone who does any work on this same day.
Leviticus23:31 You are not to do any work. This is a permanent statute throughout your generations wherever you live.
Leviticus23:32 It will be a Sabbath of complete rest for you
Leviticus23:33 The LORD spoke to Moses:
Leviticus23:34 “Tell the Israelites: The Festival of Shelters to the LORD begins on the fifteenth day of this seventh month and continues for seven days.
Leviticus23:35 There is to be a sacred assembly on the first day; you are not to do any daily work.
Leviticus23:36 You are to present a fire offering to the LORD for seven days. On the eighth day you are to hold a sacred assembly and present a fire offering to the LORD. It is a solemn gathering; you are not to do any daily work.
Leviticus23:37 “These are the LORD’s appointed times that you are to proclaim as sacred assemblies for presenting fire offerings to the LORD
Leviticus23:38 These are in addition to the offerings for the LORD’s Sabbaths
Leviticus23:39 “You are to celebrate the LORD’s festival on the fifteenth day of the seventh month for seven days after you have gathered the produce of the land. There will be complete rest on the first day and complete rest on the eighth day.
Leviticus23:40 On the first day you are to take the product of majestic trees — palm fronds
Leviticus23:41 You are to celebrate it as a festival to the LORD seven days each year. This is a permanent statute for you throughout your generations; celebrate it in the seventh month.
Leviticus23:42 You are to live in shelters for seven days. All the native-born of Israel must live in shelters
Leviticus23:43 so that your generations may know that I made the Israelites live in shelters when I brought them out of the land of Egypt; I am the LORD your God.”
Leviticus23:44 So Moses declared the LORD’s appointed times to the Israelites.

Leviticus24:3 Aaron is to tend it continually from evening until morning before the LORD outside the curtain of the testimony in the tent of meeting. This is a permanent statute throughout your generations.
Leviticus24:4 He must continually tend the lamps on the pure gold lampstand in the LORD’s presence.
Leviticus24:5 “Take fine flour and bake it into twelve loaves; each loaf is to be made with four quarts.
Leviticus24:6 Arrange them in two rows
Leviticus24:7 Place pure frankincense near each row
Leviticus24:8 The bread is to be set out before the LORD every Sabbath day as a permanent covenant obligation on the part of the Israelites.
Leviticus24:9 It belongs to Aaron and his sons

Leviticus24:11 Her son cursed and blasphemed the Name
Leviticus24:12 They put him in custody until the LORD’s decision could be made clear to them.

Leviticus24:23 After Moses spoke to the Israelites
Leviticus25:1 The LORD spoke to Moses on Mount Sinai:
Leviticus25:2 “Speak to the Israelites and tell them: When you enter the land I am giving you
Leviticus25:3 You may sow your field for six years
Leviticus25:4 But there will be a Sabbath of complete rest for the land in the seventh year
Leviticus25:5 You are not to reap what grows by itself from your crop
Leviticus25:6 Whatever the land produces during the Sabbath year can be food for you — for yourself
Leviticus25:7 All of its growth may serve as food for your livestock and the wild animals in your land.
Leviticus25:8 “You are to count seven sabbatical years
Leviticus25:9 Then you are to sound a trumpet loudly in the seventh month
Leviticus25:10 You are to consecrate the fiftieth year and proclaim freedom in the land for all its inhabitants. It will be your Jubilee
Leviticus25:11 The fiftieth year will be your Jubilee; you are not to sow
Leviticus25:12 It is to be holy to you because it is the Jubilee; you may only eat its produce directly from the field.
Leviticus25:13 “In this Year of Jubilee
Leviticus25:14 If you make a sale to your neighbor or a purchase from him
Leviticus25:15 You are to make the purchase from your neighbor based on the number of years since the last Jubilee. He is to sell to you based on the number of remaining harvest years.
Leviticus25:16 You are to increase its price in proportion to a greater amount of years
Leviticus25:17 You are not to cheat one another
Leviticus25:18 “You are to keep my statutes and ordinances and carefully observe them
Leviticus25:19 Then the land will yield its fruit
Leviticus25:20 If you wonder: ‘What will we eat in the seventh year if we don’t sow or gather our produce?’
Leviticus25:21 I will appoint my blessing for you in the sixth year
Leviticus25:22 When you sow in the eighth year
Leviticus25:23 “The land is not to be permanently sold because it is mine
Leviticus25:24 You are to allow the redemption of any land you occupy.
Leviticus25:25 If your brother becomes destitute and sells part of his property
Leviticus25:26 If a man has no family redeemer
Leviticus25:27 he may calculate the years since its sale
Leviticus25:28 But if he cannot obtain enough to repay him
Leviticus25:29 “If a man sells a residence in a walled city
Leviticus25:30 If it is not redeemed by the end of a full year
Leviticus25:31 But houses in settlements that have no walls around them are to be classified as open fields. The right to redeem such houses stays in effect
Leviticus25:32 “Concerning the Levitical cities
Leviticus25:33 Whatever property one of the Levites can redeem  — a house sold in a city they possess — is to be released at the Jubilee
Leviticus25:34 The open pastureland around their cities may not be sold
Leviticus25:35 “If your brother becomes destitute and cannot sustain himself among you
Leviticus25:36 Do not profit or take interest from him
Leviticus25:37 You are not to lend him your silver with interest or sell him your food for profit.
Leviticus25:38 I am the LORD your God
Leviticus25:39 “If your brother among you becomes destitute and sells himself to you
Leviticus25:40 Let him stay with you as a hired worker or temporary resident; he may work for you until the Year of Jubilee.
Leviticus25:41 Then he and his children are to be released from you
Leviticus25:42 They are not to be sold as slaves
Leviticus25:43 You are not to rule over them harshly but fear your God.
Leviticus25:44 Your male and female slaves are to be from the nations around you; you may purchase male and female slaves.
Leviticus25:45 You may also purchase them from the aliens residing with you
Leviticus25:46 You may leave them to your sons after you to inherit as property; you can make them slaves for life. But concerning your brothers
Leviticus25:47 “If an alien or temporary resident living among you prospers
Leviticus25:48 he has the right of redemption after he has been sold. One of his brothers may redeem him.
Leviticus25:49 His uncle or cousin may redeem him
Leviticus25:50 The one who purchased him is to calculate the time from the year he sold himself to him until the Year of Jubilee. The price of his sale will be determined by the number of years. It will be set for him like the daily wages of a hired worker.
Leviticus25:51 If many years are still left
Leviticus25:52 If only a few years remain until the Year of Jubilee
Leviticus25:53 He will stay with him like a man hired year by year. A resident alien is not to rule over him harshly in your sight.
Leviticus25:54 If he is not redeemed in any of these ways
Leviticus25:55 For the Israelites are my servants. They are my servants that I brought out of the land of Egypt; I am the LORD your God.
Leviticus26:1 “Do not make idols for yourselves
Leviticus26:2 Keep my Sabbaths and revere my sanctuary; I am the LORD.
Leviticus26:3 “If you follow my statutes and faithfully observe my commands
Leviticus26:4 I will give you rain at the right time
Leviticus26:5 Your threshing will continue until grape harvest
Leviticus26:6 I will give peace to the land
Leviticus26:7 You will pursue your enemies
Leviticus26:8 Five of you will pursue a hundred
Leviticus26:9 “I will turn to you
Leviticus26:10 You will eat the old grain of the previous year and will clear out the old to make room for the new.
Leviticus26:11 I will place my residence among you
Leviticus26:12 I will walk among you and be your God
Leviticus26:13 I am the LORD your God
Leviticus26:14 “But if you do not obey me and observe all these commands —
Leviticus26:15 if you reject my statutes and despise my ordinances
Leviticus26:16 then I will do this to you: I will bring terror on you — wasting disease and fever that will cause your eyes to fail and your life to ebb away. You will sow your seed in vain because your enemies will eat it.
Leviticus26:17 I will turn against you
Leviticus26:18 “But if after these things you will not obey me
Leviticus26:19 I will break down your strong pride. I will make your sky like iron and your land like bronze
Leviticus26:20 and your strength will be used up for nothing. Your land will not yield its produce
Leviticus26:21 “If you act with hostility toward me and are unwilling to obey me
Leviticus26:22 I will send wild animals against you that will deprive you of your children
Leviticus26:23 “If in spite of these things you do not accept my discipline
Leviticus26:24 then I will act with hostility toward you; I also will strike you seven times for your sins.
Leviticus26:25 I will bring a sword against you to execute the vengeance of the covenant. Though you withdraw into your cities
Leviticus26:26 When I cut off your supply of bread
Leviticus26:27 “And if in spite of this you do not obey me but act with hostility toward me
Leviticus26:28 I will act with furious hostility toward you; I will also discipline you seven times for your sins.
Leviticus26:29 You will eat the flesh of your sons; you will eat the flesh of your daughters.
Leviticus26:30 I will destroy your high places
Leviticus26:31 I will reduce your cities to ruins and devastate your sanctuaries. I will not smell the pleasing aroma of your sacrifices.
Leviticus26:32 I also will devastate the land
Leviticus26:33 But I will scatter you among the nations
Leviticus26:34 “Then the land will make up for its Sabbath years during the time it lies desolate
Leviticus26:35 As long as it lies desolate
Leviticus26:36 “I will put anxiety in the hearts of those of you who survive in the lands of their enemies. The sound of a wind-driven leaf will put them to flight
Leviticus26:37 They will stumble over one another as if fleeing from a sword though no one is pursuing them. You will not be able to stand against your enemies.
Leviticus26:38 You will perish among the nations; the land of your enemies will devour you.
Leviticus26:39 Those who survive in the lands of your enemies will waste away because of their iniquity; they will also waste away because of their fathers’ iniquities along with theirs.
Leviticus26:40 “But when they confess their iniquity and the iniquity of their fathers — their unfaithfulness that they practiced against me
Leviticus26:41 and I acted with hostility toward them and brought them into the land of their enemies — and when their uncircumcised hearts are humbled and they make amends for their iniquity
Leviticus26:42 then I will remember my covenant with Jacob. I will also remember my covenant with Isaac and my covenant with Abraham
Leviticus26:43 For the land abandoned by them will make up for its Sabbaths by lying desolate without the people
Leviticus26:44 Yet in spite of this
Leviticus26:45 For their sake I will remember the covenant with their fathers
Leviticus26:46 These are the statutes
Leviticus27:1 The LORD spoke to Moses:
Leviticus27:2 “Speak to the Israelites and tell them: When someone makes a special vow to the LORD that involves the assessment of people
Leviticus27:3 if the assessment concerns a male from twenty to sixty years old
Leviticus27:4 If the person is a female
Leviticus27:5 If the person is from five to twenty years old
Leviticus27:6 If the person is from one month to five years old
Leviticus27:7 If the person is sixty years or more
Leviticus27:8 But if one is too poor to pay the assessment
Leviticus27:9 “If the vow involves one of the animals that may be brought as an offering to the LORD
Leviticus27:10 He may not replace it or make a substitution for it
Leviticus27:11 “If the vow involves any of the unclean animals that may not be brought as an offering to the LORD
Leviticus27:12 The priest will set its value
Leviticus27:13 If the one who brought it decides to redeem it
Leviticus27:14 “When a man consecrates his house as holy to the LORD
Leviticus27:15 But if the one who consecrated his house redeems it
Leviticus27:16 “If a man consecrates to the LORD any part of a field that he possesses
Leviticus27:17 If he consecrates his field during the Year of Jubilee
Leviticus27:18 But if he consecrates his field after the Jubilee
Leviticus27:19 If the one who consecrated the field decides to redeem it
Leviticus27:20 But if he does not redeem the field or if he has sold it to another man
Leviticus27:21 When the field is released in the Jubilee
Leviticus27:22 “If a person consecrates to the LORD a field he has purchased that is not part of his inherited landholding
Leviticus27:23 then the priest will calculate for him the amount of the assessment up to the Year of Jubilee
Leviticus27:24 In the Year of Jubilee the field will return to the one he bought it from
Leviticus27:25 All your assessed values will be measured by the standard sanctuary shekel
Leviticus27:26 “But no one can consecrate a firstborn of the livestock
Leviticus27:27 If it is one of the unclean livestock
Leviticus27:28 “Nothing that a man permanently sets apart to the LORD from all he owns
Leviticus27:29 No person who has been set apart for destruction is to be ransomed; he must be put to death.
Leviticus27:30 “Every tenth of the land’s produce
Leviticus27:31 If a man decides to redeem any part of this tenth
Leviticus27:32 Every tenth animal from the herd or flock
Leviticus27:33 He is not to inspect whether it is good or bad
Leviticus27:34 These are the commands the LORD gave Moses for the Israelites on Mount Sinai.
Numbers1:1 The LORD spoke to Moses in the tent of meeting in the Wilderness of Sinai
Numbers1:2 “Take a census of the entire Israelite community by their clans and their fathers’ families
Numbers1:3 You and Aaron are to register those who are twenty years old or more by their military divisions — everyone who can serve in Israel’s army.
Numbers1:4 A man from each tribe is to be with you
Numbers1:5 These are the names of the men who are to assist you: Elizur son of Shedeur from Reuben;
Numbers1:6 Shelumiel son of Zurishaddai from Simeon;
Numbers1:7 Nahshon son of Amminadab from Judah;
Numbers1:8 Nethanel son of Zuar from Issachar;
Numbers1:9 Eliab son of Helon from Zebulun;
Numbers1:10 from the sons of Joseph: Elishama son of Ammihud from Ephraim
Numbers1:11 Abidan son of Gideoni from Benjamin;
Numbers1:12 Ahiezer son of Ammishaddai from Dan;
Numbers1:13 Pagiel son of Ochran from Asher;
Numbers1:14 Eliasaph son of Deuel from Gad;
Numbers1:15 Ahira son of Enan from Naphtali.
Numbers1:16 These are the men called from the community; they are leaders of their ancestral tribes
Numbers1:17 So Moses and Aaron took these men who had been designated by name
Numbers1:18 and they assembled the whole community on the first day of the second month. They recorded their ancestry by their clans and their ancestral families
Numbers1:19 just as the LORD commanded Moses. He registered them in the Wilderness of Sinai:
Numbers1:20 The descendants of Reuben
Numbers1:21 those registered for the tribe of Reuben numbered 46
Numbers1:22 The descendants of Simeon: according to their family records by their clans and their ancestral families
Numbers1:23 those registered for the tribe of Simeon numbered 59
Numbers1:24 The descendants of Gad: according to their family records by their clans and their ancestral families
Numbers1:25 those registered for the tribe of Gad numbered 45
Numbers1:26 The descendants of Judah: according to their family records by their clans and their ancestral families
Numbers1:27 those registered for the tribe of Judah numbered 74
Numbers1:28 The descendants of Issachar: according to their family records by their clans and their ancestral families
Numbers1:29 those registered for the tribe of Issachar numbered 54
Numbers1:30 The descendants of Zebulun: according to their family records by their clans and their ancestral families
Numbers1:31 those registered for the tribe of Zebulun numbered 57
Numbers1:32 The descendants of Joseph: The descendants of Ephraim: according to their family records by their clans and their ancestral families
Numbers1:33 those registered for the tribe of Ephraim numbered 40
Numbers1:34 The descendants of Manasseh: according to their family records by their clans and their ancestral families
Numbers1:35 those registered for the tribe of Manasseh numbered 32
Numbers1:36 The descendants of Benjamin: according to their family records by their clans and their ancestral families
Numbers1:37 those registered for the tribe of Benjamin numbered 35
Numbers1:38 The descendants of Dan: according to their family records by their clans and their ancestral families
Numbers1:39 those registered for the tribe of Dan numbered 62
Numbers1:40 The descendants of Asher: according to their family records by their clans and their ancestral families
Numbers1:41 those registered for the tribe of Asher numbered 41
Numbers1:42 The descendants of Naphtali: according to their family records by their clans and their ancestral families
Numbers1:43 those registered for the tribe of Naphtali numbered 53
Numbers1:44 These are the men Moses and Aaron registered
Numbers1:45 So all the Israelites twenty years old or more
Numbers1:46 All those registered numbered 603
Numbers1:47 But the Levites were not registered with them by their ancestral tribe.
Numbers1:48 For the LORD had told Moses:
Numbers1:49 “Do not register or take a census of the tribe of Levi with the other Israelites.
Numbers1:50 Appoint the Levites over the tabernacle of the testimony
Numbers1:51 Whenever the tabernacle is to move
Numbers1:52 “The Israelites are to camp by their military divisions
Numbers1:53 The Levites are to camp around the tabernacle of the testimony and watch over it
Numbers1:54 The Israelites did everything just as the LORD had commanded Moses.
Numbers2:1 The LORD spoke to Moses and Aaron:
Numbers2:2 “The Israelites are to camp under their respective banners beside the flags of their ancestral families. They are to camp around the tent of meeting at a distance from it:
Numbers2:3 Judah’s military divisions will camp on the east side toward the sunrise under their banner. The leader of the descendants of Judah is Nahshon son of Amminadab.
Numbers2:4 His military division numbers 74
Numbers2:5 The tribe of Issachar will camp next to it. The leader of the Issacharites is Nethanel son of Zuar.
Numbers2:6 His military division numbers 54
Numbers2:7 The tribe of Zebulun will be next. The leader of the Zebulunites is Eliab son of Helon.
Numbers2:8 His military division numbers 57
Numbers2:9 The total number in their military divisions who belong to Judah’s encampment is 186
Numbers2:10 Reuben’s military divisions will camp on the south side under their banner. The leader of the Reubenites is Elizur son of Shedeur.
Numbers2:11 His military division numbers 46
Numbers2:12 The tribe of Simeon will camp next to it. The leader of the Simeonites is Shelumiel son of Zurishaddai.
Numbers2:13 His military division numbers 59
Numbers2:14 The tribe of Gad will be next. The leader of the Gadites is Eliasaph son of Deuel.
Numbers2:15 His military division numbers 45
Numbers2:16 The total number in their military divisions who belong to Reuben’s encampment is 151
Numbers2:17 The tent of meeting is to move out with the Levites’ camp
Numbers2:18 Ephraim’s military divisions will camp on the west side under their banner. The leader of the Ephraimites is Elishama son of Ammihud.
Numbers2:19 His military division numbers 40
Numbers2:20 The tribe of Manasseh will be next to it. The leader of the Manassites is Gamaliel son of Pedahzur.
Numbers2:21 His military division numbers 32
Numbers2:22 The tribe of Benjamin will be next. The leader of the Benjaminites is Abidan son of Gideoni.
Numbers2:23 His military division numbers 35
Numbers2:24 The total in their military divisions who belong to Ephraim’s encampment number 108
Numbers2:25 Dan’s military divisions will camp on the north side under their banner. The leader of the Danites is Ahiezer son of Ammishaddai.
Numbers2:26 His military division numbers 62
Numbers2:27 The tribe of Asher will camp next to it. The leader of the Asherites is Pagiel son of Ochran.
Numbers2:28 His military division numbers 41
Numbers2:29 The tribe of Naphtali will be next. The leader of the Naphtalites is Ahira son of Enan.
Numbers2:30 His military division numbers 53
Numbers2:31 The total number who belong to Dan’s encampment is 157
Numbers2:32 These are the Israelites registered by their ancestral families. The total number in the camps by their military divisions is 603
Numbers2:33 But the Levites were not registered among the Israelites
Numbers2:34 The Israelites did everything the LORD commanded Moses; they camped by their banners in this way and moved out the same way
Numbers3:1 These are the family records of Aaron and Moses at the time the LORD spoke with Moses on Mount Sinai.
Numbers3:2 These are the names of Aaron’s sons: Nadab
Numbers3:3 These are the names of Aaron’s sons
Numbers3:4 But Nadab and Abihu died in the LORD’s presence when they presented unauthorized fire before the LORD in the Wilderness of Sinai
Numbers3:5 The LORD spoke to Moses:
Numbers3:6 “Bring the tribe of Levi near and present them to the priest Aaron to assist him.
Numbers3:7 They are to perform duties for him and the entire community before the tent of meeting by attending to the service of the tabernacle.
Numbers3:8 They are to take care of all the furnishings of the tent of meeting and perform duties for the Israelites by attending to the service of the tabernacle.
Numbers3:9 Assign the Levites to Aaron and his sons; they have been assigned exclusively to him from the Israelites.
Numbers3:10 You are to appoint Aaron and his sons to carry out their priestly responsibilities
Numbers3:11 The LORD spoke to Moses:
Numbers3:12 “See
Numbers3:13 because every firstborn belongs to me. At the time I struck down every firstborn in the land of Egypt
Numbers3:14 The LORD spoke to Moses in the Wilderness of Sinai:
Numbers3:15 “Register the Levites by their ancestral families and their clans. You are to register every male one month old or more.”
Numbers3:16 So Moses registered them in obedience to the LORD as he had been commanded:
Numbers3:17 These were Levi’s sons by name: Gershon
Numbers3:18 These were the names of Gershon’s sons by their clans: Libni and Shimei.
Numbers3:19 Kohath’s sons by their clans were Amram
Numbers3:20 Merari’s sons by their clans were Mahli and Mushi. These were the Levite clans by their ancestral families.
Numbers3:21 The Libnite clan and the Shimeite clan came from Gershon; these were the Gershonite clans.
Numbers3:22 Those registered
Numbers3:23 The Gershonite clans camped behind the tabernacle on the west side
Numbers3:24 and the leader of the Gershonite families was Eliasaph son of Lael.
Numbers3:25 The Gershonites’ duties at the tent of meeting involved the tabernacle
Numbers3:26 the hangings of the courtyard
Numbers3:27 The Amramite clan
Numbers3:28 Counting every male one month old or more
Numbers3:29 The clans of the Kohathites camped on the south side of the tabernacle
Numbers3:30 and the leader of the families of the Kohathite clans was Elizaphan son of Uzziel.
Numbers3:31 Their duties involved the ark
Numbers3:32 The chief of the Levite leaders was Eleazar son of Aaron the priest; he had oversight of those responsible for the duties of the sanctuary.
Numbers3:33 The Mahlite clan and the Mushite clan came from Merari; these were the Merarite clans.
Numbers3:34 Those registered
Numbers3:35 The leader of the families of the Merarite clans was Zuriel son of Abihail; they camped on the north side of the tabernacle.
Numbers3:36 The assigned duties of Merari’s descendants involved the tabernacle’s supports
Numbers3:37 in addition to the posts of the surrounding courtyard with their bases
Numbers3:38 Moses
Numbers3:39 The total number of all the Levite males one month old or more that Moses and Aaron registered by their clans at the LORD’s command was 22
Numbers3:40 The LORD told Moses: “Register every firstborn male of the Israelites one month old or more
Numbers3:41 You are to take the Levites for me — I am the LORD  — in place of every firstborn among the Israelites
Numbers3:42 So Moses registered every firstborn among the Israelites
Numbers3:43 The total number of the firstborn males one month old or more listed by name was 22
Numbers3:44 The LORD spoke to Moses again:
Numbers3:45 “Take the Levites in place of every firstborn among the Israelites
Numbers3:46 As the redemption price for the 273 firstborn Israelites who outnumber the Levites
Numbers3:47 collect five shekels for each person
Numbers3:48 Give the silver to Aaron and his sons as the redemption price for those who are in excess among the Israelites.”
Numbers3:49 So Moses collected the redemption amount from those in excess of the ones redeemed by the Levites.
Numbers3:50 He collected the silver from the firstborn Israelites: 1
Numbers3:51 He gave the redemption silver to Aaron and his sons in obedience to the LORD
Numbers4:1 The LORD spoke to Moses and Aaron:
Numbers4:2 “Among the Levites
Numbers4:3 men from thirty years old to fifty years old — everyone who is qualified to do work at the tent of meeting.
Numbers4:4 “The service of the Kohathites at the tent of meeting concerns the most holy objects.
Numbers4:5 Whenever the camp is about to move on
Numbers4:6 They are to place over this a covering made of fine leather
Numbers4:7 “They are to spread a blue cloth over the table of the Presence and place the plates and cups on it
Numbers4:8 They are to spread a scarlet cloth over them
Numbers4:9 “They are to take a blue cloth and cover the lampstand used for light
Numbers4:10 Then they are to place it with all its utensils inside a covering made of fine leather and put them on the carrying frame.
Numbers4:11 “They are to spread a blue cloth over the gold altar
Numbers4:12 They are to take all the serving utensils they use in the sanctuary
Numbers4:13 “They are to remove the ashes from the bronze altar
Numbers4:14 and place all the equipment on it that they use in serving: the firepans
Numbers4:15 “Aaron and his sons are to finish covering the holy objects and all their equipment whenever the camp is to move on. The Kohathites will come and carry them
Numbers4:16 “Eleazar
Numbers4:17 Then the LORD spoke to Moses and Aaron:
Numbers4:18 “Do not allow the Kohathite tribal clans to be wiped out from the Levites.
Numbers4:19 Do this for them so that they may live and not die when they come near the most holy objects: Aaron and his sons are to go in and assign each man his task and transportation duty.
Numbers4:20 The Kohathites are not to go in and look at the holy objects as they are covered or they will die.”
Numbers4:21 The LORD spoke to Moses:
Numbers4:22 “Take a census of the Gershonites also
Numbers4:23 Register men from thirty years old to fifty years old
Numbers4:24 This is the service of the Gershonite clans regarding work and transportation duties:
Numbers4:25 They are to transport the tabernacle curtains
Numbers4:26 the hangings of the courtyard
Numbers4:27 “All the service of the Gershonites
Numbers4:28 This is the service of the Gershonite clans at the tent of meeting
Numbers4:29 “As for the Merarites
Numbers4:30 Register men from thirty years old to fifty years old
Numbers4:31 This is what they are responsible to carry as the whole of their service at the tent of meeting: the supports of the tabernacle
Numbers4:32 the posts of the surrounding courtyard with their bases
Numbers4:33 This is the service of the Merarite clans regarding all their work at the tent of meeting
Numbers4:34 So Moses
Numbers4:35 men from thirty years old to fifty years old
Numbers4:36 The men registered by their clans numbered 2
Numbers4:37 These were the registered men of the Kohathite clans
Numbers4:38 The Gershonites were registered by their clans and their ancestral families
Numbers4:39 men from thirty years old to fifty years old
Numbers4:40 The men registered by their clans and their ancestral families numbered 2
Numbers4:41 These were the registered men of the Gershonite clans. At the LORD’s command Moses and Aaron registered everyone who could serve at the tent of meeting.
Numbers4:42 The men of the Merarite clans were registered by their clans and their ancestral families
Numbers4:43 those from thirty years old to fifty years old
Numbers4:44 The men registered by their clans numbered 3
Numbers4:45 These were the registered men of the Merarite clans; Moses and Aaron registered them at the LORD’s command through Moses.
Numbers4:46 Moses
Numbers4:47 from thirty years old to fifty years old
Numbers4:48 Their registered men numbered 8
Numbers4:49 At the LORD’s command they were registered under the direction of Moses
Numbers5:1 The LORD instructed Moses:
Numbers5:2 “Command the Israelites to send away anyone from the camp who is afflicted with a skin disease
Numbers5:3 Send away both male or female; send them outside the camp
Numbers5:4 The Israelites did this
Numbers5:5 The LORD spoke to Moses:
Numbers5:6 “Tell the Israelites: When a man or woman commits any sin against another
Numbers5:7 The person is to confess the sin he has committed. He is to pay full compensation
Numbers5:8 But if that individual has no relative to receive compensation
Numbers5:9 Every holy contribution the Israelites present to the priest will be his.
Numbers5:10 Each one’s holy contribution is his to give; what each one gives to the priest will be his.”
Numbers5:11 The LORD spoke to Moses:
Numbers5:12 “Speak to the Israelites and tell them: If any man’s wife goes astray
Numbers5:13 and sleeps with another
Numbers5:14 and if a feeling of jealousy comes over the husband and he becomes jealous because of his wife who has defiled herself — or if a feeling of jealousy comes over him and he becomes jealous of her though she has not defiled herself —
Numbers5:15 then the man is to bring his wife to the priest. He is also to bring an offering for her of two quarts of barley flour. He is not to pour oil over it or put frankincense on it because it is a grain offering of jealousy
Numbers5:16 “The priest is to bring her forward and have her stand before the LORD.
Numbers5:17 Then the priest is to take holy water in a clay bowl
Numbers5:18 After the priest has the woman stand before the LORD
Numbers5:19 The priest will require the woman to take an oath and will say to her
Numbers5:20 But if you have gone astray while under your husband’s authority
Numbers5:21 at this point the priest will make the woman take the oath with the sworn curse
Numbers5:22 May this water that brings a curse enter your stomach
Numbers5:23 “Then the priest is to write these curses on a scroll and wash them off into the bitter water.
Numbers5:24 He will require the woman to drink the bitter water that brings a curse
Numbers5:25 The priest is to take the grain offering of jealousy from the woman
Numbers5:26 The priest is to take a handful of the grain offering as a memorial portion and burn it on the altar. Afterward
Numbers5:27 “When he makes her drink the water
Numbers5:28 But if the woman has not defiled herself and is pure
Numbers5:29 “This is the law regarding jealousy when a wife goes astray and defiles herself while under her husband’s authority
Numbers5:30 or when a feeling of jealousy comes over a husband and he becomes jealous of his wife. He is to have the woman stand before the LORD
Numbers5:31 The husband will be free of guilt
Numbers6:1 The LORD instructed Moses:
Numbers6:2 “Speak to the Israelites and tell them: When a man or woman makes a special vow
Numbers6:3 he is to abstain from wine and beer. He must not drink vinegar made from wine or from beer. He must not drink any grape juice or eat fresh grapes or raisins.
Numbers6:4 He is not to eat anything produced by the grapevine
Numbers6:5 “You must not cut his hair throughout the time of his vow of consecration. He may be holy until the time is completed during which he consecrates himself to the LORD; he is to let the hair of his head grow long.
Numbers6:6 He must not go near a dead body during the time he consecrates himself to the LORD.
Numbers6:7 He is not to defile himself for his father or mother
Numbers6:8 He is holy to the LORD during the time of consecration.
Numbers6:9 “If someone suddenly dies near him
Numbers6:10 On the eighth day he is to bring two turtledoves or two young pigeons to the priest at the entrance to the tent of meeting.
Numbers6:11 The priest is to offer one as a sin offering and the other as a burnt offering to make atonement on behalf of the Nazirite
Numbers6:12 He is to rededicate his time of consecration to the LORD and to bring a year-old male lamb as a guilt offering. But do not count the initial period of consecration because it became defiled.
Numbers6:13 “This is the law of the Nazirite: On the day his time of consecration is completed
Numbers6:14 He is to present an offering to the LORD of one unblemished year-old male lamb as a burnt offering
Numbers6:15 along with their grain offerings and drink offerings
Numbers6:16 “The priest is to present these before the LORD and sacrifice the Nazirite’s sin offering and burnt offering.
Numbers6:17 He will also offer the ram as a fellowship sacrifice to the LORD
Numbers6:18 “The Nazirite is to shave his consecrated head at the entrance to the tent of meeting
Numbers6:19 The priest is to take the boiled shoulder from the ram
Numbers6:20 The priest is to present them as a presentation offering before the LORD. It is a holy portion for the priest
Numbers6:21 “These are the instructions about the Nazirite who vows his offering to the LORD for his consecration
Numbers6:22 The LORD spoke to Moses:
Numbers6:23 “Tell Aaron and his sons
Numbers6:24 “May the LORD bless you and protect you;
Numbers6:25 may the LORD make his face shine on you and be gracious to you;
Numbers6:26 may the LORD look with favor on you and give you peace.”’
Numbers6:27 In this way they will pronounce my name over the Israelites
Numbers7:1 On the day Moses finished setting up the tabernacle
Numbers7:2 the leaders of Israel
Numbers7:3 They brought as their offering before the LORD six covered carts and twelve oxen
Numbers7:4 The LORD said to Moses
Numbers7:5 “Accept these from them to be used in the work of the tent of meeting
Numbers7:6 So Moses took the carts and oxen and gave them to the Levites.

Numbers7:8 and gave the Merarites four carts and eight oxen corresponding to their service
Numbers7:9 But he did not give any to the Kohathites

Numbers7:85 Each silver dish weighed 3¼ pounds
Numbers7:86 The twelve gold bowls full of incense each weighed four ounces measured by the standard sanctuary shekel. The total weight of the gold bowls was 3 pounds.
Numbers7:87 All the livestock for the burnt offering totaled twelve bulls
Numbers7:88 All the livestock for the fellowship sacrifice totaled twenty-four bulls
Numbers7:89 When Moses entered the tent of meeting to speak with the LORD
Numbers8:1 The LORD spoke to Moses:
Numbers8:2 “Speak to Aaron and tell him: When you set up the lamps
Numbers8:3 So Aaron did this; he set up its lamps to give light in front of the lampstand just as the LORD had commanded Moses.
Numbers8:4 This is the way the lampstand was made: it was a hammered work of gold
Numbers8:5 The LORD spoke to Moses:
Numbers8:6 “Take the Levites from among the Israelites and ceremonially cleanse them.
Numbers8:7 Do this to them for their purification: Sprinkle them with the purification water. Have them shave their entire bodies and wash their clothes
Numbers8:8 “They are to take a young bull and its grain offering of fine flour mixed with oil
Numbers8:9 Bring the Levites before the tent of meeting and assemble the entire Israelite community.
Numbers8:10 Then present the Levites before the LORD
Numbers8:11 Aaron is to present the Levites before the LORD as a presentation offering from the Israelites
Numbers8:12 Next the Levites are to lay their hands on the heads of the bulls. Sacrifice one as a sin offering and the other as a burnt offering to the LORD
Numbers8:13 “You are to have the Levites stand before Aaron and his sons
Numbers8:14 In this way you are to separate the Levites from the rest of the Israelites so that the Levites will belong to me.
Numbers8:15 After that the Levites may come to serve at the tent of meeting
Numbers8:16 For they have been exclusively assigned to me from the Israelites. I have taken them for myself in place of all who come first from the womb
Numbers8:17 For every firstborn among the Israelites is mine
Numbers8:18 But I have taken the Levites in place of every firstborn among the Israelites.
Numbers8:19 From the Israelites
Numbers8:20 Moses
Numbers8:21 The Levites purified themselves and washed their clothes; then Aaron presented them before the LORD as a presentation offering. Aaron also made atonement for them to cleanse them ceremonially.
Numbers8:22 After that
Numbers8:23 The LORD spoke to Moses:
Numbers8:24 “In regard to the Levites: From twenty-five years old or more
Numbers8:25 But at fifty years old he is to retire from his service in the work and no longer serve.
Numbers8:26 He may assist his brothers to fulfill responsibilities at the tent of meeting
Numbers9:1 In the first month of the second year after their departure from the land of Egypt
Numbers9:2 “The Israelites are to observe the Passover at its appointed time.
Numbers9:3 You must observe it at its appointed time on the fourteenth day of this month at twilight; you are to observe it according to all its statutes and ordinances.”
Numbers9:4 So Moses told the Israelites to observe the Passover
Numbers9:5 and they observed it in the first month on the fourteenth day at twilight in the Wilderness of Sinai. The Israelites did everything as the LORD had commanded Moses.
Numbers9:6 But there were some men who were unclean because of a human corpse
Numbers9:7 and said to him
Numbers9:8 Moses replied to them
Numbers9:9 Then the LORD spoke to Moses:
Numbers9:10 “Tell the Israelites: When any one of you or your descendants is unclean because of a corpse or is on a distant journey
Numbers9:11 Such people are to observe it in the second month
Numbers9:12 they may not leave any of it until morning or break any of its bones. They must observe the Passover according to all its statutes.
Numbers9:13 “But the man who is ceremonially clean
Numbers9:14 “If an alien resides with you and wants to observe the Passover to the LORD
Numbers9:15 On the day the tabernacle was set up
Numbers9:16 It remained that way continuously: the cloud would cover it
Numbers9:17 Whenever the cloud was lifted up above the tent
Numbers9:18 At the LORD’s command the Israelites set out
Numbers9:19 Even when the cloud stayed over the tabernacle many days
Numbers9:20 Sometimes the cloud remained over the tabernacle for only a few days. They would camp at the LORD’s command and set out at the LORD’s command.
Numbers9:21 Sometimes the cloud remained only from evening until morning; when the cloud lifted in the morning
Numbers9:22 Whether it was two days
Numbers9:23 They camped at the LORD’s command
Numbers10:1 The LORD spoke to Moses:
Numbers10:2 “Make two trumpets of hammered silver to summon the community and have the camps set out.

Numbers10:4 However
Numbers10:5 “When you sound short blasts
Numbers10:6 When you sound short blasts a second time
Numbers10:7 When calling the assembly together
Numbers10:8 The sons of Aaron
Numbers10:9 “When you enter into battle in your land against an adversary who is attacking you
Numbers10:10 You are to sound the trumpets over your burnt offerings and your fellowship sacrifices and on your joyous occasions
Numbers10:11 During the second year
Numbers10:12 The Israelites traveled on from the Wilderness of Sinai
Numbers10:13 They set out for the first time according to the LORD’s command through Moses.

Numbers10:15 Nethanel son of Zuar was over the division of the tribe of Issachar’s descendants
Numbers10:16 and Eliab son of Helon was over the division of the tribe of Zebulun’s descendants.

Numbers10:20 and Eliasaph son of Deuel was over the division of the tribe of Gad’s descendants.
Numbers10:21 The Kohathites then set out

Numbers10:24 and Abidan son of Gideoni was over the division of the tribe of Benjamin’s descendants.
Numbers10:25 The military divisions of the camp of Dan’s descendants with their banner set out
Numbers10:26 Pagiel son of Ochran was over the division of the tribe of Asher’s descendants
Numbers10:27 and Ahira son of Enan was over the division of the tribe of Naphtali’s descendants.
Numbers10:28 This was the order of march for the Israelites by their military divisions as they set out.
Numbers10:29 Moses said to Hobab
Numbers10:30 But he replied to him
Numbers10:31 “Please don’t leave us
Numbers10:32 If you come with us
Numbers10:33 They set out from the mountain of the LORD on a three-day journey with the ark of the LORD’s covenant traveling ahead of them for those three days to seek a resting place for them.
Numbers10:34 Meanwhile
Numbers10:35 Whenever the ark set out
Numbers10:36 When it came to rest
Numbers11:1 Now the people began complaining openly before the LORD about hardship. When the LORD heard
Numbers11:2 Then the people cried out to Moses
Numbers11:3 So that place was named Taberah
Numbers11:4 The riffraff among them had a strong craving for other food. The Israelites wept again and said
Numbers11:5 We remember the free fish we ate in Egypt
Numbers11:6 But now our appetite is gone; there’s nothing to look at but this manna!”
Numbers11:7 The manna resembled coriander seed
Numbers11:8 The people walked around and gathered it. They ground it on a pair of grinding stones or crushed it in a mortar
Numbers11:9 When the dew fell on the camp at night
Numbers11:10 Moses heard the people
Numbers11:11 So Moses asked the LORD
Numbers11:12 Did I conceive all these people? Did I give them birth so you should tell me
Numbers11:13 Where can I get meat to give all these people? For they are weeping to me
Numbers11:14 I can’t carry all these people by myself. They are too much for me.
Numbers11:15 If you are going to treat me like this
Numbers11:16 The LORD answered Moses
Numbers11:17 Then I will come down and speak with you there. I will take some of the Spirit who is on you and put the Spirit on them. They will help you bear the burden of the people
Numbers11:18 “Tell the people: Consecrate yourselves in readiness for tomorrow
Numbers11:19 You will eat
Numbers11:20 but for a whole month — until it comes out of your nostrils and becomes nauseating to you — because you have rejected the LORD who is among you
Numbers11:21 But Moses replied
Numbers11:22 If flocks and herds were slaughtered for them
Numbers11:23 The LORD answered Moses
Numbers11:24 Moses went out and told the people the words of the LORD. He brought seventy men from the elders of the people and had them stand around the tent.
Numbers11:25 Then the LORD descended in the cloud and spoke to him. He took some of the Spirit that was on Moses and placed the Spirit on the seventy elders. As the Spirit rested on them
Numbers11:26 Two men had remained in the camp
Numbers11:27 A young man ran and reported to Moses
Numbers11:28 Joshua son of Nun
Numbers11:29 But Moses asked him
Numbers11:30 Then Moses returned to the camp along with the elders of Israel.
Numbers11:31 A wind sent by the LORD came up and blew quail in from the sea; it dropped them all around the camp. They were flying three feet off the ground for about a day’s journey in every direction.
Numbers11:32 The people were up all that day and night and all the next day gathering the quail — the one who took the least gathered fifty bushels  — and they spread them out all around the camp.
Numbers11:33 While the meat was still between their teeth
Numbers11:34 So they named that place Kibroth-hattaavah
Numbers11:35 From Kibroth-hattaavah the people moved on to Hazeroth and remained there.
Numbers12:1 Miriam and Aaron criticized Moses because of the Cushite woman he married (for he had married a Cushite woman).
Numbers12:2 They said
Numbers12:3 Moses was a very humble man
Numbers12:4 Suddenly the LORD said to Moses
Numbers12:5 Then the LORD descended in a pillar of cloud
Numbers12:6 he said: “Listen to what I say: If there is a prophet among you from the LORD
Numbers12:7 Not so with my servant Moses; he is faithful in all my household.
Numbers12:8 I speak with him directly
Numbers12:9 The LORD’s anger burned against them
Numbers12:10 As the cloud moved away from the tent

Numbers12:12 Please don’t let her be like a dead baby whose flesh is half eaten away when he comes out of his mother’s womb.”
Numbers12:13 Then Moses cried out to the LORD
Numbers12:14 The LORD answered Moses
Numbers12:15 So Miriam was confined outside the camp for seven days
Numbers12:16 After that
Numbers13:1 The LORD spoke to Moses:
Numbers13:2 “Send men to scout out the land of Canaan I am giving to the Israelites. Send one man who is a leader among them from each of their ancestral tribes.”
Numbers13:3 Moses sent them from the Wilderness of Paran at the LORD’s command. All the men were leaders in Israel.
Numbers13:4 These were their names: Shammua son of Zaccur from the tribe of Reuben;
Numbers13:5 Shaphat son of Hori from the tribe of Simeon;
Numbers13:6 Caleb son of Jephunneh from the tribe of Judah;
Numbers13:7 Igal son of Joseph from the tribe of Issachar;
Numbers13:8 Hoshea son of Nun from the tribe of Ephraim;
Numbers13:9 Palti son of Raphu from the tribe of Benjamin;
Numbers13:10 Gaddiel son of Sodi from the tribe of Zebulun;
Numbers13:11 Gaddi son of Susi from the tribe of Manasseh (from the tribe of Joseph);
Numbers13:12 Ammiel son of Gemalli from the tribe of Dan;
Numbers13:13 Sethur son of Michael from the tribe of Asher;
Numbers13:14 Nahbi son of Vophsi from the tribe of Naphtali;
Numbers13:15 Geuel son of Machi from the tribe of Gad.
Numbers13:16 These were the names of the men Moses sent to scout out the land
Numbers13:17 When Moses sent them to scout out the land of Canaan
Numbers13:18 See what the land is like
Numbers13:19 Is the land they live in good or bad? Are the cities they live in encampments or fortifications?
Numbers13:20 Is the land fertile or unproductive? Are there trees in it or not? Be courageous. Bring back some fruit from the land.” It was the season for the first ripe grapes.
Numbers13:21 So they went up and scouted out the land from the Wilderness of Zin as far as Rehob near the entrance to Hamath.
Numbers13:22 They went up through the Negev and came to Hebron
Numbers13:23 When they came to the Valley of Eshcol
Numbers13:24 That place was called the Valley of Eshcol because of the cluster of grapes the Israelites cut there.
Numbers13:25 At the end of forty days they returned from scouting out the land.
Numbers13:26 The men went back to Moses
Numbers13:27 They reported to Moses: “We went into the land where you sent us. Indeed it is flowing with milk and honey
Numbers13:28 However
Numbers13:29 The Amalekites are living in the land of the Negev; the Hethites
Numbers13:30 Then Caleb quieted the people in the presence of Moses and said
Numbers13:31 But the men who had gone up with him responded
Numbers13:32 So they gave a negative report to the Israelites about the land they had scouted: “The land we passed through to explore is one that devours its inhabitants
Numbers13:33 We even saw the Nephilim there — the descendants of Anak come from the Nephilim! To ourselves we seemed like grasshoppers
Numbers14:1 Then the whole community broke into loud cries
Numbers14:2 All the Israelites complained about Moses and Aaron
Numbers14:3 Why is the LORD bringing us into this land to die by the sword? Our wives and children will become plunder. Wouldn’t it be better for us to go back to Egypt?”
Numbers14:4 So they said to one another
Numbers14:5 Then Moses and Aaron fell facedown in front of the whole assembly of the Israelite community.
Numbers14:6 Joshua son of Nun and Caleb son of Jephunneh
Numbers14:7 and said to the entire Israelite community: “The land we passed through and explored is an extremely good land.
Numbers14:8 If the LORD is pleased with us
Numbers14:9 Only don’t rebel against the LORD
Numbers14:10 While the whole community threatened to stone them
Numbers14:11 The LORD said to Moses
Numbers14:12 I will strike them with a plague and destroy them. Then I will make you into a greater and mightier nation than they are.”
Numbers14:13 But Moses replied to the LORD
Numbers14:14 They will tell it to the inhabitants of this land. They have heard that you
Numbers14:15 If you kill this people with a single blow
Numbers14:16 ‘Since the LORD wasn’t able to bring this people into the land he swore to give them
Numbers14:17 “So now
Numbers14:18 The LORD is slow to anger and abounding in faithful love
Numbers14:19 Please pardon the iniquity of this people
Numbers14:20 The LORD responded
Numbers14:21 Yet as surely as I live and as the whole earth is filled with the LORD’s glory
Numbers14:22 none of the men who have seen my glory and the signs I performed in Egypt and in the wilderness
Numbers14:23 will ever see the land I swore to give their fathers. None of those who have despised me will see it.
Numbers14:24 But since my servant Caleb has a different spirit and has remained loyal to me
Numbers14:25 Since the Amalekites and Canaanites are living in the lowlands
Numbers14:26 Then the LORD spoke to Moses and Aaron:
Numbers14:27 “How long must I endure this evil community that keeps complaining about me? I have heard the Israelites’ complaints that they make against me.
Numbers14:28 Tell them: As surely as I live — this is the LORD’s declaration — I will do to you exactly as I heard you say.
Numbers14:29 Your corpses will fall in this wilderness — all of you who were registered in the census
Numbers14:30 I swear that none of you will enter the land I promised to settle you in
Numbers14:31 I will bring your children whom you said would become plunder into the land you rejected
Numbers14:32 But as for you
Numbers14:33 Your children will be shepherds in the wilderness for forty years and bear the penalty for your acts of unfaithfulness until all your corpses lie scattered in the wilderness.
Numbers14:34 You will bear the consequences of your iniquities forty years based on the number of the forty days that you scouted the land
Numbers14:35 I
Numbers14:36 So the men Moses sent to scout out the land
Numbers14:37 those men who spread the negative report about the land were struck down by the LORD.
Numbers14:38 Only Joshua son of Nun and Caleb son of Jephunneh remained alive of those men who went to scout out the land.
Numbers14:39 When Moses reported these words to all the Israelites
Numbers14:40 They got up early the next morning and went up the ridge of the hill country
Numbers14:41 But Moses responded
Numbers14:42 Don’t go
Numbers14:43 The Amalekites and Canaanites are right in front of you
Numbers14:44 But they dared to go up the ridge of the hill country
Numbers14:45 Then the Amalekites and Canaanites who lived in that part of the hill country came down
Numbers15:1 The LORD instructed Moses:
Numbers15:2 “Speak to the Israelites and tell them: When you enter the land I am giving you to settle in
Numbers15:3 and you make a fire offering to the LORD from the herd or flock — either a burnt offering or a sacrifice
Numbers15:4 the one presenting his offering to the LORD is also to present a grain offering of two quarts of fine flour mixed with a quart of oil.
Numbers15:5 Prepare a quart of wine as a drink offering with the burnt offering or sacrifice of each lamb.
Numbers15:6 “If you prepare a grain offering with a ram
Numbers15:7 Also present a third of a gallon of wine for a drink offering as a pleasing aroma to the LORD.
Numbers15:8 “If you prepare a young bull as a burnt offering or as a sacrifice
Numbers15:9 a grain offering of six quarts of fine flour mixed with two quarts of oil is to be presented with the bull.
Numbers15:10 Also present two quarts of wine as a drink offering. It is a fire offering of pleasing aroma to the LORD.
Numbers15:11 This is to be done for each ox
Numbers15:12 This is how you are to prepare each of them
Numbers15:13 “Every Israelite is to prepare these things in this way when he presents a fire offering as a pleasing aroma to the LORD.
Numbers15:14 When an alien resides with you or someone else is among you and wants to prepare a fire offering as a pleasing aroma to the LORD
Numbers15:15 The assembly is to have the same statute for both you and the resident alien as a permanent statute throughout your generations. You and the alien will be alike before the LORD.
Numbers15:16 The same law and the same ordinance will apply to both you and the alien who resides with you.”
Numbers15:17 The LORD instructed Moses:
Numbers15:18 “Speak to the Israelites and tell them: After you enter the land where I am bringing you
Numbers15:19 you are to offer a contribution to the LORD when you eat from the food of the land.
Numbers15:20 You are to offer a loaf from your first batch of dough as a contribution; offer it just like a contribution from the threshing floor.
Numbers15:21 Throughout your generations
Numbers15:22 “When you sin unintentionally and do not obey all these commands that the LORD spoke to Moses  —
Numbers15:23 all that the LORD has commanded you through Moses
Numbers15:24 and if it was done unintentionally without the community’s awareness
Numbers15:25 The priest will then make atonement for the entire Israelite community so that they may be forgiven
Numbers15:26 The entire Israelite community and the alien who resides among them will be forgiven
Numbers15:27 “If one person sins unintentionally
Numbers15:28 The priest will then make atonement before the LORD on behalf of the person who acts in error sinning unintentionally
Numbers15:29 You are to have the same law for the person who acts in error
Numbers15:30 “But the person who acts defiantly
Numbers15:31 He will certainly be cut off
Numbers15:32 While the Israelites were in the wilderness
Numbers15:33 Those who found him gathering wood brought him to Moses
Numbers15:34 They placed him in custody because it had not been decided what should be done to him.
Numbers15:35 Then the LORD told Moses
Numbers15:36 So the entire community brought him outside the camp and stoned him to death
Numbers15:37 The LORD said to Moses
Numbers15:38 “Speak to the Israelites and tell them that throughout their generations they are to make tassels for the corners of their garments
Numbers15:39 These will serve as tassels for you to look at
Numbers15:40 This way you will remember and obey all my commands and be holy to your God.
Numbers15:41 I am the LORD your God who brought you out of the land of Egypt to be your God; I am the LORD your God.”
Numbers16:1 Now Korah son of Izhar
Numbers16:2 two hundred and fifty prominent Israelite men who were leaders of the community and representatives in the assembly
Numbers16:3 They came together against Moses and Aaron and told them
Numbers16:4 When Moses heard this
Numbers16:5 Then he said to Korah and all his followers
Numbers16:6 Korah
Numbers16:7 place fire in them and put incense on them before the LORD. Then the man the LORD chooses will be the one who is set apart. It is you Levites who have gone too far!”
Numbers16:8 Moses also told Korah
Numbers16:9 Isn’t it enough for you that the God of Israel has separated you from the Israelite community to bring you near to himself
Numbers16:10 He has brought you near
Numbers16:11 Therefore
Numbers16:12 Moses sent for Dathan and Abiram
Numbers16:13 Is it not enough that you brought us up from a land flowing with milk and honey to kill us in the wilderness? Do you also have to appoint yourself as ruler over us?
Numbers16:14 Furthermore
Numbers16:15 Then Moses became angry and said to the LORD
Numbers16:16 So Moses told Korah
Numbers16:17 Each of you is to take his firepan
Numbers16:18 Each man took his firepan
Numbers16:19 After Korah assembled the whole community against them at the entrance to the tent of meeting
Numbers16:20 The LORD spoke to Moses and Aaron
Numbers16:21 “Separate yourselves from this community so I may consume them instantly.”
Numbers16:22 But Moses and Aaron fell facedown and said
Numbers16:23 The LORD replied to Moses
Numbers16:24 “Tell the community: Get away from the dwellings of Korah
Numbers16:25 Moses got up and went to Dathan and Abiram
Numbers16:26 He warned the community
Numbers16:27 So they got away from the dwellings of Korah
Numbers16:28 Then Moses said
Numbers16:29 If these men die naturally as all people would
Numbers16:30 But if the LORD brings about something unprecedented
Numbers16:31 Just as he finished speaking all these words
Numbers16:32 The earth opened its mouth and swallowed them and their households
Numbers16:33 They went down alive into Sheol with all that belonged to them. The earth closed over them
Numbers16:34 At their cries
Numbers16:35 Fire also came out from the LORD and consumed the 250 men who were presenting the incense.
Numbers16:36 Then the LORD spoke to Moses:
Numbers16:37 “Tell Eleazar son of Aaron the priest to remove the firepans from the burning debris
Numbers16:38 As for the firepans of those who sinned at the cost of their own lives
Numbers16:39 So the priest Eleazar took the bronze firepans that those who were burned had presented
Numbers16:40 just as the LORD commanded him through Moses. It was to be a reminder for the Israelites that no unauthorized person outside the lineage of Aaron should approach to offer incense before the LORD and become like Korah and his followers.
Numbers16:41 The next day the entire Israelite community complained about Moses and Aaron
Numbers16:42 When the community assembled against them
Numbers16:43 Moses and Aaron went to the front of the tent of meeting
Numbers16:44 and the LORD said to Moses
Numbers16:45 “Get away from this community so that I may consume them instantly.” But they fell facedown.
Numbers16:46 Then Moses told Aaron
Numbers16:47 So Aaron took his firepan as Moses had ordered
Numbers16:48 He stood between the dead and the living
Numbers16:49 But those who died from the plague numbered 14
Numbers16:50 Aaron then returned to Moses at the entrance to the tent of meeting
Numbers17:1 The LORD instructed Moses:
Numbers17:2 “Speak to the Israelites and take one staff from them for each ancestral tribe
Numbers17:3 Write Aaron’s name on Levi’s staff

Numbers18:1 The LORD said to Aaron
Numbers18:2 But also bring your relatives with you from the tribe of Levi
Numbers18:3 They are to perform duties for you and for the whole tent. They must not come near the sanctuary equipment or the altar; otherwise
Numbers18:4 They are to join you and guard the tent of meeting
Numbers18:5 “You are to guard the sanctuary and the altar so that wrath may not fall on the Israelites again.
Numbers18:6 Look
Numbers18:7 But you and your sons will carry out your priestly responsibilities for everything concerning the altar and for what is inside the curtain
Numbers18:8 Then the LORD spoke to Aaron
Numbers18:9 A portion of the holiest offerings kept from the fire will be yours; every one of their offerings that they give me
Numbers18:10 You are to eat it as a most holy offering. Every male may eat it; it is to be holy to you.
Numbers18:11 “The contribution of their gifts also belongs to you. I have given all the Israelites’ presentation offerings to you and to your sons and daughters as a permanent statute. Every ceremonially clean person in your house may eat it.
Numbers18:12 I am giving you all the best of the fresh oil
Numbers18:13 The firstfruits of all that is in their land
Numbers18:14 “Everything in Israel that is permanently dedicated to the LORD belongs to you.
Numbers18:15 The firstborn of every living thing

Numbers18:17 “However
Numbers18:18 But their meat belongs to you. It belongs to you like the breast of the presentation offering and the right thigh.

Numbers18:20 The LORD told Aaron
Numbers18:21 “Look
Numbers18:22 The Israelites must never again come near the tent of meeting
Numbers18:23 The Levites will do the work of the tent of meeting
Numbers18:24 For I have given them the tenth that the Israelites present to the LORD as a contribution for their inheritance. That is why I told them that they would not receive an inheritance among the Israelites.”
Numbers18:25 The LORD instructed Moses
Numbers18:26 “Speak to the Levites and tell them: When you receive from the Israelites the tenth that I have given you as your inheritance
Numbers18:27 Your offering will be credited to you as if it were your grain from the threshing floor or the full harvest from the winepress.
Numbers18:28 You are to present an offering to the LORD from every tenth you receive from the Israelites. Give some of it to the priest Aaron as an offering to the LORD.
Numbers18:29 You must present the entire offering due the LORD from all your gifts. The best part of the tenth is to be consecrated.
Numbers18:30 “Tell them further: Once you have presented the best part of the tenth
Numbers18:31 then you and your household may eat it anywhere. It is your wage in return for your work at the tent of meeting.
Numbers18:32 You will not incur guilt because of it once you have presented the best part of it
Numbers19:1 The LORD spoke to Moses and Aaron
Numbers19:2 “This is the legal statute that the LORD has commanded: Instruct the Israelites to bring you an unblemished red cow that has no defect and has never been yoked.
Numbers19:3 Give it to the priest Eleazar
Numbers19:4 The priest Eleazar is to take some of its blood with his finger and sprinkle it seven times toward the front of the tent of meeting.
Numbers19:5 The cow is to be burned in his sight. Its hide
Numbers19:6 The priest is to take cedar wood
Numbers19:7 Then the priest must wash his clothes and bathe his body in water; after that he may enter the camp
Numbers19:8 The one who burned the cow must also wash his clothes and bathe his body in water
Numbers19:9 “A man who is clean is to gather up the cow’s ashes and deposit them outside the camp in a ceremonially clean place. The ashes will be kept by the Israelite community for preparing the water to remove impurity; it is a sin offering.
Numbers19:10 Then the one who gathers up the cow’s ashes must wash his clothes
Numbers19:11 “The person who touches any human corpse will be unclean for seven days.
Numbers19:12 He is to purify himself with the water on the third day and the seventh day; then he will be clean. But if he does not purify himself on the third and seventh days
Numbers19:13 Anyone who touches a body of a person who has died
Numbers19:14 “This is the law when a person dies in a tent: everyone who enters the tent and everyone who is already in the tent will be unclean for seven days
Numbers19:15 and any open container without a lid tied on it is unclean.
Numbers19:16 Anyone in the open field who touches a person who has been killed by the sword or has died
Numbers19:17 For the purification of the unclean person
Numbers19:18 A person who is clean is to take hyssop
Numbers19:19 “The one who is clean is to sprinkle the unclean person on the third day and the seventh day. After he purifies the unclean person on the seventh day
Numbers19:20 But a person who is unclean and does not purify himself
Numbers19:21 This is a permanent statute for them. The person who sprinkles the water for impurity is to wash his clothes
Numbers19:22 Anything the unclean person touches will become unclean

Numbers28:1 The LORD spoke to Moses
Numbers28:2 “Command the Israelites and say to them: Be sure to present to me at its appointed time my offering and my food as my fire offering
Numbers28:3 And say to them: This is the fire offering you are to present to the LORD: “Each day present two unblemished year-old male lambs as a regular burnt offering.
Numbers28:4 Offer one lamb in the morning and the other lamb at twilight
Numbers28:5 along with two quarts of fine flour for a grain offering mixed with a quart of olive oil from crushed olives.
Numbers28:6 It is a regular burnt offering established at Mount Sinai for a pleasing aroma
Numbers28:7 The drink offering is to be a quart with each lamb. Pour out the offering of beer to the LORD in the sanctuary area.
Numbers28:8 Offer the second lamb at twilight
Numbers28:9 “On the Sabbath day present two unblemished year-old male lambs
Numbers28:10 It is the burnt offering for every Sabbath
Numbers28:11 “At the beginning of each of your months present a burnt offering to the LORD: two young bulls
Numbers28:12 with six quarts of fine flour mixed with oil as a grain offering for each bull
Numbers28:13 and two quarts of fine flour mixed with oil as a grain offering for each lamb. It is a burnt offering
Numbers28:14 Their drink offerings are to be two quarts of wine with each bull
Numbers28:15 And one male goat is to be offered as a sin offering to the LORD
Numbers28:16 “The Passover to the LORD comes in the first month
Numbers28:17 On the fifteenth day of this month there will be a festival; unleavened bread is to be eaten for seven days.
Numbers28:18 On the first day there is to be a sacred assembly; you are not to do any daily work.
Numbers28:19 Present a fire offering
Numbers28:20 The grain offering with them is to be of fine flour mixed with oil; offer six quarts with each bull and four quarts with the ram.
Numbers28:21 Offer two quarts with each of the seven lambs
Numbers28:22 and one male goat for a sin offering to make atonement for yourselves.
Numbers28:23 Offer these with the morning burnt offering that is part of the regular burnt offering.
Numbers28:24 You are to offer the same food each day for seven days as a fire offering
Numbers28:25 On the seventh day you are to hold a sacred assembly; you are not to do any daily work.
Numbers28:26 “On the day of firstfruits
Numbers28:27 Present a burnt offering for a pleasing aroma to the LORD: two young bulls
Numbers28:28 with their grain offering of fine flour mixed with oil
Numbers28:29 and two quarts with each of the seven lambs
Numbers28:30 and one male goat to make atonement for yourselves.
Numbers28:31 Offer them with their drink offerings in addition to the regular burnt offering and its grain offering. Your animals are to be unblemished.
Numbers29:1 “You are to hold a sacred assembly in the seventh month
Numbers29:2 Offer a burnt offering as a pleasing aroma to the LORD: one young bull
Numbers29:3 with their grain offering of fine flour mixed with oil
Numbers29:4 and two quarts with each of the seven male lambs.
Numbers29:5 Also offer one male goat as a sin offering to make atonement for yourselves.
Numbers29:6 These are in addition to the monthly and regular burnt offerings with their prescribed grain offerings and drink offerings. They are a pleasing aroma
Numbers29:7 “You are to hold a sacred assembly on the tenth day of this seventh month and practice self-denial; do not do any work.
Numbers29:8 Present a burnt offering to the LORD
Numbers29:9 Their grain offering is to be of fine flour mixed with oil
Numbers29:10 and two quarts with each of the seven lambs.
Numbers29:11 Offer one male goat for a sin offering. The regular burnt offering with its grain offering and drink offerings are in addition to the sin offering of atonement.
Numbers29:12 “You are to hold a sacred assembly on the fifteenth day of the seventh month; you do not do any daily work. You are to celebrate a seven-day festival for the LORD.
Numbers29:13 Present a burnt offering
Numbers29:14 Their grain offering is to be of fine flour mixed with oil
Numbers29:15 and two quarts with each of the fourteen lambs.
Numbers29:16 Also offer one male goat as a sin offering. These are in addition to the regular burnt offering with its grain and drink offerings.
Numbers29:17 “On the second day present twelve young bulls
Numbers29:18 with their grain and drink offerings for the bulls
Numbers29:19 Also offer one male goat as a sin offering. These are in addition to the regular burnt offering with its grain and drink and their drink offerings.
Numbers29:20 “On the third day present eleven bulls
Numbers29:21 with their grain and drink offerings for the bulls
Numbers29:22 Also offer one male goat as a sin offering. These are in addition to the regular burnt offering with its grain and drink offerings.
Numbers29:23 “On the fourth day present ten bulls
Numbers29:24 with their grain and drink offerings for the bulls
Numbers29:25 Also offer one male goat as a sin offering. These are in addition to the regular burnt offering with its grain and drink offerings.
Numbers29:26 “On the fifth day present nine bulls
Numbers29:27 with their grain and drink offerings for the bulls
Numbers29:28 Also offer one male goat as a sin offering. These are in addition to the regular burnt offering with its grain and drink offerings.
Numbers29:29 “On the sixth day present eight bulls
Numbers29:30 with their grain and drink offerings for the bulls
Numbers29:31 Also offer one male goat as a sin offering. These are in addition to the regular burnt offering with its grain and drink offerings.
Numbers29:32 “On the seventh day present seven bulls
Numbers29:33 with their grain and drink offerings for the bulls
Numbers29:34 Also offer one male goat as a sin offering. These are in addition to the regular burnt offering with its grain and drink offerings.
Numbers29:35 “On the eighth day you are to hold a solemn assembly; you are not to do any daily work.

Numbers29:37 with their grain and drink offerings for the bulls
Numbers29:38 Also offer one male goat as a sin offering. These are in addition to the regular burnt offering with its grain and drink offerings.

Numbers35:1 The LORD again spoke to Moses in the plains of Moab by the Jordan across from Jericho:
Numbers35:2 “Command the Israelites to give cities out of their hereditary property for the Levites to live in and pastureland around the cities.
Numbers35:3 The cities will be for them to live in

Numbers35:13 The cities you select will be your six cities of refuge.
Numbers35:14 Select three cities across the Jordan and three cities in the land of Canaan to be cities of refuge.
Numbers35:15 These six cities will serve as a refuge for the Israelites and for the alien or temporary resident among them
Numbers35:16 “If anyone strikes a person with an iron object and death results
Numbers35:17 If anyone has in his hand a stone capable of causing death and strikes another person and he dies
Numbers35:18 If anyone has in his hand a wooden object capable of causing death and strikes another person and he dies
Numbers35:19 The avenger of blood himself is to kill the murderer; when he finds him
Numbers35:20 Likewise
Numbers35:21 or if in hostility he strikes him with his hand and he dies
Numbers35:22 “But if anyone suddenly pushes a person without hostility or throws any object at him without malicious intent
Numbers35:23 or without looking drops a stone that could kill a person and he dies
Numbers35:24 the assembly is to judge between the person who kills someone and the avenger of blood according to these ordinances.
Numbers35:25 The assembly is to protect the one who kills someone from the avenger of blood. Then the assembly will return him to the city of refuge he fled to
Numbers35:26 “If the one who kills someone ever goes outside the border of the city of refuge he fled to
Numbers35:27 and the avenger of blood finds him outside the border of his city of refuge and kills him
Numbers35:28 for the one who killed a person was supposed to live in his city of refuge until the death of the high priest. Only after the death of the high priest may the one who has killed a person return to the land he possesses.
Numbers35:29 These instructions will be a statutory ordinance for you throughout your generations wherever you live.
Numbers35:30 “If anyone kills a person
Numbers35:31 You are not to accept a ransom for the life of someone who is guilty of murder; he must be put to death.
Numbers35:32 Neither should you accept a ransom for the person who flees to his city of refuge
Numbers35:33 “Do not defile the land where you live
Numbers35:34 Do not make the land unclean where you live and where I dwell; for I

Deuteronomy16:1 “Set aside the month of Abib and observe the Passover to the LORD your God
Deuteronomy16:2 Sacrifice to the LORD your God a Passover animal from the herd or flock in the place where the LORD chooses to have his name dwell.
Deuteronomy16:3 Do not eat leavened bread with it. For seven days you are to eat unleavened bread with it
Deuteronomy16:4 No yeast is to be found anywhere in your territory for seven days
Deuteronomy16:5 You are not to sacrifice the Passover animal in any of the towns the LORD your God is giving you.
Deuteronomy16:6 Sacrifice the Passover animal only at the place where the LORD your God chooses to have his name dwell. Do this in the evening as the sun sets at the same time of day you departed from Egypt.
Deuteronomy16:7 You are to cook and eat it in the place the LORD your God chooses
Deuteronomy16:8 Eat unleavened bread for six days. On the seventh day there is to be a solemn assembly to the LORD your God; do not do any work.
Deuteronomy16:9 “You are to count seven weeks
Deuteronomy16:10 You are to celebrate the Festival of Weeks to the LORD your God with a freewill offering that you give in proportion to how the LORD your God has blessed you.
Deuteronomy16:11 Rejoice before the LORD your God in the place where he chooses to have his name dwell — you
Deuteronomy16:12 Remember that you were slaves in Egypt; carefully follow these statutes.
Deuteronomy16:13 “You are to celebrate the Festival of Shelters for seven days when you have gathered in everything from your threshing floor and winepress.
Deuteronomy16:14 Rejoice during your festival — you
Deuteronomy16:15 You are to hold a seven-day festival for the LORD your God in the place he chooses
Deuteronomy16:16 “All your males are to appear three times a year before the LORD your God in the place he chooses: at the Festival of Unleavened Bread
Deuteronomy16:17 Everyone must appear with a gift suited to his means
Deuteronomy16:18 “Appoint judges and officials for your tribes in all your towns the LORD your God is giving you. They are to judge the people with righteous judgment.
