Deuteronomy30:1 “When all these things happen to you — the blessings and curses I have set before you — and you come to your senses while you are in all the nations where the LORD your God has driven you
Deuteronomy30:2 and you and your children return to the LORD your God and obey him with all your heart and all your soul by doing everything I am commanding you today
Deuteronomy30:3 then he will restore your fortunes
Deuteronomy30:4 Even if your exiles are at the farthest horizon
Deuteronomy30:5 The LORD your God will bring you into the land your fathers possessed
Deuteronomy30:6 The LORD your God will circumcise your heart and the hearts of your descendants
Deuteronomy30:7 The LORD your God will put all these curses on your enemies who hate and persecute you.
Deuteronomy30:8 Then you will again obey him and follow all his commands I am commanding you today.
Deuteronomy30:9 The LORD your God will make you prosper abundantly in all the work of your hands
Deuteronomy30:10 when you obey the LORD your God by keeping his commands and statutes that are written in this book of the law and return to him with all your heart and all your soul.
Deuteronomy30:11 “This command that I give you today is certainly not too difficult or beyond your reach.
Deuteronomy30:12 It is not in heaven so that you have to ask
Deuteronomy30:13 And it is not across the sea so that you have to ask
Deuteronomy30:14 But the message is very near you
Deuteronomy30:15 See
Deuteronomy30:16 For I am commanding you today to love the LORD your God
Deuteronomy30:17 But if your heart turns away and you do not listen and you are led astray to bow in worship to other gods and serve them
Deuteronomy30:18 I tell you today that you will certainly perish and will not prolong your days in the land you are entering to possess across the Jordan.
Deuteronomy30:19 I call heaven and earth as witnesses against you today that I have set before you life and death
Deuteronomy30:20 love the LORD your God
Deuteronomy31:1 Then Moses continued to speak these words to all Israel
Deuteronomy31:2 saying
Deuteronomy31:3 The LORD your God is the one who will cross ahead of you. He will destroy these nations before you
Deuteronomy31:4 The LORD will deal with them as he did Sihon and Og
Deuteronomy31:5 The LORD will deliver them over to you
Deuteronomy31:6 Be strong and courageous; don’t be terrified or afraid of them. For the LORD your God is the one who will go with you; he will not leave you or abandon you.”
Deuteronomy31:7 Moses then summoned Joshua and said to him in the sight of all Israel
Deuteronomy31:8 The LORD is the one who will go before you. He will be with you; he will not leave you or abandon you. Do not be afraid or discouraged.”
Deuteronomy31:9 Moses wrote down this law and gave it to the priests
Deuteronomy31:10 Moses commanded them
Deuteronomy31:11 when all Israel assembles in the presence of the LORD your God at the place he chooses
Deuteronomy31:12 Gather the people — men
Deuteronomy31:13 Then their children who do not know the law will listen and learn to fear the LORD your God as long as you live in the land you are crossing the Jordan to possess.”
Deuteronomy31:14 The LORD said to Moses
Deuteronomy31:15 the LORD appeared at the tent in a pillar of cloud
Deuteronomy31:16 The LORD said to Moses
Deuteronomy31:17 My anger will burn against them on that day; I will abandon them and hide my face from them so that they will become easy prey. Many troubles and afflictions will come to them. On that day they will say
Deuteronomy31:18 I will certainly hide my face on that day because of all the evil they have done by turning to other gods.
Deuteronomy31:19 Therefore write down this song for yourselves and teach it to the Israelites; have them sing it
Deuteronomy31:20 When I bring them into the land I swore to give their fathers
Deuteronomy31:21 And when many troubles and afflictions come to them
Deuteronomy31:22 So Moses wrote down this song on that day and taught it to the Israelites.
Deuteronomy31:23 The LORD commissioned Joshua son of Nun
Deuteronomy31:24 When Moses had finished writing down on a scroll every single word of this law
Deuteronomy31:25 he commanded the Levites who carried the ark of the LORD’s covenant
Deuteronomy31:26 “Take this book of the law and place it beside the ark of the covenant of the LORD your God so that it may remain there as a witness against you.
Deuteronomy31:27 For I know how rebellious and stiff-necked you are. If you are rebelling against the LORD now
Deuteronomy31:28 Assemble all your tribal elders and officers before me so that I may speak these words directly to them and call heaven and earth as witnesses against them.
Deuteronomy31:29 For I know that after my death you will become completely corrupt and turn from the path I have commanded you. Disaster will come to you in the future
Deuteronomy31:30 Then Moses recited aloud every single word of this song to the entire assembly of Israel:
Deuteronomy32:1 Pay attention
Deuteronomy32:2 Let my teaching fall like rain and my word settle like dew
Deuteronomy32:3 For I will proclaim the LORD’s name. Declare the greatness of our God!
Deuteronomy32:4 The Rock  — his work is perfect; all his ways are just. A faithful God
Deuteronomy32:5 His people have acted corruptly toward him; this is their defect  — they are not his children but a devious and crooked generation.
Deuteronomy32:6 Is this how you repay the LORD
Deuteronomy32:7 Remember the days of old; consider the years of past generations. Ask your father
Deuteronomy32:8 When the Most High gave the nations their inheritance and divided the human race
Deuteronomy32:9 But the LORD’s portion is his people
Deuteronomy32:10 He found him in a desolate land
Deuteronomy32:11 He watches over his nest like an eagle and hovers over his young; he spreads his wings
Deuteronomy32:12 The LORD alone led him
Deuteronomy32:13 He made him ride on the heights of the land and eat the produce of the field. He nourished him with honey from the rock and oil from flinty rock
Deuteronomy32:14 curds from the herd and milk from the flock
Deuteronomy32:15 Then Jeshurun became fat and rebelled — you became fat
Deuteronomy32:16 They provoked his jealousy with different gods; they enraged him with detestable practices.
Deuteronomy32:17 They sacrificed to demons
Deuteronomy32:18 You ignored the Rock who gave you birth; you forgot the God who gave birth to you.
Deuteronomy32:19 When the LORD saw this
Deuteronomy32:20 He said: “I will hide my face from them; I will see what will become of them
Deuteronomy32:21 They have provoked my jealousy with what is not a god; they have enraged me with their worthless idols. So I will provoke their jealousy with what is not a people; I will enrage them with a foolish nation.
Deuteronomy32:22 For fire has been kindled because of my anger and burns to the depths of Sheol; it devours the land and its produce
Deuteronomy32:23 “I will pile disasters on them; I will use up my arrows against them.
Deuteronomy32:24 They will be weak from hunger
Deuteronomy32:25 Outside
Deuteronomy32:26 “I would have said: I will cut them to pieces and blot out the memory of them from mankind
Deuteronomy32:27 if I had not feared provocation from the enemy
Deuteronomy32:28 Israel is a nation lacking sense with no understanding at all.
Deuteronomy32:29 If only they were wise
Deuteronomy32:30 How could one pursue a thousand
Deuteronomy32:31 But their “rock” is not like our Rock
Deuteronomy32:32 For their vine is from the vine of Sodom and from the fields of Gomorrah. Their grapes are poisonous; their clusters are bitter.
Deuteronomy32:33 Their wine is serpents’ venom
Deuteronomy32:34 “Is it not stored up with me
Deuteronomy32:35 Vengeance belongs to me; I will repay. In time their foot will slip
Deuteronomy32:36 The LORD will indeed vindicate his people and have compassion on his servants when he sees that their strength is gone and no one is left — slave or free.
Deuteronomy32:37 He will say: “Where are their gods
Deuteronomy32:38 Who ate the fat of their sacrifices and drank the wine of their drink offerings? Let them rise up and help you; let it be a shelter for you.
Deuteronomy32:39 See now that I alone am he; there is no God but me. I bring death and I give life; I wound and I heal. No one can rescue anyone from my power.
Deuteronomy32:40 I raise my hand to heaven and declare: As surely as I live forever
Deuteronomy32:41 when I sharpen my flashing sword
Deuteronomy32:42 I will make my arrows drunk with blood while my sword devours flesh — the blood of the slain and the captives
Deuteronomy32:43 Rejoice
Deuteronomy32:44 Moses came with Joshua son of Nun and recited all the words of this song in the presence of the people.
Deuteronomy32:45 After Moses finished reciting all these words to all Israel
Deuteronomy32:46 he said to them
Deuteronomy32:47 For they are not meaningless words to you but they are your life
Deuteronomy32:48 On that same day the LORD spoke to Moses
Deuteronomy32:49 “Go up Mount Nebo in the Abarim range in the land of Moab
Deuteronomy32:50 Then you will die on the mountain that you go up
Deuteronomy32:51 For both of you broke faith with me among the Israelites at the Waters of Meribath-kadesh in the Wilderness of Zin by failing to treat me as holy in their presence.
Deuteronomy32:52 Although from a distance you will view the land that I am giving the Israelites
Deuteronomy33:1 This is the blessing that Moses
Deuteronomy33:2 He said: The LORD came from Sinai and appeared to them from Seir; he shone on them from Mount Paran and came with ten thousand holy ones
Deuteronomy33:3 Indeed he loves the people. All your holy ones are in your hand
Deuteronomy33:4 Moses gave us instruction
Deuteronomy33:5 So he became King in Jeshurun when the leaders of the people gathered with the tribes of Israel.
Deuteronomy33:6 Let Reuben live and not die though his people become few.
Deuteronomy33:7 He said this about Judah: LORD
Deuteronomy33:8 He said about Levi: Your Thummim and Urim belong to your faithful one; you tested him at Massah and contended with him at the Waters of Meribah.
Deuteronomy33:9 He said about his father and mother
Deuteronomy33:10 They will teach your ordinances to Jacob and your instruction to Israel; they will set incense before you and whole burnt offerings on your altar.
Deuteronomy33:11 LORD
Deuteronomy33:12 He said about Benjamin: The LORD’s beloved rests securely on him. He shields him all day long
Deuteronomy33:13 He said about Joseph: May his land be blessed by the LORD with the dew of heaven’s bounty and the watery depths that lie beneath;
Deuteronomy33:14 with the bountiful harvest from the sun and the abundant yield of the seasons;
Deuteronomy33:15 with the best products of the ancient mountains and the bounty of the eternal hills;
Deuteronomy33:16 with the choice gifts of the land and everything in it; and with the favor of him who appeared in the burning bush. May these rest on the head of Joseph
Deuteronomy33:17 His firstborn bull has splendor
Deuteronomy33:18 He said about Zebulun: Rejoice
Deuteronomy33:19 They summon the peoples to a mountain; there they offer acceptable sacrifices. For they draw from the wealth of the seas and the hidden treasures of the sand.
Deuteronomy33:20 He said about Gad: The one who enlarges Gad’s territory will be blessed. He lies down like a lion and tears off an arm or even a head.
Deuteronomy33:21 He chose the best part for himself
Deuteronomy33:22 He said about Dan: Dan is a young lion
Deuteronomy33:23 He said about Naphtali: Naphtali
Deuteronomy33:24 He said about Asher: May Asher be the most blessed of the sons; may he be the most favored among his brothers and dip his foot in olive oil.
Deuteronomy33:25 May the bolts of your gate be iron and bronze
Deuteronomy33:26 There is none like the God of Jeshurun
Deuteronomy33:27 The God of old is your dwelling place
Deuteronomy33:28 So Israel dwells securely; Jacob lives untroubled in a land of grain and new wine; even his skies drip with dew.
Deuteronomy33:29 How happy you are

Deuteronomy34:2 all of Naphtali
Deuteronomy34:3 the Negev
Deuteronomy34:4 The LORD then said to him
Deuteronomy34:5 So Moses the servant of the LORD died there in the land of Moab
Deuteronomy34:6 He buried him in the valley in the land of Moab facing Beth-peor
Deuteronomy34:7 Moses was one hundred twenty years old when he died; his eyes were not weak
Deuteronomy34:8 The Israelites wept for Moses in the plains of Moab thirty days. Then the days of weeping and mourning for Moses came to an end.
Deuteronomy34:9 Joshua son of Nun was filled with the spirit of wisdom because Moses had laid his hands on him. So the Israelites obeyed him and did as the LORD had commanded Moses.
Deuteronomy34:10 No prophet has arisen again in Israel like Moses
Deuteronomy34:11 He was unparalleled for all the signs and wonders the LORD sent him to do against the land of Egypt — to Pharaoh
Deuteronomy34:12 and for all the mighty acts of power and terrifying deeds that Moses performed in the sight of all Israel.
Joshua1:1 After the death of Moses the LORD’s servant
Joshua1:2 “Moses my servant is dead. Now you and all the people prepare to cross over the Jordan to the land I am giving the Israelites.
Joshua1:3 I have given you every place where the sole of your foot treads
Joshua1:4 Your territory will be from the wilderness and Lebanon to the great river
Joshua1:5 No one will be able to stand against you as long as you live. I will be with you
Joshua1:6 “Be strong and courageous
Joshua1:7 Above all
Joshua1:8 This book of instruction must not depart from your mouth; you are to meditate on it day and night so that you may carefully observe everything written in it. For then you will prosper and succeed in whatever you do.
Joshua1:9 Haven’t I commanded you: be strong and courageous? Do not be afraid or discouraged
Joshua1:10 Then Joshua commanded the officers of the people:
Joshua1:11 “Go through the camp and tell the people
Joshua1:12 Joshua said to the Reubenites

Joshua1:15 until the LORD gives your brothers rest
Joshua1:16 They answered Joshua
Joshua1:17 We will obey you
Joshua1:18 Anyone who rebels against your order and does not obey your words in all that you command him
Joshua2:1 Joshua son of Nun secretly sent two men as spies from the Acacia Grove
Joshua2:2 The king of Jericho was told
Joshua2:3 Then the king of Jericho sent word to Rahab and said
Joshua2:4 But the woman had taken the two men and hidden them. So she said
Joshua2:5 At nightfall
Joshua2:6 But she had taken them up to the roof and hidden them among the stalks of flax that she had arranged on the roof.
Joshua2:7 The men pursued them along the road to the fords of the Jordan
Joshua2:8 Before the men fell asleep
Joshua2:9 and said to them
Joshua2:10 For we have heard how the LORD dried up the water of the Red Sea before you when you came out of Egypt
Joshua2:11 When we heard this
Joshua2:12 Now please swear to me by the LORD that you will also show kindness to my father’s family
Joshua2:13 that you will spare the lives of my father
Joshua2:14 The men answered her
Joshua2:15 Then she let them down by a rope through the window
Joshua2:16 “Go to the hill country so that the men pursuing you won’t find you
Joshua2:17 The men said to her
Joshua2:18 unless
Joshua2:19 If anyone goes out the doors of your house
Joshua2:20 And if you report our mission
Joshua2:21 “Let it be as you say
Joshua2:22 So the two men went into the hill country and stayed there three days until the pursuers had returned. They searched all along the way
Joshua2:23 Then the men returned

Joshua3:1 Joshua started early the next morning and left the Acacia Grove with all the Israelites. They went as far as the Jordan and stayed there before crossing.
Joshua3:2 After three days the officers went through the camp
Joshua3:3 and commanded the people: “When you see the ark of the covenant of the LORD your God carried by the Levitical priests
Joshua3:4 But keep a distance of about a thousand yards between yourselves and the ark. Don’t go near it
Joshua3:5 Joshua told the people
Joshua3:6 Then he said to the priests
Joshua3:7 The LORD spoke to Joshua: “Today I will begin to exalt you in the sight of all Israel
Joshua3:8 Command the priests carrying the ark of the covenant: When you reach the edge of the water
Joshua3:9 Then Joshua told the Israelites
Joshua3:10 He said: “You will know that the living God is among you and that he will certainly dispossess before you the Canaanites
Joshua3:11 when the ark of the covenant of the Lord of the whole earth goes ahead of you into the Jordan.
Joshua3:12 Now choose twelve men from the tribes of Israel
Joshua3:13 When the feet of the priests who carry the ark of the LORD
Joshua3:14 When the people broke camp to cross the Jordan
Joshua3:15 Now the Jordan overflows its banks throughout the harvest season. But as soon as the priests carrying the ark reached the Jordan
Joshua3:16 and the water flowing downstream stood still
Joshua3:17 The priests carrying the ark of the LORD’s covenant stood firmly on dry ground in the middle of the Jordan

Joshua4:2 “Choose twelve men from the people

Joshua4:4 So Joshua summoned the twelve men he had selected from the Israelites
Joshua4:5 and said to them
Joshua4:6 so that this will be a sign among you. In the future
Joshua4:7 you should tell them
Joshua4:8 The Israelites did just as Joshua had commanded them. The twelve men took stones from the middle of the Jordan
Joshua4:9 Joshua also set up twelve stones in the middle of the Jordan where the priests who carried the ark of the covenant were standing. The stones are still there today.
Joshua4:10 The priests carrying the ark continued standing in the middle of the Jordan until everything was completed that the LORD had commanded Joshua to tell the people
Joshua4:11 and after everyone had finished crossing

Joshua4:23 For the LORD your God dried up the water of the Jordan before you until you had crossed over
Joshua4:24 This is so that all the peoples of the earth may know that the LORD’s hand is mighty

Joshua5:2 At that time the LORD said to Joshua
Joshua5:3 So Joshua made flint knives and circumcised the Israelite men at Gibeath-haaraloth.
Joshua5:4 This is the reason Joshua circumcised them: All the people who came out of Egypt who were males — all the men of war — had died in the wilderness along the way after they had come out of Egypt.
Joshua5:5 Though all the people who came out were circumcised
Joshua5:6 For the Israelites wandered in the wilderness forty years until all the nation’s men of war who came out of Egypt had died off because they did not obey the LORD. So the LORD vowed never to let them see the land he had sworn to their fathers to give us
Joshua5:7 He raised up their sons in their place; it was these Joshua circumcised. They were still uncircumcised

Joshua6:5 When there is a prolonged blast of the horn and you hear its sound

Joshua6:7 He said to the troops
Joshua6:8 After Joshua had spoken to the troops
Joshua6:9 While the trumpets were blowing
Joshua6:10 But Joshua had commanded the troops: “Do not shout or let your voice be heard. Don’t let one word come out of your mouth until the time I say
Joshua6:11 So the ark of the LORD was carried around the city
Joshua6:12 Joshua got up early the next morning. The priests took the ark of the LORD
Joshua6:13 and the seven priests carrying seven trumpets marched in front of the ark of the LORD. While the trumpets were blowing
Joshua6:14 On the second day they marched around the city once and returned to the camp. They did this for six days.
Joshua6:15 Early on the seventh day
Joshua6:16 After the seventh time
Joshua6:17 But the city and everything in it are set apart to the LORD for destruction. Only Rahab the prostitute and everyone with her in the house will live
Joshua6:18 But keep yourselves from the things set apart
Joshua6:19 For all the silver and gold

Joshua6:25 However
Joshua6:26 At that time Joshua imposed this curse: The man who undertakes the rebuilding of this city

Joshua7:8 What can I say

Joshua7:12 This is why the Israelites cannot stand against their enemies. They will turn their backs and run from their enemies

Joshua7:26 and raised over him a large pile of rocks that remains still today. Then the LORD turned from his burning anger. Therefore that place is called the Valley of Achor still today.
Joshua8:1 The LORD said to Joshua
Joshua8:2 Treat Ai and its king as you did Jericho and its king
Joshua8:3 So Joshua and all the troops set out to attack Ai. Joshua selected thirty thousand of his best soldiers and sent them out at night.
Joshua8:4 He commanded them: “Pay attention. Lie in ambush behind the city
Joshua8:5 Then I and all the people who are with me will approach the city. When they come out against us as they did the first time
Joshua8:6 They will come after us until we have drawn them away from the city
Joshua8:7 you are to come out of your ambush and seize the city. The LORD your God will hand it over to you.
Joshua8:8 After taking the city
Joshua8:9 So Joshua sent them out
Joshua8:10 Joshua started early the next morning and mobilized them. Then he and the elders of Israel led the troops up to Ai.
Joshua8:11 All those who were with him went up and approached the city
Joshua8:12 Now Joshua had taken about five thousand men and set them in ambush between Bethel and Ai
Joshua8:13 The troops were stationed in this way: the main camp to the north of the city and its rear guard to the west of the city. And that night Joshua went into the valley.
Joshua8:14 When the king of Ai saw the Israelites
Joshua8:15 Joshua and all Israel pretended to be beaten back by them and fled toward the wilderness.
Joshua8:16 Then all the troops of Ai were summoned to pursue them
Joshua8:17 Not a man was left in Ai or Bethel who did not go out after Israel
Joshua8:18 Then the LORD said to Joshua
Joshua8:19 When he held out his hand
Joshua8:20 The men of Ai turned and looked back
Joshua8:21 When Joshua and all Israel saw that the men in ambush had captured the city and that smoke was rising from it
Joshua8:22 Then men in ambush came out of the city against them
Joshua8:23 but they captured the king of Ai alive and brought him to Joshua.
Joshua8:24 When Israel had finished killing everyone living in Ai who had pursued them into the open country
Joshua8:25 The total of those who fell that day
Joshua8:26 Joshua did not draw back his hand that was holding the javelin until all the inhabitants of Ai were completely destroyed.
Joshua8:27 Israel plundered only the cattle and spoil of that city for themselves
Joshua8:28 Joshua burned Ai and left it a permanent ruin
Joshua8:29 He hung the body of the king of Ai on a tree until evening
Joshua8:30 At that time Joshua built an altar on Mount Ebal to the LORD
Joshua8:31 just as Moses the LORD’s servant had commanded the Israelites. He built it according to what is written in the book of the law of Moses: an altar of uncut stones on which no iron tool has been used. Then they offered burnt offerings to the LORD and sacrificed fellowship offerings on it.
Joshua8:32 There on the stones
Joshua8:33 All Israel — resident alien and citizen alike — with their elders
Joshua8:34 Afterward
Joshua8:35 There was not a word of all that Moses had commanded that Joshua did not read before the entire assembly of Israel

Joshua9:2 they formed a unified alliance to fight against Joshua and Israel.
Joshua9:3 When the inhabitants of Gibeon heard what Joshua had done to Jericho and Ai
Joshua9:4 they acted deceptively. They gathered provisions and took worn-out sacks on their donkeys and old wineskins
Joshua9:5 They wore old
Joshua9:6 They went to Joshua in the camp at Gilgal and said to him and the men of Israel
Joshua9:7 The men of Israel replied to the Hivites
Joshua9:8 They said to Joshua
Joshua9:9 They replied to him
Joshua9:10 and all that he did to the two Amorite kings beyond the Jordan — King Sihon of Heshbon and King Og of Bashan
Joshua9:11 So our elders and all the inhabitants of our land told us
Joshua9:12 This bread of ours was warm when we took it from our houses as food on the day we left to come to you; but see
Joshua9:13 These wineskins were new when we filled them; but see
Joshua9:14 Then the men of Israel took some of their provisions
Joshua9:15 So Joshua established peace with them and made a treaty to let them live
Joshua9:16 Three days after making the treaty with them
Joshua9:17 So the Israelites set out and reached the Gibeonite cities on the third day. Now their cities were Gibeon
Joshua9:18 But the Israelites did not attack them
Joshua9:19 All the leaders answered them
Joshua9:20 This is how we will treat them: we will let them live
Joshua9:21 They also said
Joshua9:22 Joshua summoned the Gibeonites and said to them
Joshua9:23 Therefore you are cursed and will always be slaves — woodcutters and water carriers for the house of my God.”
Joshua9:24 The Gibeonites answered him
Joshua9:25 Now we are in your hands. Do to us whatever you think is right.”
Joshua9:26 This is what Joshua did to them: he rescued them from the Israelites
Joshua9:27 On that day he made them woodcutters and water carriers — as they are today — for the community and for the LORD’s altar at the place he would choose.
Joshua10:1 Now King Adoni-zedek of Jerusalem heard that Joshua had captured Ai and completely destroyed it
Joshua10:2 So Adoni-zedek and his people were greatly alarmed because Gibeon was a large city like one of the royal cities; it was larger than Ai
Joshua10:3 Therefore King Adoni-zedek of Jerusalem sent word to King Hoham of Hebron
Joshua10:4 “Come up and help me. We will attack Gibeon

Joshua22:27 Instead

Joshua23:7 and so that you do not associate with these nations remaining among you. Do not call on the names of their gods or make an oath to them; do not serve them or bow in worship to them.
Joshua23:8 Instead

Joshua23:10 One of you routed a thousand because the LORD your God was fighting for you

Joshua23:13 know for certain that the LORD your God will not continue to drive these nations out before you. They will become a snare and a trap for you

Joshua23:15 Since every good thing the LORD your God promised you has come about

1Samuel2:35 “‘Then I will raise up a faithful priest for myself. He will do whatever is in my heart and mind. I will establish a lasting dynasty for him
1Samuel2:36 Anyone who is left in your family will come and bow down to him for a piece of silver or a loaf of bread. He will say: Please appoint me to some priestly office so I can have a piece of bread to eat.’”
1Samuel3:1 The boy Samuel served the LORD in Eli’s presence. In those days the word of the LORD was rare and prophetic visions were not widespread.

1Samuel3:3 Before the lamp of God had gone out

1Samuel3:14 Therefore

1Samuel3:16 but Eli called him and said

1Samuel3:19 Samuel grew
1Samuel3:20 All Israel from Dan to Beer-sheba knew that Samuel was a confirmed prophet of the LORD.

2Samuel22:1 David spoke the words of this song to the LORD on the day the LORD rescued him from the grasp of all his enemies and from the grasp of Saul.
2Samuel22:2 He said: The LORD is my rock
2Samuel22:3 my God
2Samuel22:4 I called to the LORD
2Samuel22:5 For the waves of death engulfed me; the torrents of destruction terrified me.
2Samuel22:6 The ropes of Sheol entangled me; the snares of death confronted me.
2Samuel22:7 I called to the LORD in my distress; I called to my God. From his temple he heard my voice
2Samuel22:8 Then the earth shook and quaked; the foundations of the heavens trembled; they shook because he burned with anger.
2Samuel22:9 Smoke rose from his nostrils
2Samuel22:10 He bent the heavens and came down
2Samuel22:11 He rode on a cherub and flew
2Samuel22:12 He made darkness a canopy around him
2Samuel22:13 From the radiance of his presence
2Samuel22:14 The LORD thundered from heaven; the Most High made his voice heard.
2Samuel22:15 He shot arrows and scattered them; he hurled lightning bolts and routed them.
2Samuel22:16 The depths of the sea became visible
2Samuel22:17 He reached down from on high and took hold of me; he pulled me out of deep water.
2Samuel22:18 He rescued me from my powerful enemy and from those who hated me
2Samuel22:19 They confronted me in the day of my calamity
2Samuel22:20 He brought me out to a spacious place; he rescued me because he delighted in me.
2Samuel22:21 The LORD rewarded me according to my righteousness; he repaid me according to the cleanness of my hands.
2Samuel22:22 For I have kept the ways of the LORD and have not turned from my God to wickedness.
2Samuel22:23 Indeed
2Samuel22:24 I was blameless before him and kept myself from my iniquity.
2Samuel22:25 So the LORD repaid me according to my righteousness
2Samuel22:26 With the faithful you prove yourself faithful
2Samuel22:27 with the pure you prove yourself pure; but with the crooked you prove yourself shrewd.
2Samuel22:28 You rescue an oppressed people
2Samuel22:29 LORD
2Samuel22:30 With you I can attack a barricade
2Samuel22:31 God — his way is perfect; the word of the LORD is pure. He is a shield to all who take refuge in him.
2Samuel22:32 For who is God besides the LORD? And who is a rock? Only our God.
2Samuel22:33 God is my strong refuge; he makes my way perfect.
2Samuel22:34 He makes my feet like the feet of a deer and sets me securely on the heights.
2Samuel22:35 He trains my hands for war; my arms can bend a bow of bronze.
2Samuel22:36 You have given me the shield of your salvation; your help exalts me.
2Samuel22:37 You make a spacious place beneath me for my steps
2Samuel22:38 I pursue my enemies and destroy them; I do not turn back until they are wiped out.
2Samuel22:39 I wipe them out and crush them
2Samuel22:40 You have clothed me with strength for battle; you subdue my adversaries beneath me.
2Samuel22:41 You have made my enemies retreat before me; I annihilate those who hate me.
2Samuel22:42 They look
2Samuel22:43 I pulverize them like dust of the earth; I crush them and trample them like mud in the streets.
2Samuel22:44 You have freed me from the feuds among my people; you have preserved me as head of nations; a people I had not known serve me.
2Samuel22:45 Foreigners submit to me cringing; as soon as they hear
2Samuel22:46 Foreigners lose heart and come trembling from their fortifications.
2Samuel22:47 The LORD lives — blessed be my rock! God
2Samuel22:48 God — he grants me vengeance and casts down peoples under me.
2Samuel22:49 He frees me from my enemies. You exalt me above my adversaries; you rescue me from violent men.
2Samuel22:50 Therefore I will give thanks to you among the nations
2Samuel22:51 He is a tower of salvation for his king; he shows loyalty to his anointed

1Kings3:6 And Solomon replied
1Kings3:7 “LORD my God
1Kings3:8 Your servant is among your people you have chosen
1Kings3:9 So give your servant a receptive heart to judge your people and to discern between good and evil. For who is able to judge this great people of yours?”
1Kings3:10 Now it pleased the Lord that Solomon had requested this.
1Kings3:11 So God said to him
1Kings3:12 I will therefore do what you have asked. I will give you a wise and understanding heart
1Kings3:13 In addition
1Kings3:14 If you walk in my ways and keep my statutes and commands just as your father David did
1Kings3:15 Then Solomon woke up and realized it had been a dream. He went to Jerusalem
1Kings3:16 Then two women who were prostitutes came to the king and stood before him.
1Kings3:17 One woman said
1Kings3:18 On the third day after I gave birth
1Kings3:19 During the night this woman’s son died because she lay on him.
1Kings3:20 She got up in the middle of the night and took my son from my side while your servant was asleep. She laid him in her arms
1Kings3:21 When I got up in the morning to nurse my son
1Kings3:22 “No
1Kings3:23 The king replied
1Kings3:24 The king continued
1Kings3:25 And the king said
1Kings3:26 The woman whose son was alive spoke to the king because she felt great compassion for her son. “My lord
1Kings3:27 The king responded

1Kings4:1 King Solomon reigned over all Israel
1Kings4:2 and these were his officials: Azariah son of Zadok

1Kings4:4 Benaiah son of Jehoiada
1Kings4:5 Azariah son of Nathan
1Kings4:6 Ahishar
1Kings4:7 Solomon had twelve deputies for all Israel. They provided food for the king and his household; each one made provision for one month out of the year.
1Kings4:8 These were their names: Ben-hur
1Kings4:9 Ben-deker
1Kings4:10 Ben-hesed
1Kings4:11 Ben-abinadab
1Kings4:12 Baana son of Ahilud

1Kings4:18 Shimei son of Ela
1Kings4:19 Geber son of Uri
1Kings4:20 Judah and Israel were as numerous as the sand by the sea; they were eating
1Kings4:21 Solomon ruled all the kingdoms from the Euphrates River to the land of the Philistines and as far as the border of Egypt. They offered tribute and served Solomon all the days of his life.
1Kings4:22 Solomon’s provisions for one day were 150 bushels of fine flour and 300 bushels of meal
1Kings4:23 ten fattened cattle
1Kings4:24 for he had dominion over everything west of the Euphrates from Tiphsah to Gaza and over all the kings west of the Euphrates. He had peace on all his surrounding borders.
1Kings4:25 Throughout Solomon’s reign
1Kings4:26 Solomon had forty thousand stalls of horses for his chariots
1Kings4:27 Each of those deputies for a month in turn provided food for King Solomon and for everyone who came to King Solomon’s table. They neglected nothing.
1Kings4:28 Each man brought the barley and the straw for the chariot teams and the other horses to the required place according to his assignment.
1Kings4:29 God gave Solomon wisdom
1Kings4:30 Solomon’s wisdom was greater than the wisdom of all the people of the East
1Kings4:31 He was wiser than anyone  — wiser than Ethan the Ezrahite

1Kings4:33 He spoke about trees

1Kings5:1 King Hiram of Tyre sent his emissaries to Solomon when he heard that he had been anointed king in his father’s place
1Kings5:2 Solomon sent this message to Hiram:
1Kings5:3 “You know my father David was not able to build a temple for the name of the LORD his God. This was because of the warfare all around him until the LORD put his enemies under his feet.
1Kings5:4 The LORD my God has now given me rest on every side; there is no enemy or crisis.
1Kings5:5 So I plan to build a temple for the name of the LORD my God
1Kings5:6 “Therefore
1Kings5:7 When Hiram heard Solomon’s words
1Kings5:8 Then Hiram sent a reply to Solomon
1Kings5:9 My servants will bring the logs down from Lebanon to the sea
1Kings5:10 So Hiram provided Solomon with all the cedar and cypress timber he wanted
1Kings5:11 and Solomon provided Hiram with one hundred thousand bushels of wheat as food for his household and one hundred ten thousand gallons of oil from crushed olives. Solomon did this for Hiram year after year.
1Kings5:12 The LORD gave Solomon wisdom
1Kings5:13 Then King Solomon drafted forced laborers from all Israel; the labor force numbered thirty thousand men.
1Kings5:14 He sent ten thousand to Lebanon each month in shifts; one month they were in Lebanon
1Kings5:15 Solomon had seventy thousand porters and eighty thousand stonecutters in the mountains
1Kings5:16 not including his thirty-three hundred deputies in charge of the work. They supervised the people doing the work.
1Kings5:17 The king commanded them to quarry large
1Kings5:18 So Solomon’s builders and Hiram’s builders
1Kings6:1 Solomon began to build the temple for the LORD in the four hundred eightieth year after the Israelites came out of the land of Egypt
1Kings6:2 The temple that King Solomon built for the LORD was ninety feet long
1Kings6:3 The portico in front of the temple sanctuary was thirty feet long extending across the temple’s width
1Kings6:4 He also made windows with beveled frames for the temple.
1Kings6:5 He then built a chambered structure along the temple wall
1Kings6:6 The lowest chamber was 7½ feet wide
1Kings6:7 The temple’s construction used finished stones cut at the quarry so that no hammer
1Kings6:8 The door for the lowest side chamber was on the right side of the temple. They went up a stairway to the middle chamber
1Kings6:9 When he finished building the temple
1Kings6:10 He built the chambers along the entire temple
1Kings6:11 The word of the LORD came to Solomon:
1Kings6:12 “As for this temple you are building — if you walk in my statutes
1Kings6:13 I will dwell among the Israelites and not abandon my people Israel.”
1Kings6:14 When Solomon finished building the temple
1Kings6:15 he paneled the interior temple walls with cedar boards; from the temple floor to the surface of the ceiling he overlaid the interior with wood. He also overlaid the floor with cypress boards.
1Kings6:16 Then he lined thirty feet of the rear of the temple with cedar boards from the floor to the surface of the ceiling
1Kings6:17 The temple
1Kings6:18 The cedar paneling inside the temple was carved with ornamental gourds and flower blossoms. Everything was cedar; not a stone could be seen.
1Kings6:19 He prepared the inner sanctuary inside the temple to put the ark of the LORD’s covenant there.
1Kings6:20 The interior of the sanctuary was thirty feet long
1Kings6:21 Next
1Kings6:22 So he added the gold overlay to the entire temple until everything was completely finished
1Kings6:23 In the inner sanctuary he made two cherubim 15 feet high out of olive wood.
1Kings6:24 One wing of the first cherub was 7½ feet long
1Kings6:25 The second cherub also was 15 feet; both cherubim had the same size and shape.
1Kings6:26 The first cherub’s height was 15 feet and so was the second cherub’s.
1Kings6:27 Then he put the cherubim inside the inner temple. Since their wings were spread out
1Kings6:28 He also overlaid the cherubim with gold.
1Kings6:29 He carved all the surrounding temple walls with carved engravings — cherubim
1Kings6:30 He overlaid the temple floor with gold in both the inner and the outer sanctuaries.
1Kings6:31 For the entrance of the inner sanctuary
1Kings6:32 The two doors were made of olive wood. He carved cherubim
1Kings6:33 In the same way
1Kings6:34 The two doors were made of cypress wood; the first door had two folding sides
1Kings6:35 He carved cherubim
1Kings6:36 He built the inner courtyard with three rows of dressed stone and a row of trimmed cedar beams.
1Kings6:37 The foundation of the LORD’s temple was laid in Solomon’s fourth year in the month of Ziv.
1Kings6:38 In his eleventh year in the month of Bul
1Kings7:1 Solomon completed his entire palace complex after thirteen years of construction.
1Kings7:2 He built the House of the Forest of Lebanon. It was one hundred fifty feet long
1Kings7:3 It was paneled above with cedar at the top of the chambers that rested on forty-five pillars
1Kings7:4 There were three rows of window frames
1Kings7:5 All the doors and doorposts had rectangular frames
1Kings7:6 He made the hall of pillars seventy-five feet long and forty-five feet wide. A portico was in front of the pillars
1Kings7:7 He made the Hall of the Throne where he would judge  — the Hall of Judgment. It was paneled with cedar from the floor to the rafters.
1Kings7:8 Solomon’s own palace where he would live
1Kings7:9 All of these buildings were of costly stones
1Kings7:10 The foundation was made of large
1Kings7:11 Above were also costly stones
1Kings7:12 Around the great courtyard
1Kings7:13 King Solomon had Hiram brought from Tyre.
1Kings7:14 He was a widow’s son from the tribe of Naphtali
1Kings7:15 He cast two bronze pillars
1Kings7:16 He also made two capitals of cast bronze to set on top of the pillars; 7½ feet was the height of the first capital
1Kings7:17 The capitals on top of the pillars had gratings of latticework
1Kings7:18 He made the pillars with two encircling rows of pomegranates on the one grating to cover the capital on top; he did the same for the second capital.
1Kings7:19 And the capitals on top of the pillars in the portico were shaped like lilies
1Kings7:20 The capitals on the two pillars were also immediately above the rounded surface next to the grating
1Kings7:21 He set up the pillars at the portico of the sanctuary: he set up the right pillar and named it Jachin; then he set up the left pillar and named it Boaz.
1Kings7:22 The tops of the pillars were shaped like lilies. Then the work of the pillars was completed.
1Kings7:23 He made the cast metal basin
1Kings7:24 Ornamental gourds encircled it below the brim
1Kings7:25 It stood on twelve oxen
1Kings7:26 The basin was three inches thick
1Kings7:27 Then he made ten bronze water carts. Each water cart was 6 feet long
1Kings7:28 This was the design of the carts: They had frames; the frames were between the cross-pieces
1Kings7:29 and on the frames between the cross-pieces were lions
1Kings7:30 Each cart had four bronze wheels with bronze axles. Underneath the four corners of the basin were cast supports
1Kings7:31 And the water cart’s opening inside the crown on top was eighteen inches wide. The opening was round
1Kings7:32 There were four wheels under the frames
1Kings7:33 The wheels’ design was similar to that of chariot wheels: their axles
1Kings7:34 Four supports were at the four corners of each water cart; each support was one piece with the water cart.
1Kings7:35 At the top of the cart was a band nine inches high encircling it; also
1Kings7:36 He engraved cherubim
1Kings7:37 In this way he made the ten water carts using the same casting
1Kings7:38 Then he made ten bronze basins  — each basin held 220 gallons and each was six feet wide — one basin for each of the ten water carts.
1Kings7:39 He set five water carts on the right side of the temple and five on the left side. He put the basin near the right side of the temple toward the southeast.
1Kings7:40 Then Hiram made the basins
1Kings7:41 two pillars; bowls for the capitals that were on top of the two pillars; the two gratings for covering both bowls of the capitals that were on top of the pillars;
1Kings7:42 the four hundred pomegranates for the two gratings (two rows of pomegranates for each grating covering both capitals’ bowls on top of the pillars );
1Kings7:43 the ten water carts; the ten basins on the water carts;
1Kings7:44 the basin; the twelve oxen underneath the basin;
1Kings7:45 and the pots
1Kings7:46 The king had them cast in clay molds in the Jordan Valley between Succoth and Zarethan.
1Kings7:47 Solomon left all the utensils unweighed because there were so many; the weight of the bronze was not determined.
1Kings7:48 Solomon also made all the equipment in the LORD’s temple: the gold altar; the gold table that the Bread of the Presence was placed on;
1Kings7:49 the pure gold lampstands in front of the inner sanctuary
1Kings7:50 the pure gold ceremonial bowls
1Kings7:51 So all the work King Solomon did in the LORD’s temple was completed. Then Solomon brought in the consecrated things of his father David  — the silver

1Kings8:10 When the priests came out of the holy place
1Kings8:11 and because of the cloud
1Kings8:12 Then Solomon said: The LORD said that he would dwell in total darkness.
1Kings8:13 I have indeed built an exalted temple for you
1Kings8:14 The king turned around and blessed the entire congregation of Israel while they were standing.
1Kings8:15 He said: Blessed be the LORD God of Israel! He spoke directly to my father David
1Kings8:16 “Since the day I brought my people Israel out of Egypt
1Kings8:17 My father David had his heart set on building a temple for the name of the LORD
1Kings8:18 But the LORD said to my father David
1Kings8:19 Yet you are not the one to build it; instead
1Kings8:20 The LORD has fulfilled what he promised. I have taken the place of my father David
1Kings8:21 I have provided a place there for the ark
1Kings8:22 Then Solomon stood before the altar of the LORD in front of the entire congregation of Israel and spread out his hands toward heaven.
1Kings8:23 He said: LORD God of Israel
1Kings8:24 You have kept what you promised to your servant
1Kings8:25 Therefore
1Kings8:26 Now LORD God of Israel
1Kings8:27 But will God indeed live on earth? Even heaven
1Kings8:28 Listen to your servant’s prayer and his petition
1Kings8:29 so that your eyes may watch over this temple night and day
1Kings8:30 Hear the petition of your servant and your people Israel
1Kings8:31 When a man sins against his neighbor and is forced to take an oath
1Kings8:32 may you hear in heaven and act. May you judge your servants
1Kings8:33 When your people Israel are defeated before an enemy
1Kings8:34 may you hear in heaven and forgive the sin of your people Israel. May you restore them to the land you gave their ancestors.
1Kings8:35 When the skies are shut and there is no rain
1Kings8:36 may you hear in heaven and forgive the sin of your servants and your people Israel
1Kings8:37 When there is famine in the land
1Kings8:38 every prayer or petition that any person or that all your people Israel may have — they each know their own affliction  — as they spread out their hands toward this temple
1Kings8:39 may you hear in heaven
1Kings8:40 so that they may fear you all the days they live on the land you gave our ancestors.
1Kings8:41 Even for the foreigner who is not of your people Israel but has come from a distant land because of your name —
1Kings8:42 for they will hear of your great name
1Kings8:43 may you hear in heaven
1Kings8:44 When your people go out to fight against their enemies
1Kings8:45 may you hear their prayer and petition in heaven and uphold their cause.
1Kings8:46 When they sin against you — for there is no one who does not sin  — and you are angry with them and hand them over to the enemy
1Kings8:47 and when they come to their senses in the land where they were deported and repent and petition you in their captors’ land: “We have sinned and done wrong; we have been wicked
1Kings8:48 and when they return to you with all their heart and all their soul in the land of their enemies who took them captive
1Kings8:49 may you hear in heaven
1Kings8:50 May you forgive your people who sinned against you and all their rebellions against you
1Kings8:51 For they are your people and your inheritance; you brought them out of Egypt
1Kings8:52 May your eyes be open to your servant’s petition and to the petition of your people Israel
1Kings8:53 For you
1Kings8:54 When Solomon finished praying this entire prayer and petition to the LORD
1Kings8:55 and he stood and blessed the whole congregation of Israel with a loud voice:
1Kings8:56 “Blessed be the LORD! He has given rest to his people Israel according to all he has said. Not one of all the good promises he made through his servant Moses has failed.
1Kings8:57 May the LORD our God be with us as he was with our ancestors. May he not abandon us or leave us
1Kings8:58 so that he causes us to be devoted to him
1Kings8:59 May my words with which I have made my petition before the LORD be near the LORD our God day and night. May he uphold his servant’s cause and the cause of his people Israel
1Kings8:60 May all the peoples of the earth know that the LORD is God. There is no other!
1Kings8:61 Be wholeheartedly devoted to the LORD our God to walk in his statutes and to keep his commands
1Kings8:62 The king and all Israel with him were offering sacrifices in the LORD’s presence.
1Kings8:63 Solomon offered a sacrifice of fellowship offerings to the LORD: twenty-two thousand cattle and one hundred twenty thousand sheep and goats. In this manner the king and all the Israelites dedicated the LORD’s temple.
1Kings8:64 On the same day
1Kings8:65 Solomon and all Israel with him — a great assembly
1Kings8:66 On the fifteenth day he sent the people away. So they blessed the king and went to their homes rejoicing and with happy hearts for all the goodness that the LORD had done for his servant David and for his people Israel.

2Kings16:1 In the seventeenth year of Pekah son of Remaliah
2Kings16:2 Ahaz was twenty years old when he became king

2Kings16:7 So Ahaz sent messengers to King Tiglath-pileser of Assyria

2Kings17:1 In the twelfth year of Judah’s King Ahaz
2Kings17:2 He did what was evil in the LORD’s sight

2Kings17:4 But the king of Assyria caught Hoshea in a conspiracy: He had sent envoys to So king of Egypt and had not paid tribute to the king of Assyria as in previous years. Therefore the king of Assyria arrested him and put him in prison.

2Kings17:6 In the ninth year of Hoshea
2Kings17:7 This disaster happened because the people of Israel sinned against the LORD their God who had brought them out of the land of Egypt from the power of Pharaoh king of Egypt and because they worshiped other gods.
2Kings17:8 They lived according to the customs of the nations that the LORD had dispossessed before the Israelites and according to what the kings of Israel did.
2Kings17:9 The Israelites secretly did things against the LORD their God that were not right. They built high places in all their towns from watchtower to fortified city.
2Kings17:10 They set up for themselves sacred pillars and Asherah poles on every high hill and under every green tree.
2Kings17:11 They burned incense there on all the high places just like the nations that the LORD had driven out before them had done. They did evil things
2Kings17:12 They served idols
2Kings17:13 Still
2Kings17:14 But they would not listen. Instead they became obstinate like their ancestors who did not believe the LORD their God.
2Kings17:15 They rejected his statutes and his covenant he had made with their ancestors and the warnings he had given them. They followed worthless idols and became worthless themselves
2Kings17:16 They abandoned all the commands of the LORD their God. They made cast images for themselves
2Kings17:17 They sacrificed their sons and daughters in the fire and practiced divination and interpreted omens. They devoted themselves to do what was evil in the LORD’s sight and angered him.
2Kings17:18 Therefore
2Kings17:19 Even Judah did not keep the commands of the LORD their God but lived according to the customs Israel had practiced.
2Kings17:20 So the LORD rejected all the descendants of Israel
2Kings17:21 When the LORD tore Israel from the house of David
2Kings17:22 The Israelites persisted in all the sins that Jeroboam committed and did not turn away from them.
2Kings17:23 Finally

2Kings17:28 So one of the priests they had deported came and lived in Bethel
2Kings17:29 But the people of each nation were still making their own gods in the cities where they lived and putting them in the shrines of the high places that the people of Samaria had made.

2Kings17:32 They feared the LORD
2Kings17:33 They feared the LORD

1Chronicles28:1 David assembled all the leaders of Israel in Jerusalem: the leaders of the tribes

1Chronicles28:4 “Yet the LORD God of Israel chose me out of all my father’s family to be king over Israel forever. For he chose Judah as leader
1Chronicles28:5 And out of all my sons  — for the LORD has given me many sons — he has chosen my son Solomon to sit on the throne of the LORD’s kingdom over Israel.
1Chronicles28:6 He said to me
1Chronicles28:7 I will establish his kingdom forever if he perseveres in keeping my commands and my ordinances as he is doing today.’
1Chronicles28:8 “So now in the sight of all Israel
1Chronicles28:9 “As for you

1Chronicles28:14 the weight of gold for all the articles for every kind of service; the weight of all the silver articles for every kind of service;
1Chronicles28:15 the weight of the gold lampstands and their gold lamps

1Chronicles28:17 the pure gold for the forks
1Chronicles28:18 the weight of refined gold for the altar of incense; and the plans for the chariot of the gold cherubim that spread out their wings and cover the ark of the LORD’s covenant.
1Chronicles28:19 David concluded
1Chronicles28:20 Then David said to his son Solomon

2Chronicles1:17 A chariot could be imported from Egypt for fifteen pounds of silver and a horse for nearly four pounds. In the same way

2Chronicles3:1 Then Solomon began to build the LORD’s temple in Jerusalem on Mount Moriah where the LORD had appeared to his father David
2Chronicles3:2 He began to build on the second day of the second month in the fourth year of his reign.
2Chronicles3:3 These are Solomon’s foundations for building God’s temple: the length was ninety feet
2Chronicles3:4 The portico
2Chronicles3:5 The larger room he paneled with cypress wood
2Chronicles3:6 He adorned the temple with precious stones for beauty
2Chronicles3:7 He overlaid the temple — the beams
2Chronicles3:8 Then he made the most holy place; its length corresponded to the width of the temple
2Chronicles3:9 The weight of the nails was twenty ounces of gold
2Chronicles3:10 He made two cherubim of sculptured work
2Chronicles3:11 The overall length of the wings of the cherubim was 30 feet: the wing of one was 7½ feet
2Chronicles3:12 The wing of the other cherub was 7½ feet
2Chronicles3:13 The wingspan of these cherubim was 30 feet. They stood on their feet and faced the larger room.
2Chronicles3:14 He made the curtain of blue
2Chronicles3:15 In front of the temple he made two pillars
2Chronicles3:16 He had made chainwork in the inner sanctuary and also put it on top of the pillars. He made a hundred pomegranates and fastened them into the chainwork.
2Chronicles3:17 Then he set up the pillars in front of the sanctuary

2Chronicles6:8 However
2Chronicles6:9 Yet
2Chronicles6:10 So the LORD has fulfilled what he promised. I have taken the place of my father David and I sit on the throne of Israel
2Chronicles6:11 I have put the ark there
2Chronicles6:12 Then Solomon stood before the altar of the LORD in front of the entire congregation of Israel and spread out his hands.
2Chronicles6:13 For Solomon had made a bronze platform 7½ feet long

2Chronicles6:16 Therefore
2Chronicles6:17 Now
2Chronicles6:18 But will God indeed live on earth with humans? Even heaven
2Chronicles6:19 Listen to your servant’s prayer and his petition
2Chronicles6:20 so that your eyes watch over this temple day and night
2Chronicles6:21 Hear the petitions of your servant and your people Israel
2Chronicles6:22 If a man sins against his neighbor and is forced to take an oath and he comes to take an oath before your altar in this temple
2Chronicles6:23 may you hear in heaven and act. May you judge your servants
2Chronicles6:24 If your people Israel are defeated before an enemy
2Chronicles6:25 may you hear in heaven and forgive the sin of your people Israel. May you restore them to the land you gave them and their ancestors.
2Chronicles6:26 When the skies are shut and there is no rain because they have sinned against you
2Chronicles6:27 may you hear in heaven and forgive the sin of your servants and your people Israel
2Chronicles6:28 When there is famine in the land
2Chronicles6:29 every prayer or petition that any person or that all your people Israel may have — they each know their own affliction and suffering — as they spread out their hands toward this temple
2Chronicles6:30 may you hear in heaven
2Chronicles6:31 so that they may fear you and walk in your ways all the days they live on the land you gave our ancestors.
2Chronicles6:32 Even for the foreigner who is not of your people Israel but has come from a distant land because of your great name and your strong hand and outstretched arm: when he comes and prays toward this temple
2Chronicles6:33 may you hear in heaven in your dwelling place
2Chronicles6:34 When your people go out to fight against their enemies
2Chronicles6:35 may you hear their prayer and petition in heaven and uphold their cause.
2Chronicles6:36 When they sin against you — for there is no one who does not sin  — and you are angry with them and hand them over to the enemy
2Chronicles6:37 and when they come to their senses in the land where they were deported and repent and petition you in their captors’ land
2Chronicles6:38 and when they return to you with all their mind and all their heart in the land of their captivity where they were taken captive
2Chronicles6:39 may you hear their prayer and petitions in heaven
2Chronicles6:40 Now
2Chronicles6:41 Now therefore: Arise
2Chronicles6:42 LORD God

2Chronicles8:8 their descendants who remained in the land after them
2Chronicles8:9 But Solomon did not consign the Israelites to be slaves for his work; they were soldiers
2Chronicles8:10 These were King Solomon’s deputies: 250 who supervised the people.

2Chronicles8:12 At that time Solomon offered burnt offerings to the LORD on the LORD’s altar he had made in front of the portico.
2Chronicles8:13 He followed the daily requirement for offerings according to the commandment of Moses for Sabbaths
2Chronicles8:14 According to the ordinances of his father David
2Chronicles8:15 They did not turn aside from the king’s command regarding the priests and the Levites concerning any matter or concerning the treasuries.
2Chronicles8:16 All of Solomon’s work was carried out from the day the foundation was laid for the LORD’s temple until it was finished. So the LORD’s temple was completed.

2Chronicles9:1 The queen of Sheba heard of Solomon’s fame
2Chronicles9:2 So Solomon answered all her questions; nothing was too difficult for Solomon to explain to her.
2Chronicles9:3 When the queen of Sheba observed Solomon’s wisdom
2Chronicles9:4 the food at his table
2Chronicles9:5 She said to the king
2Chronicles9:6 But I didn’t believe their reports until I came and saw with my own eyes. Indeed
2Chronicles9:7 How happy are your men. How happy are these servants of yours
2Chronicles9:8 Blessed be the LORD your God! He delighted in you and put you on his throne as king for the LORD your God. Because your God loved Israel enough to establish them forever
2Chronicles9:9 Then she gave the king four and a half tons of gold
2Chronicles9:10 In addition
2Chronicles9:11 The king made the algum wood into walkways for the LORD’s temple and for the king’s palace and into lyres and harps for the singers. Never before had anything like them been seen in the land of Judah.
2Chronicles9:12 King Solomon gave the queen of Sheba her every desire
2Chronicles9:13 The weight of gold that came to Solomon annually was twenty-five tons
2Chronicles9:14 besides what was brought by the merchants and traders. All the Arabian kings and governors of the land also brought gold and silver to Solomon.
2Chronicles9:15 King Solomon made two hundred large shields of hammered gold; 15 pounds of hammered gold went into each shield.
2Chronicles9:16 He made three hundred small shields of hammered gold; 7½ pounds of gold went into each shield. The king put them in the House of the Forest of Lebanon.
2Chronicles9:17 The king also made a large ivory throne and overlaid it with pure gold.
2Chronicles9:18 The throne had six steps; there was a footstool covered in gold for the throne
2Chronicles9:19 Twelve lions were standing there on the six steps
2Chronicles9:20 All of King Solomon’s drinking cups were gold
2Chronicles9:21 for the king’s ships kept going to Tarshish with Hiram’s servants
2Chronicles9:22 King Solomon surpassed all the kings of the world in riches and wisdom.
2Chronicles9:23 All the kings of the world wanted an audience with Solomon to hear the wisdom God had put in his heart.

2Chronicles25:19 You have said
2Chronicles25:20 But Amaziah would not listen

2Chronicles25:22 Judah was routed before Israel
2Chronicles25:23 King Jehoash of Israel captured Judah’s King Amaziah son of Joash
2Chronicles25:24 He took all the gold
2Chronicles25:25 Judah’s King Amaziah son of Joash lived fifteen years after the death of Israel’s King Jehoash son of Jehoahaz.

2Chronicles26:7 God helped him against the Philistines

2Chronicles26:10 Since he had many cattle both in the Judean foothills and the plain

2Chronicles26:13 Under their authority was an army of 307
2Chronicles26:14 Uzziah provided the entire army with shields

2Chronicles27:6 So Jotham strengthened his position because he did not waver in obeying the LORD his God.

Nehemiah9:13 You came down on Mount Sinai

Nehemiah9:15 You provided bread from heaven for their hunger; you brought them water from the rock for their thirst. You told them to go in and possess the land you had sworn to give them.
Nehemiah9:16 But our ancestors acted arrogantly; they became stiff-necked and did not listen to your commands.
Nehemiah9:17 They refused to listen and did not remember your wonders you performed among them. They became stiff-necked and appointed a leader to return to their slavery in Egypt. But you are a forgiving God
Nehemiah9:18 Even after they had cast an image of a calf for themselves and said
Nehemiah9:19 you did not abandon them in the wilderness because of your great compassion. During the day the pillar of cloud never turned away from them

Nehemiah9:21 You provided for them in the wilderness forty years
Nehemiah9:22 You gave them kingdoms and peoples and established boundaries for them. They took possession of the land of King Sihon of Heshbon and of the land of King Og of Bashan.
Nehemiah9:23 You multiplied their descendants like the stars of the sky and brought them to the land you told their ancestors to go in and possess.

Nehemiah9:25 They captured fortified cities and fertile land and took possession of well-supplied houses
Nehemiah9:26 But they were disobedient and rebelled against you. They flung your law behind their backs and killed your prophets who warned them in order to turn them back to you. They committed terrible blasphemies.
Nehemiah9:27 So you handed them over to their enemies
Nehemiah9:28 But as soon as they had relief
Nehemiah9:29 You warned them to turn back to your law

Nehemiah9:33 You are righteous concerning all that has happened to us

Esther1:1 These events took place during the days of Ahasuerus
Esther1:2 In those days King Ahasuerus reigned from his royal throne in the fortress at Susa.
Esther1:3 He held a feast in the third year of his reign for all his officials and staff
Esther1:4 He displayed the glorious wealth of his kingdom and the magnificent splendor of his greatness for a total of 180 days.
Esther1:5 At the end of this time
Esther1:6 White and violet linen hangings were fastened with fine white and purple linen cords to silver rods on marble columns. Gold and silver couches were arranged on a mosaic pavement of red feldspar
Esther1:7 Drinks were served in an array of gold goblets
Esther1:8 The drinking was according to royal decree: “There are no restrictions.” The king had ordered every wine steward in his household to serve whatever each person wanted.
Esther1:9 Queen Vashti also gave a feast for the women of King Ahasuerus’s palace.

Esther1:11 to bring Queen Vashti before him with her royal crown. He wanted to show off her beauty to the people and the officials
Esther1:12 But Queen Vashti refused to come at the king’s command that was delivered by his eunuchs. The king became furious and his anger burned within him.

Esther9:2 In each of King Ahasuerus’s provinces the Jews assembled in their cities to attack those who intended to harm them. Not a single person could withstand them; fear of them fell on every nationality.

Esther9:12 The king said to Queen Esther
Esther9:13 Esther answered
Esther9:14 The king gave the orders for this to be done
Esther9:15 The Jews in Susa assembled again on the fourteenth day of the month of Adar and killed three hundred men in Susa
Esther9:16 The rest of the Jews in the royal provinces assembled
Esther9:17 They fought on the thirteenth day of the month of Adar and rested on the fourteenth
Esther9:18 But the Jews in Susa had assembled on the thirteenth and the fourteenth days of the month. They rested on the fifteenth day of the month
Esther9:19 This explains why the rural Jews who live in villages observe the fourteenth day of the month of Adar as a time of rejoicing and feasting. It is a holiday when they send gifts to one another.
Esther9:20 Mordecai recorded these events and sent letters to all the Jews in all of King Ahasuerus’s provinces
Esther9:21 He ordered them to celebrate the fourteenth and fifteenth days of the month of Adar every year
Esther9:22 because during those days the Jews gained relief from their enemies. That was the month when their sorrow was turned into rejoicing and their mourning into a holiday. They were to be days of feasting
Esther9:23 So the Jews agreed to continue the practice they had begun
Esther9:24 For Haman son of Hammedatha the Agagite
Esther9:25 But when the matter was brought before the king
Esther9:26 For this reason these days are called Purim
Esther9:27 the Jews bound themselves
Esther9:28 These days are remembered and celebrated by every generation
Esther9:29 Queen Esther
Esther9:30 He sent letters with assurances of peace and security to all the Jews who were in the 127 provinces of the kingdom of Ahasuerus
Esther9:31 in order to confirm these days of Purim at their proper time just as Mordecai the Jew and Esther the queen had established them and just as they had committed themselves and their descendants to the practices of fasting and lamentation.
Esther9:32 So Esther’s command confirmed these customs of Purim
Esther10:1 King Ahasuerus imposed a tax throughout the land even to the farthest shores.
Esther10:2 All of his powerful and magnificent accomplishments and the detailed account of Mordecai’s great rank with which the king had honored him
Esther10:3 Mordecai the Jew was second only to King Ahasuerus. He was famous among the Jews and highly esteemed by many of his relatives. He continued to pursue prosperity for his people and to speak for the well-being of all his descendants.
Job1:1 There was a man in the country of Uz named Job. He was a man of complete integrity
Job1:2 He had seven sons and three daughters.
Job1:3 His estate included seven thousand sheep and goats
Job1:4 His sons used to take turns having banquets at their homes. They would send an invitation to their three sisters to eat and drink with them.
Job1:5 Whenever a round of banqueting was over
Job1:6 One day the sons of God came to present themselves before the LORD
Job1:7 The LORD asked Satan
Job1:8 Then the LORD said to Satan
Job1:9 Satan answered the LORD
Job1:10 Haven’t you placed a hedge around him
Job1:11 But stretch out your hand and strike everything he owns
Job1:12 “Very well
Job1:13 One day when Job’s sons and daughters were eating and drinking wine in their oldest brother’s house
Job1:14 a messenger came to Job and reported: “While the oxen were plowing and the donkeys grazing nearby
Job1:15 the Sabeans swooped down and took them away. They struck down the servants with the sword
Job1:16 He was still speaking when another messenger came and reported: “God’s fire fell from heaven. It burned the sheep and the servants and devoured them
Job1:17 That messenger was still speaking when yet another came and reported: “The Chaldeans formed three bands
Job1:18 He was still speaking when another messenger came and reported: “Your sons and daughters were eating and drinking wine in their oldest brother’s house.
Job1:19 Suddenly a powerful wind swept in from the desert and struck the four corners of the house. It collapsed on the young people so that they died
Job1:20 Then Job stood up
Job1:21 saying: Naked I came from my mother’s womb
Job1:22 Throughout all this Job did not sin or blame God for anything.
Job2:1 One day the sons of God came again to present themselves before the LORD
Job2:2 The LORD asked Satan
Job2:3 Then the LORD said to Satan
Job2:4 “Skin for skin!” Satan answered the LORD. “A man will give up everything he owns in exchange for his life.
Job2:5 But stretch out your hand and strike his flesh and bones
Job2:6 “Very well
Job2:7 So Satan left the LORD’s presence and infected Job with terrible boils from the soles of his feet to the top of his head.
Job2:8 Then Job took a piece of broken pottery to scrape himself while he sat among the ashes.
Job2:9 His wife said to him
Job2:10 “You speak as a foolish woman speaks
Job2:11 Now when Job’s three friends — Eliphaz the Temanite
Job2:12 When they looked from a distance
Job2:13 Then they sat on the ground with him seven days and nights
Job3:1 After this
Job3:2 He said:
Job3:3 May the day I was born perish
Job3:4 If only that day had turned to darkness! May God above not care about it
Job3:5 May darkness and gloom reclaim it
Job3:6 If only darkness had taken that night away! May it not appear among the days of the year or be listed in the calendar.
Job3:7 Yes
Job3:8 Let those who curse days condemn it
Job3:9 May its morning stars grow dark. May it wait for daylight but have none; may it not see the breaking of dawn.
Job3:10 For that night did not shut the doors of my mother’s womb
Job3:11 Why was I not stillborn; why didn’t I die as I came from the womb?
Job3:12 Why did the knees receive me
Job3:13 Now I would certainly be lying down in peace; I would be asleep. Then I would be at rest
Job3:14 with the kings and counselors of the earth
Job3:15 or with princes who had gold
Job3:16 Or why was I not hidden like a miscarried child
Job3:17 There the wicked cease to make trouble
Job3:18 The captives are completely at rest; they do not hear a taskmaster’s voice.
Job3:19 Both small and great are there
Job3:20 Why is light given to one burdened with grief
Job3:21 who wait for death
Job3:22 who are filled with much joy and are glad when they reach the grave?
Job3:23 Why is life given to a man whose path is hidden
Job3:24 I sigh when food is put before me
Job3:25 For the thing I feared has overtaken me
Job3:26 I cannot relax or be calm; I have no rest
Job4:1 Then Eliphaz the Temanite replied:
Job4:2 Should anyone try to speak with you when you are exhausted? Yet who can keep from speaking?
Job4:3 Indeed
Job4:4 Your words have steadied the one who was stumbling and braced the knees that were buckling.
Job4:5 But now that this has happened to you
Job4:6 Isn’t your piety your confidence
Job4:7 Consider: Who has perished when he was innocent? Where have the honest been destroyed?
Job4:8 In my experience
Job4:9 They perish at a single blast from God and come to an end by the breath of his nostrils.
Job4:10 The lion may roar and the fierce lion growl
Job4:11 The strong lion dies if it catches no prey
Job4:12 A word was brought to me in secret; my ears caught a whisper of it.
Job4:13 Among unsettling thoughts from visions in the night
Job4:14 fear and trembling came over me and made all my bones shake.
Job4:15 I felt a draft on my face
Job4:16 A figure stood there
Job4:17 “Can a mortal be righteous before God? Can a man be more pure than his Maker?”
Job4:18 If God puts no trust in his servants and he charges his angels with foolishness
Job4:19 how much more those who dwell in clay houses
Job4:20 They are smashed to pieces from dawn to dusk; they perish forever while no one notices.
Job4:21 Are their tent cords not pulled up? They die without wisdom.
Job5:1 Call out! Will anyone answer you? Which of the holy ones will you turn to?
Job5:2 For anger kills a fool
Job5:3 I have seen a fool taking root
Job5:4 His children are far from safety. They are crushed at the city gate
Job5:5 The hungry consume his harvest
Job5:6 For distress does not grow out of the soil
Job5:7 But humans are born for trouble as surely as sparks fly upward.
Job5:8 However
Job5:9 He does great and unsearchable things
Job5:10 He gives rain to the earth and sends water to the fields.
Job5:11 He sets the lowly on high
Job5:12 He frustrates the schemes of the crafty so that they achieve no success.
Job5:13 He traps the wise in their craftiness so that the plans of the deceptive are quickly brought to an end.
Job5:14 They encounter darkness by day
Job5:15 He saves the needy from their sharp words and from the clutches of the powerful.
Job5:16 So the poor have hope
Job5:17 See how happy is the person whom God corrects; so do not reject the discipline of the Almighty.
Job5:18 For he wounds but he also bandages; he strikes
Job5:19 He will rescue you from six calamities; no harm will touch you in seven.
Job5:20 In famine he will redeem you from death
Job5:21 You will be safe from slander and not fear destruction when it comes.
Job5:22 You will laugh at destruction and hunger and not fear the land’s wild creatures.
Job5:23 For you will have a covenant with the stones of the field
Job5:24 You will know that your tent is secure
Job5:25 You will also know that your offspring will be many and your descendants like the grass of the earth.
Job5:26 You will approach the grave in full vigor
Job5:27 We have investigated this
Job6:1 Then Job answered:
Job6:2 If only my grief could be weighed and my devastation placed with it on the scales.
Job6:3 For then it would outweigh the sand of the seas! That is why my words are rash.
Job6:4 Surely the arrows of the Almighty have pierced me; my spirit drinks their poison. God’s terrors are arrayed against me.
Job6:5 Does a wild donkey bray over fresh grass or an ox low over its fodder?
Job6:6 Is bland food eaten without salt? Is there flavor in an egg white?
Job6:7 I refuse to touch them; they are like contaminated food.
Job6:8 If only my request would be granted and God would provide what I hope for:
Job6:9 that he would decide to crush me
Job6:10 It would still bring me comfort
Job6:11 What strength do I have
Job6:12 Is my strength that of stone
Job6:13 Since I cannot help myself
Job6:14 A despairing man should receive loyalty from his friends
Job6:15 My brothers are as treacherous as a wadi
Job6:16 and become darkened because of ice
Job6:17 The wadis evaporate in warm weather; they disappear from their channels in hot weather.
Job6:18 Caravans turn away from their routes
Job6:19 The caravans of Tema look for these streams. The traveling merchants of Sheba hope for them.
Job6:20 They are ashamed because they had been confident of finding water. When they arrive there
Job6:21 So this is what you have now become to me. When you see something dreadful
Job6:22 Have I ever said: “Give me something” or “Pay a bribe for me from your wealth”
Job6:23 or “Deliver me from the enemy’s hand” or “Redeem me from the hand of the ruthless”?
Job6:24 Teach me
Job6:25 How painful honest words can be! But what does your rebuke prove?
Job6:26 Do you think that you can disprove my words or that a despairing man’s words are mere wind?
Job6:27 No doubt you would cast lots for a fatherless child and negotiate a price to sell your friend.
Job6:28 But now
Job6:29 Reconsider; don’t be unjust. Reconsider; my righteousness is still the issue.
Job6:30 Is there injustice on my tongue or can my palate not taste disaster?
Job7:1 Isn’t each person consigned to forced labor on earth? Are not his days like those of a hired worker?
Job7:2 Like a slave he longs for shade; like a hired worker he waits for his pay.
Job7:3 So I have been made to inherit months of futility
Job7:4 When I lie down I think
Job7:5 My flesh is clothed with maggots and encrusted with dirt. My skin forms scabs and then oozes.
Job7:6 My days pass more swiftly than a weaver’s shuttle; they come to an end without hope.
Job7:7 Remember that my life is but a breath. My eye will never again see anything good.
Job7:8 The eye of anyone who looks on me will no longer see me. Your eyes will look for me
Job7:9 As a cloud fades away and vanishes
Job7:10 He will never return to his house; his hometown will no longer remember him.
Job7:11 Therefore I will not restrain my mouth. I will speak in the anguish of my spirit; I will complain in the bitterness of my soul.
Job7:12 Am I the sea or a sea monster
Job7:13 When I say
Job7:14 then you frighten me with dreams
Job7:15 so that I prefer strangling  — death rather than life in this body.

Job8:2 How long will you go on saying these things? Your words are a blast of wind.

Job8:4 Since your children sinned against him
Job8:5 But if you earnestly seek God and ask the Almighty for mercy
Job8:6 if you are pure and upright
Job8:7 Then
Job8:8 For ask the previous generation
Job8:9 since we were born only yesterday and know nothing. Our days on earth are but a shadow.
Job8:10 Will they not teach you and tell you and speak from their understanding?
Job8:11 Does papyrus grow where there is no marsh? Do reeds flourish without water?
Job8:12 While still uncut shoots
Job8:13 Such is the destiny of all who forget God; the hope of the godless will perish.
Job8:14 His source of confidence is fragile; what he trusts in is a spider’s web.
Job8:15 He leans on his web
Job8:16 He is a well-watered plant in the sunshine; his shoots spread out over his garden.
Job8:17 His roots are intertwined around a pile of rocks. He looks for a home among the stones.
Job8:18 If he is uprooted from his place
Job8:19 Surely this is the joy of his way of life; yet others will sprout from the dust.
Job8:20 Look
Job8:21 He will yet fill your mouth with laughter and your lips with a shout of joy.
Job8:22 Your enemies will be clothed with shame; the tent of the wicked will no longer exist.

Job9:2 Yes
Job9:3 If one wanted to take him to court

Job9:32 For he is not a man like me

Job12:1 Then Job answered:
Job12:2 No doubt you are the people
Job12:3 But I also have a mind like you; I am not inferior to you. Who doesn’t know the things you are talking about?

Job12:5 The one who is at ease holds calamity in contempt and thinks it is prepared for those whose feet are slipping.
Job12:6 The tents of robbers are safe
Job12:7 But ask the animals

Job12:10 The life of every living thing is in his hand
Job12:11 Doesn’t the ear test words as the palate tastes food?
Job12:12 Wisdom is found with the elderly

Job12:16 True wisdom and power belong to him. The deceived and the deceiver are his.

Job13:2 Everything you know
Job13:3 Yet I prefer to speak to the Almighty and argue my case before God.

Job13:8 Would you show partiality to him or argue the case in his defense?
Job13:9 Would it go well if he examined you? Could you deceive him as you would deceive a man?

Job14:1 Anyone born of woman is short of days and full of trouble.
Job14:2 He blossoms like a flower
Job14:3 Do you really take notice of one like this? Will you bring me into judgment against you?
Job14:4 Who can produce something pure from what is impure? No one!
Job14:5 Since a person’s days are determined and the number of his months depends on you
Job14:6 look away from him and let him rest so that he can enjoy his day like a hired worker.
Job14:7 There is hope for a tree: If it is cut down
Job14:8 If its roots grow old in the ground and its stump starts to die in the soil
Job14:9 the scent of water makes it thrive and produce twigs like a sapling.
Job14:10 But a person dies and fades away; he breathes his last — where is he?
Job14:11 As water disappears from a lake and a river becomes parched and dry
Job14:12 so people lie down never to rise again. They will not wake up until the heavens are no more; they will not stir from their sleep.

Job14:15 You would call

Job14:19 as water wears away stones and torrents wash away the soil from the land
Job14:20 You completely overpower him
Job14:21 If his sons receive honor
Job14:22 He feels only the pain of his own body and mourns only for himself.

Job15:22 He doesn’t believe he will return from darkness; he is destined for the sword.

Job15:24 Trouble and distress terrify him

Job15:27 Though his face is covered with fat and his waistline bulges with it
