Job15:31 Let him not put trust in worthless things
Job15:32 It will be accomplished before his time
Job15:33 He will be like a vine that drops its unripe grapes and like an olive tree that sheds its blossoms.
Job15:34 For the company of the godless will have no children
Job15:35 They conceive trouble and give birth to evil; their womb prepares deception.
Job16:1 Then Job answered:
Job16:2 I have heard many things like these. You are all miserable comforters.
Job16:3 Is there no end to your empty words? What provokes you that you continue testifying?
Job16:4 If you were in my place I could also talk like you. I could string words together against you and shake my head at you.
Job16:5 Instead
Job16:6 If I speak
Job16:7 Surely he has now exhausted me. You have devastated my entire family.
Job16:8 You have shriveled me up  — it has become a witness; my frailty rises up against me and testifies to my face.
Job16:9 His anger tears at me
Job16:10 They open their mouths against me and strike my cheeks with contempt; they join themselves together against me.
Job16:11 God hands me over to the unjust; he throws me to the wicked.
Job16:12 I was at ease
Job16:13 his archers surround me. He pierces my kidneys without mercy and pours my bile on the ground.

Job16:17 although my hands are free from violence and my prayer is pure.

Job16:19 Even now my witness is in heaven
Job16:20 My friends scoff at me as I weep before God.
Job16:21 I wish that someone might argue for a man with God just as anyone would for a friend.
Job16:22 For only a few years will pass before I go the way of no return.

Job18:5 Yes
Job18:6 The light in his tent grows dark
Job18:7 His powerful stride is shortened
Job18:8 For his own feet lead him into a net
Job18:9 A trap catches him by the heel; a noose seizes him.
Job18:10 A rope lies hidden for him on the ground
Job18:11 Terrors frighten him on every side and harass him at every step.
Job18:12 His strength is depleted; disaster lies ready for him to stumble.
Job18:13 Parts of his skin are eaten away; death’s firstborn consumes his limbs.
Job18:14 He is ripped from the security of his tent and marched away to the king of terrors.
Job18:15 Nothing he owned remains in his tent. Burning sulfur is scattered over his home.
Job18:16 His roots below dry up
Job18:17 All memory of him perishes from the earth; he has no name anywhere.
Job18:18 He is driven from light to darkness and chased from the inhabited world.
Job18:19 He has no children or descendants among his people
Job18:20 Those in the west are appalled at his fate
Job18:21 Indeed
Job19:1 Then Job answered:
Job19:2 How long will you torment me and crush me with words?
Job19:3 You have humiliated me ten times now
Job19:4 Even if it is true that I have sinned
Job19:5 If you really want to appear superior to me and would use my disgrace as evidence against me
Job19:6 then understand that it is God who has wronged me and caught me in his net.
Job19:7 I cry out: “Violence!” but get no response; I call for help
Job19:8 He has blocked my way so that I cannot pass through; he has veiled my paths with darkness.
Job19:9 He has stripped me of my honor and removed the crown from my head.
Job19:10 He tears me down on every side so that I am ruined. He uproots my hope like a tree.
Job19:11 His anger burns against me
Job19:12 His troops advance together; they construct a ramp against me and camp around my tent.
Job19:13 He has removed my brothers from me; my acquaintances have abandoned me.
Job19:14 My relatives stop coming by
Job19:15 My house guests and female servants regard me as a stranger; I am a foreigner in their sight.
Job19:16 I call for my servant
Job19:17 My breath is offensive to my wife
Job19:18 Even young boys scorn me. When I stand up
Job19:19 All of my best friends despise me
Job19:20 My skin and my flesh cling to my bones; I have escaped with only the skin of my teeth.
Job19:21 Have mercy on me
Job19:22 Why do you persecute me as God does? Will you never get enough of my flesh?
Job19:23 I wish that my words were written down
Job19:24 or were inscribed in stone forever by an iron stylus and lead!
Job19:25 But I know that my Redeemer lives
Job19:26 Even after my skin has been destroyed
Job19:27 I will see him myself; my eyes will look at him
Job19:28 If you say
Job19:29 then be afraid of the sword
Job20:1 Then Zophar the Naamathite replied:
Job20:2 This is why my unsettling thoughts compel me to answer
Job20:3 I have heard a rebuke that insults me
Job20:4 Don’t you know that ever since antiquity
Job20:5 the joy of the wicked has been brief and the happiness of the godless has lasted only a moment?
Job20:6 Though his arrogance reaches heaven
Job20:7 he will vanish forever like his own dung. Those who know him will ask
Job20:8 He will fly away like a dream and never be found; he will be chased away like a vision in the night.
Job20:9 The eye that saw him will see him no more
Job20:10 His children will beg from the poor
Job20:11 His frame may be full of youthful vigor
Job20:12 Though evil tastes sweet in his mouth and he conceals it under his tongue
Job20:13 though he cherishes it and will not let it go but keeps it in his mouth
Job20:14 yet the food in his stomach turns into cobras’ venom inside him.
Job20:15 He swallows wealth but must vomit it up; God will force it from his stomach.
Job20:16 He will suck the poison of cobras; a viper’s fangs will kill him.
Job20:17 He will not enjoy the streams
Job20:18 He must return the fruit of his labor without consuming it; he doesn’t enjoy the profits from his trading.
Job20:19 For he oppressed and abandoned the poor; he seized a house he did not build.
Job20:20 Because his appetite is never satisfied
Job20:21 Nothing is left for him to consume; therefore
Job20:22 At the height of his success distress will come to him; the full weight of misery will crush him.
Job20:23 When he fills his stomach
Job20:24 If he flees from an iron weapon
Job20:25 He pulls it out of his back
Job20:26 Total darkness is reserved for his treasures. A fire unfanned by human hands will consume him; it will feed on what is left in his tent.
Job20:27 The heavens will expose his iniquity
Job20:28 The possessions in his house will be removed
Job20:29 This is the wicked person’s lot from God
Job21:1 Then Job answered:
Job21:2 Pay close attention to my words; let this be the consolation you offer.
Job21:3 Bear with me while I speak; then after I have spoken
Job21:4 As for me
Job21:5 Look at me and shudder; put your hand over your mouth.
Job21:6 When I think about it
Job21:7 Why do the wicked continue to live
Job21:8 Their children are established while they are still alive
Job21:9 Their homes are secure and free of fear; no rod from God strikes them.
Job21:10 Their bulls breed without fail; their cows calve and do not miscarry.
Job21:11 They let their little ones run around like lambs; their children skip about
Job21:12 singing to the tambourine and lyre and rejoicing at the sound of the flute.
Job21:13 They spend their days in prosperity and go down to Sheol in peace.
Job21:14 Yet they say to God
Job21:15 Who is the Almighty
Job21:16 But their prosperity is not of their own doing. The counsel of the wicked is far from me!
Job21:17 How often is the lamp of the wicked put out? Does disaster come on them? Does he apportion destruction in his anger?
Job21:18 Are they like straw before the wind
Job21:19 God reserves a person’s punishment for his children. Let God repay the person himself
Job21:20 Let his own eyes see his demise; let him drink from the Almighty’s wrath!
Job21:21 For what does he care about his family once he is dead
Job21:22 Can anyone teach God knowledge
Job21:23 One person dies in excellent health
Job21:24 His body is well fed
Job21:25 Yet another person dies with a bitter soul
Job21:26 But they both lie in the dust
Job21:27 I know your thoughts very well
Job21:28 For you say
Job21:29 Have you never consulted those who travel the roads? Don’t you accept their reports?
Job21:30 Indeed
Job21:31 Who would denounce his behavior to his face? Who would repay him for what he has done?
Job21:32 He is carried to the grave
Job21:33 The dirt on his grave is sweet to him. Everyone follows behind him
Job21:34 So how can you offer me such futile comfort? Your answers are deceptive.
Job22:1 Then Eliphaz the Temanite replied:
Job22:2 Can a man be of any use to God? Can even a wise man be of use to him?
Job22:3 Does it delight the Almighty if you are righteous? Does he profit if you perfect your behavior?
Job22:4 Does he correct you and take you to court because of your piety?
Job22:5 Isn’t your wickedness abundant and aren’t your iniquities endless?
Job22:6 For you took collateral from your brothers without cause
Job22:7 You gave no water to the thirsty and withheld food from the famished
Job22:8 while the land belonged to a powerful man and an influential man lived on it.
Job22:9 You sent widows away empty-handed
Job22:10 Therefore snares surround you
Job22:11 or darkness
Job22:12 Isn’t God as high as the heavens? And look at the highest stars — how lofty they are!
Job22:13 Yet you say
Job22:14 Clouds veil him so that he cannot see
Job22:15 Will you continue on the ancient path that wicked men have walked?
Job22:16 They were snatched away before their time
Job22:17 They were the ones who said to God
Job22:18 But it was he who filled their houses with good things. The counsel of the wicked is far from me!
Job22:19 The righteous see this and rejoice; the innocent mock them
Job22:20 “Surely our opponents are destroyed
Job22:21 Come to terms with God and be at peace; in this way good will come to you.
Job22:22 Receive instruction from his mouth
Job22:23 If you return to the Almighty
Job22:24 and consign your gold to the dust
Job22:25 the Almighty will be your gold and your finest silver.
Job22:26 Then you will delight in the Almighty and lift up your face to God.
Job22:27 You will pray to him
Job22:28 When you make a decision
Job22:29 When others are humiliated and you say
Job22:30 He will even rescue the guilty one
Job23:1 Then Job answered:
Job23:2 Today also my complaint is bitter. His hand is heavy despite my groaning.
Job23:3 If only I knew how to find him
Job23:4 I would plead my case before him and fill my mouth with arguments.
Job23:5 I would learn how he would answer me; and understand what he would say to me.
Job23:6 Would he prosecute me forcefully? No
Job23:7 Then an upright man could reason with him
Job23:8 If I go east
Job23:9 When he is at work to the north
Job23:10 Yet he knows the way I have taken; when he has tested me
Job23:11 My feet have followed in his tracks; I have kept to his way and not turned aside.
Job23:12 I have not departed from the commands from his lips; I have treasured the words from his mouth more than my daily food.
Job23:13 But he is unchangeable; who can oppose him? He does what he desires.
Job23:14 He will certainly accomplish what he has decreed for me
Job23:15 Therefore I am terrified in his presence; when I consider this
Job23:16 God has made my heart faint; the Almighty has terrified me.
Job23:17 Yet I am not destroyed by the darkness
Job24:1 Why does the Almighty not reserve times for judgment? Why do those who know him never see his days?
Job24:2 The wicked displace boundary markers. They steal a flock and provide pasture for it.
Job24:3 They drive away the donkeys owned by the fatherless and take the widow’s ox as collateral.
Job24:4 They push the needy off the road; the poor of the land are forced into hiding.
Job24:5 Like wild donkeys in the wilderness
Job24:6 They gather their fodder in the field and glean the vineyards of the wicked.
Job24:7 Without clothing
Job24:8 Drenched by mountain rains
Job24:9 The fatherless infant is snatched from the breast; the nursing child of the poor is seized as collateral.
Job24:10 Without clothing
Job24:11 They crush olives in their presses; they tread the winepresses
Job24:12 From the city
Job24:13 The wicked are those who rebel against the light. They do not recognize its ways or stay on its paths.
Job24:14 The murderer rises at dawn to kill the poor and needy
Job24:15 The adulterer’s eye watches for twilight
Job24:16 In the dark they break into houses; by day they lock themselves in
Job24:17 For the morning is like darkness to them. Surely they are familiar with the terrors of darkness!
Job24:18 They float on the surface of the water. Their section of the land is cursed
Job24:19 As dry ground and heat snatch away the melted snow
Job24:20 The womb forgets them; worms feed on them; they are remembered no more. So injustice is broken like a tree.
Job24:21 They prey on the childless woman who is unable to conceive
Job24:22 Yet God drags away the mighty by his power; when he rises up
Job24:23 He gives them a sense of security
Job24:24 They are exalted for a moment
Job24:25 If this is not true
Job25:1 Then Bildad the Shuhite replied:
Job25:2 Dominion and dread belong to him
Job25:3 Can his troops be numbered? Does his light not shine on everyone?
Job25:4 How can a human be justified before God? How can one born of woman be pure?
Job25:5 If even the moon does not shine and the stars are not pure in his sight
Job25:6 how much less a human
Job26:1 Then Job answered:
Job26:2 How you have helped the powerless and delivered the arm that is weak!
Job26:3 How you have counseled the unwise and abundantly provided insight!
Job26:4 With whom did you speak these words? Whose breath came out of your mouth?
Job26:5 The departed spirits tremble beneath the waters and all that inhabit them.
Job26:6 Sheol is naked before God
Job26:7 He stretches the northern skies over empty space; he hangs the earth on nothing.
Job26:8 He wraps up the water in his clouds
Job26:9 He obscures the view of his throne
Job26:10 He laid out the horizon on the surface of the waters at the boundary between light and darkness.
Job26:11 The pillars that hold up the sky tremble
Job26:12 By his power he stirred the sea
Job26:13 By his breath the heavens gained their beauty; his hand pierced the fleeing serpent.
Job26:14 These are but the fringes of his ways; how faint is the word we hear of him! Who can understand his mighty thunder?
Job27:1 Job continued his discourse
Job27:2 As God lives
Job27:3 as long as my breath is still in me and the breath from God remains in my nostrils
Job27:4 my lips will not speak unjustly
Job27:5 I will never affirm that you are right. I will maintain my integrity until I die.
Job27:6 I will cling to my righteousness and never let it go. My conscience will not accuse me as long as I live!
Job27:7 May my enemy be like the wicked and my opponent like the unjust.
Job27:8 For what hope does the godless person have when he is cut off
Job27:9 Will God hear his cry when distress comes on him?
Job27:10 Will he delight in the Almighty? Will he call on God at all times?
Job27:11 I will teach you about God’s power. I will not conceal what the Almighty has planned.
Job27:12 All of you have seen this for yourselves
Job27:13 This is a wicked man’s lot from God
Job27:14 Even if his children increase
Job27:15 Those who survive him will be buried by the plague
Job27:16 Though he piles up silver like dust and heaps up fine clothing like clay —
Job27:17 he may heap it up
Job27:18 The house he built is like a moth’s cocoon or a shelter set up by a watchman.
Job27:19 He lies down wealthy
Job27:20 Terrors overtake him like a flood; a storm wind sweeps him away at night.
Job27:21 An east wind picks him up
Job27:22 It blasts at him without mercy
Job27:23 It claps its hands at him and scoffs at him from its place.
Job28:1 Surely there is a mine for silver and a place where gold is refined.
Job28:2 Iron is taken from the ground
Job28:3 A miner puts an end to the darkness; he probes the deepest recesses for ore in the gloomy darkness.
Job28:4 He cuts a shaft far from human habitation
Job28:5 Food may come from the earth
Job28:6 Its rocks are a source of lapis lazuli
Job28:7 No bird of prey knows that path; no falcon’s eye has seen it.
Job28:8 Proud beasts have never walked on it; no lion has ever prowled over it.
Job28:9 The miner uses a flint tool and turns up ore from the root of the mountains.
Job28:10 He cuts out channels in the rocks
Job28:11 He dams up the streams from flowing so that he may bring to light what is hidden.
Job28:12 But where can wisdom be found
Job28:13 No one can know its value
Job28:14 The ocean depths say
Job28:15 Gold cannot be exchanged for it
Job28:16 Wisdom cannot be valued in the gold of Ophir
Job28:17 Gold and glass do not compare with it
Job28:18 Coral and quartz are not worth mentioning. The price of wisdom is beyond pearls.
Job28:19 Topaz from Cush cannot compare with it
Job28:20 Where then does wisdom come from
Job28:21 It is hidden from the eyes of every living thing and concealed from the birds of the sky.
Job28:22 Abaddon and Death say
Job28:23 But God understands the way to wisdom
Job28:24 For he looks to the ends of the earth and sees everything under the heavens.
Job28:25 When God fixed the weight of the wind and distributed the water by measure
Job28:26 when he established a limit for the rain and a path for the lightning
Job28:27 he considered wisdom and evaluated it; he established it and examined it.
Job28:28 He said to mankind
Job29:1 Job continued his discourse
Job29:2 If only I could be as in months gone by
Job29:3 when his lamp shone above my head
Job29:4 I would be as I was in the days of my youth when God’s friendship rested on my tent
Job29:5 when the Almighty was still with me and my children were around me
Job29:6 when my feet were bathed in curds and the rock poured out streams of oil for me!
Job29:7 When I went out to the city gate and took my seat in the town square
Job29:8 the young men saw me and withdrew
Job29:9 City officials stopped talking and covered their mouths with their hands.
Job29:10 The noblemen’s voices were hushed
Job29:11 When they heard me
Job29:12 For I rescued the poor who cried out for help
Job29:13 The dying blessed me
Job29:14 I clothed myself in righteousness
Job29:15 I was eyes to the blind and feet to the lame.
Job29:16 I was a father to the needy
Job29:17 I shattered the fangs of the unjust and snatched the prey from his teeth.
Job29:18 So I thought
Job29:19 My roots will have access to water
Job29:20 My whole being will be refreshed within me
Job29:21 Men listened to me with expectation
Job29:22 After a word from me they did not speak again; my speech settled on them like dew.
Job29:23 They waited for me as for the rain and opened their mouths as for spring showers.
Job29:24 If I smiled at them
Job29:25 I directed their course and presided as chief. I lived as a king among his troops
Job30:1 But now they mock me
Job30:2 What use to me was the strength of their hands? Their vigor had left them.
Job30:3 Emaciated from poverty and hunger
Job30:4 They plucked mallow among the shrubs
Job30:5 They were banished from human society; people shouted at them as if they were thieves.
Job30:6 They are living on the slopes of the wadis
Job30:7 They bray among the shrubs; they huddle beneath the thistles.
Job30:8 Foolish men
Job30:9 Now I am mocked by their songs; I have become an object of scorn to them.
Job30:10 They despise me and keep their distance from me; they do not hesitate to spit in my face.
Job30:11 Because God has loosened my bowstring and oppressed me
Job30:12 The rabble rise up at my right; they trap my feet and construct their siege ramp against me.
Job30:13 They tear up my path; they contribute to my destruction
Job30:14 They advance as through a gaping breach; they keep rolling in through the ruins.
Job30:15 Terrors are turned loose against me; they chase my dignity away like the wind
Job30:16 Now my life is poured out before me
Job30:17 Night pierces my bones
Job30:18 My clothing is distorted with great force; he chokes me by the neck of my garment.
Job30:19 He throws me into the mud
Job30:20 I cry out to you for help
Job30:21 You have turned against me with cruelty; you harass me with your strong hand.
Job30:22 You lift me up on the wind and make me ride it; you scatter me in the storm.
Job30:23 Yes
Job30:24 Yet no one would stretch out his hand against a ruined person when he cries out to him for help because of his distress.
Job30:25 Have I not wept for those who have fallen on hard times? Has my soul not grieved for the needy?
Job30:26 But when I hoped for good
Job30:27 I am churning within and cannot rest; days of suffering confront me.
Job30:28 I walk about blackened
Job30:29 I have become a brother to jackals and a companion of ostriches.
Job30:30 My skin blackens and flakes off
Job30:31 My lyre is used for mourning and my flute for the sound of weeping.
Job31:1 I have made a covenant with my eyes. How then could I look at a young woman?
Job31:2 For what portion would I have from God above
Job31:3 Doesn’t disaster come to the unjust and misfortune to evildoers?
Job31:4 Does he not see my ways and number all my steps?
Job31:5 If I have walked in falsehood or my foot has rushed to deceit
Job31:6 let God weigh me on accurate scales
Job31:7 If my step has turned from the way
Job31:8 let someone else eat what I have sown
Job31:9 If my heart has gone astray over a woman or I have lurked at my neighbor’s door
Job31:10 let my own wife grind grain for another man
Job31:11 For that would be a disgrace; it would be an iniquity deserving punishment.
Job31:12 For it is a fire that consumes down to Abaddon; it would destroy my entire harvest.
Job31:13 If I have dismissed the case of my male or female servants when they made a complaint against me
Job31:14 what could I do when God stands up to judge? How should I answer him when he calls me to account?
Job31:15 Did not the one who made me in the womb also make them? Did not the same God form us both in the womb?
Job31:16 If I have refused the wishes of the poor or let the widow’s eyes go blind
Job31:17 if I have eaten my few crumbs alone without letting the fatherless eat any of it —
Job31:18 for from my youth
Job31:19 if I have seen anyone dying for lack of clothing or a needy person without a cloak
Job31:20 if he did not bless me while warming himself with the fleece from my sheep
Job31:21 if I ever cast my vote against a fatherless child when I saw that I had support in the city gate
Job31:22 then let my shoulder blade fall from my back
Job31:23 For disaster from God terrifies me
Job31:24 If I placed my confidence in gold or called fine gold my trust
Job31:25 if I have rejoiced because my wealth is great or because my own hand has acquired so much
Job31:26 if I have gazed at the sun when it was shining or at the moon moving in splendor
Job31:27 so that my heart was secretly enticed and I threw them a kiss
Job31:28 this would also be an iniquity deserving punishment
Job31:29 Have I rejoiced over my enemy’s distress
Job31:30 I have not allowed my mouth to sin by asking for his life with a curse.
Job31:31 Haven’t the members of my household said
Job31:32 No stranger had to spend the night on the street
Job31:33 Have I covered my transgressions as others do by hiding my iniquity in my heart
Job31:34 because I greatly feared the crowds and because the contempt of the clans terrified me
Job31:35 If only I had someone to hear my case! Here is my signature; let the Almighty answer me. Let my Opponent compose his indictment.
Job31:36 I would surely carry it on my shoulder and wear it like a crown.
Job31:37 I would give him an account of all my steps; I would approach him like a prince.
Job31:38 If my land cries out against me and its furrows join in weeping
Job31:39 if I have consumed its produce without payment or shown contempt for its tenants
Job31:40 then let thorns grow instead of wheat and stinkweed instead of barley. The words of Job are concluded.
Job32:1 So these three men quit answering Job
Job32:2 Then Elihu son of Barachel the Buzite from the family of Ram became angry. He was angry at Job because he had justified himself rather than God.
Job32:3 He was also angry at Job’s three friends because they had failed to refute him and yet had condemned him.
Job32:4 Now Elihu had waited to speak to Job because they were all older than he.
Job32:5 But when he saw that the three men could not answer Job
Job32:6 So Elihu son of Barachel the Buzite replied: I am young in years
Job32:7 I thought that age should speak and maturity should teach wisdom.
Job32:8 But it is the spirit in a person— the breath from the Almighty— that gives anyone understanding.
Job32:9 It is not only the old who are wise or the elderly who understand how to judge.
Job32:10 Therefore I say
Job32:11 Look
Job32:12 I paid close attention to you. Yet no one proved Job wrong; not one of you refuted his arguments.
Job32:13 So do not claim
Job32:14 But Job has not directed his argument to me
Job32:15 Job’s friends are dismayed and can no longer answer; words have left them.
Job32:16 Should I continue to wait now that they are silent
Job32:17 I too will answer; yes
Job32:18 For I am full of words
Job32:19 My heart is like unvented wine; it is about to burst like new wineskins.
Job32:20 I must speak so that I can find relief; I must open my lips and respond.
Job32:21 I will be partial to no one
Job32:22 For I do not know how to give such titles; otherwise
Job33:1 But now
Job33:2 I am going to open my mouth; my tongue will form words on my palate.
Job33:3 My words come from my upright heart
Job33:4 The Spirit of God has made me
Job33:5 Refute me if you can. Prepare your case against me; take your stand.
Job33:6 I am just like you before God; I was also pinched off from a piece of clay.
Job33:7 Fear of me should not terrify you; no pressure from me should weigh you down.
Job33:8 Surely you have spoken in my hearing
Job33:9 “I am pure
Job33:10 But he finds reasons to oppose me; he regards me as his enemy.
Job33:11 He puts my feet in the stocks; he stands watch over all my paths.”
Job33:12 But I tell you that you are wrong in this matter
Job33:13 Why do you take him to court for not answering anything a person asks?
Job33:14 For God speaks time and again
Job33:15 In a dream
Job33:16 he uncovers their ears and terrifies them with warnings
Job33:17 in order to turn a person from his actions and suppress the pride of a person.
Job33:18 God spares his soul from the Pit
Job33:19 A person may be disciplined on his bed with pain and constant distress in his bones
Job33:20 so that he detests bread
Job33:21 His flesh wastes away to nothing
Job33:22 He draws near to the Pit
Job33:23 If there is an angel on his side
Job33:24 and to be gracious to him and say
Job33:25 then his flesh will be healthier than in his youth
Job33:26 He will pray to God
Job33:27 He will look at men and say
Job33:28 He redeemed my soul from going down to the Pit
Job33:29 God certainly does all these things two or three times to a person
Job33:30 in order to turn him back from the Pit
Job33:31 Pay attention
Job33:32 But if you have something to say
Job33:33 If not
Job34:1 Then Elihu continued
Job34:2 Hear my words
Job34:3 Doesn’t the ear test words as the palate tastes food?
Job34:4 Let us judge for ourselves what is right; let us decide together what is good.
Job34:5 For Job has declared
Job34:6 Would I lie about my case? My wound is incurable
Job34:7 What man is like Job? He drinks derision like water.
Job34:8 He keeps company with evildoers and walks with wicked men.
Job34:9 For he has said
Job34:10 Therefore listen to me
Job34:11 For he repays a person according to his deeds
Job34:12 Indeed
Job34:13 Who gave him authority over the earth? Who put him in charge of the entire world?
Job34:14 If he put his mind to it and withdrew the spirit and breath he gave
Job34:15 every living thing would perish together and mankind would return to the dust.
Job34:16 If you have understanding
Job34:17 Could one who hates justice govern the world? Will you condemn the mighty Righteous One
Job34:18 who says to a king
Job34:19 God is not partial to princes and does not favor the rich over the poor
Job34:20 They die suddenly in the middle of the night; people shudder
Job34:21 For his eyes watch over a man’s ways
Job34:22 There is no darkness
Job34:23 God does not need to examine a person further
Job34:24 He shatters the mighty without an investigation and sets others in their place.
Job34:25 Therefore
Job34:26 In full view of the public
Job34:27 because they turned aside from following him and did not understand any of his ways
Job34:28 but caused the poor to cry out to him
Job34:29 But when God is silent
Job34:30 so that godless men should not rule or ensnare the people.
Job34:31 Suppose someone says to God
Job34:32 Teach me what I cannot see; if I have done wrong
Job34:33 Should God repay you on your terms when you have rejected his? You must choose
Job34:34 Reasonable men will say to me
Job34:35 “Job speaks without knowledge; his words are without insight.”
Job34:36 If only Job were tested to the limit
Job34:37 For he adds rebellion to his sin; he scornfully claps in our presence
Job35:1 Then Elihu continued
Job35:2 Do you think it is just when you say
Job35:3 For you ask
Job35:4 I will answer you and your friends with you.
Job35:5 Look at the heavens and see; gaze at the clouds high above you.
Job35:6 If you sin
Job35:7 If you are righteous
Job35:8 Your wickedness affects a person like yourself
Job35:9 People cry out because of severe oppression; they shout for help because of the power of the mighty.
Job35:10 But no one asks
Job35:11 who gives us more understanding than the animals of the earth and makes us wiser than the birds of the sky?”
Job35:12 There they cry out
Job35:13 Indeed
Job35:14 how much less when you complain that you do not see him
Job35:15 But now
Job35:16 Job opens his mouth in vain and multiplies words without knowledge.
Job36:1 Then Elihu continued
Job36:2 Be patient with me a little longer
Job36:3 I will get my knowledge from a distant place and ascribe justice to my Maker.
Job36:4 Indeed
Job36:5 Yes
Job36:6 He does not keep the wicked alive
Job36:7 He does not withdraw his gaze from the righteous
Job36:8 If people are bound with chains and trapped by the cords of affliction
Job36:9 God tells them what they have done and how arrogantly they have transgressed.
Job36:10 He opens their ears to correction and tells them to repent from iniquity.
Job36:11 If they listen and serve him
Job36:12 But if they do not listen
Job36:13 Those who have a godless heart harbor anger; even when God binds them
Job36:14 They die in their youth; their life ends among male cult prostitutes.
Job36:15 God rescues the afflicted by their affliction; he instructs them by their torment.
Job36:16 Indeed
Job36:17 Yet now you are obsessed with the judgment due the wicked; judgment and justice have seized you.
Job36:18 Be careful that no one lures you with riches; do not let a large ransom lead you astray.
Job36:19 Can your wealth or all your physical exertion keep you from distress?
Job36:20 Do not long for the night when nations will disappear from their places.
Job36:21 Be careful that you do not turn to iniquity
Job36:22 Look
Job36:23 Who has appointed his way for him
Job36:24 Remember that you should praise his work
Job36:25 All mankind has seen it; people have looked at it from a distance.
Job36:26 Yes
Job36:27 For he makes waterdrops evaporate; they distill the rain into its mist
Job36:28 which the clouds pour out and shower abundantly on mankind.
Job36:29 Can anyone understand how the clouds spread out or how the thunder roars from God’s pavilion?
Job36:30 See how he spreads his lightning around him and covers the depths of the sea.
Job36:31 For he judges the nations with these; he gives food in abundance.
Job36:32 He covers his hands with lightning and commands it to hit its mark.
Job36:33 The thunder declares his presence; the cattle also
Job37:1 My heart pounds at this and leaps from my chest.
Job37:2 Just listen to his thunderous voice and the rumbling that comes from his mouth.
Job37:3 He lets it loose beneath the entire sky; his lightning to the ends of the earth.
Job37:4 Then there comes a roaring sound; God thunders with his majestic voice. He does not restrain the lightning when his rumbling voice is heard.
Job37:5 God thunders wondrously with his voice; he does great things that we cannot comprehend.
Job37:6 For he says to the snow
Job37:7 serve as his sign to all mankind
Job37:8 The wild animals enter their lairs and stay in their dens.
Job37:9 The windstorm comes from its chamber
Job37:10 Ice is formed by the breath of God
Job37:11 He saturates clouds with moisture; he scatters his lightning through them.
Job37:12 They swirl about
Job37:13 He causes this to happen for punishment
Job37:14 Listen to this
Job37:15 Do you know how God directs his clouds or makes their lightning flash?
Job37:16 Do you understand how the clouds float
Job37:17 You whose clothes get hot when the south wind brings calm to the land
Job37:18 can you help God spread out the skies as hard as a cast metal mirror?
Job37:19 Teach us what we should say to him; we cannot prepare our case because of our darkness.
Job37:20 Should he be told that I want to speak? Can a man speak when he is confused?
Job37:21 Now no one can even look at the sun when it is in the skies
Job37:22 Yet out of the north he comes
Job37:23 The Almighty — we cannot reach him — he is exalted in power! He will not violate justice and abundant righteousness
Job37:24 therefore
Job38:1 Then the LORD answered Job from the whirlwind. He said:
Job38:2 Who is this who obscures my counsel with ignorant words?
Job38:3 Get ready to answer me like a man; when I question you
Job38:4 Where were you when I established the earth? Tell me
Job38:5 Who fixed its dimensions? Certainly you know! Who stretched a measuring line across it?
Job38:6 What supports its foundations? Or who laid its cornerstone
Job38:7 while the morning stars sang together and all the sons of God shouted for joy?
Job38:8 Who enclosed the sea behind doors when it burst from the womb
Job38:9 when I made the clouds its garment and total darkness its blanket
Job38:10 when I determined its boundaries and put its bars and doors in place
Job38:11 when I declared: “You may come this far
Job38:12 Have you ever in your life commanded the morning or assigned the dawn its place
Job38:13 so it may seize the edges of the earth and shake the wicked out of it?
Job38:14 The earth is changed as clay is by a seal; its hills stand out like the folds of a garment.
Job38:15 Light is withheld from the wicked
Job38:16 Have you traveled to the sources of the sea or walked in the depths of the oceans?
Job38:17 Have the gates of death been revealed to you? Have you seen the gates of deep darkness?
Job38:18 Have you comprehended the extent of the earth? Tell me
Job38:19 Where is the road to the home of light? Do you know where darkness lives
Job38:20 so you can lead it back to its border? Are you familiar with the paths to its home?
Job38:21 Don’t you know? You were already born; you have lived so long!
Job38:22 Have you entered the place where the snow is stored? Or have you seen the storehouses of hail
Job38:23 which I hold in reserve for times of trouble
Job38:24 What road leads to the place where light is dispersed? Where is the source of the east wind that spreads across the earth?
Job38:25 Who cuts a channel for the flooding rain or clears the way for lightning
Job38:26 to bring rain on an uninhabited land
Job38:27 to satisfy the parched wasteland and cause the grass to sprout?
Job38:28 Does the rain have a father? Who fathered the drops of dew?
Job38:29 Whose womb did the ice come from? Who gave birth to the frost of heaven
Job38:30 when water becomes as hard as stone
Job38:31 Can you fasten the chains of the Pleiades or loosen the belt of Orion?
Job38:32 Can you bring out the constellations in their season and lead the Bear and her cubs?
Job38:33 Do you know the laws of heaven? Can you impose its authority on earth?
Job38:34 Can you command the clouds so that a flood of water covers you?
Job38:35 Can you send out lightning bolts
Job38:36 Who put wisdom in the heart or gave the mind understanding?
Job38:37 Who has the wisdom to number the clouds? Or who can tilt the water jars of heaven
Job38:38 when the dust hardens like cast metal and the clods of dirt stick together?
Job38:39 Can you hunt prey for a lioness or satisfy the appetite of young lions
Job38:40 when they crouch in their dens and lie in wait within their lairs?
Job38:41 Who provides the raven’s food when its young cry out to God and wander about for lack of food?
Job39:1 Do you know when mountain goats give birth? Have you watched the deer in labor?
Job39:2 Can you count the months they are pregnant so you can know the time they give birth?
Job39:3 They crouch down to give birth to their young; they deliver their newborn.
Job39:4 Their offspring are healthy and grow up in the open field. They leave and do not return.
Job39:5 Who set the wild donkey free? Who released the swift donkey from its harness?
Job39:6 I made the desert its home
Job39:7 It scoffs at the noise of the village and never hears the shouts of a driver.
Job39:8 It roams the mountains for its pastureland
Job39:9 Would the wild ox be willing to serve you? Would it spend the night by your feeding trough?
Job39:10 Can you hold the wild ox to a furrow by its harness? Will it plow the valleys behind you?
Job39:11 Can you depend on it because its strength is great? Would you leave it to do your hard work?
Job39:12 Can you trust the wild ox to harvest your grain and bring it to your threshing floor?
Job39:13 The wings of the ostrich flap joyfully
Job39:14 She abandons her eggs on the ground and lets them be warmed in the sand.
Job39:15 She forgets that a foot may crush them or that some wild animal may trample them.
Job39:16 She treats her young harshly
Job39:17 For God has deprived her of wisdom; he has not endowed her with understanding.
Job39:18 When she proudly spreads her wings
Job39:19 Do you give strength to the horse? Do you adorn his neck with a mane?
Job39:20 Do you make him leap like a locust? His proud snorting fills one with terror.
Job39:21 He paws in the valley and rejoices in his strength; he charges into battle.
Job39:22 He laughs at fear
Job39:23 A quiver rattles at his side
Job39:24 He charges ahead with trembling rage; he cannot stand still at the trumpet’s sound.
Job39:25 When the trumpet blasts
Job39:26 Does the hawk take flight by your understanding and spread its wings to the south?
Job39:27 Does the eagle soar at your command and make its nest on high?
Job39:28 It lives on a cliff where it spends the night; its stronghold is on a rocky crag.
Job39:29 From there it searches for prey; its eyes penetrate the distance.
Job39:30 Its brood gulps down blood
Job40:1 The LORD answered Job:
Job40:2 Will the one who contends with the Almighty correct him? Let him who argues with God give an answer.
Job40:3 Then Job answered the LORD:
Job40:4 I am so insignificant. How can I answer you? I place my hand over my mouth.
Job40:5 I have spoken once
Job40:6 Then the LORD answered Job from the whirlwind:
Job40:7 Get ready to answer me like a man; When I question you
Job40:8 Would you really challenge my justice? Would you declare me guilty to justify yourself?
Job40:9 Do you have an arm like God’s? Can you thunder with a voice like his?
Job40:10 Adorn yourself with majesty and splendor
Job40:11 Pour out your raging anger; look on every proud person and humiliate him.
Job40:12 Look on every proud person and humble him; trample the wicked where they stand.
Job40:13 Hide them together in the dust; imprison them in the grave.
Job40:14 Then I will confess to you that your own right hand can deliver you.
Job40:15 Look at Behemoth
Job40:16 Look at the strength of his back and the power in the muscles of his belly.
Job40:17 He stiffens his tail like a cedar tree; the tendons of his thighs are woven firmly together.
Job40:18 His bones are bronze tubes; his limbs are like iron rods.
Job40:19 He is the foremost of God’s works; only his Maker can draw the sword against him.
Job40:20 The hills yield food for him
Job40:21 He lies under the lotus plants
Job40:22 Lotus plants cover him with their shade; the willows by the brook surround him.
Job40:23 Though the river rages
Job40:24 Can anyone capture him while he looks on
Job41:1 Can you pull in Leviathan with a hook or tie his tongue down with a rope?
Job41:2 Can you put a cord through his nose or pierce his jaw with a hook?
Job41:3 Will he beg you for mercy or speak softly to you?
Job41:4 Will he make a covenant with you so that you can take him as a slave forever?
Job41:5 Can you play with him like a bird or put him on a leash for your girls?
Job41:6 Will traders bargain for him or divide him among the merchants?
Job41:7 Can you fill his hide with harpoons or his head with fishing spears?
Job41:8 Lay a hand on him. You will remember the battle and never repeat it!
Job41:9 Any hope of capturing him proves false. Does a person not collapse at the very sight of him?
Job41:10 No one is ferocious enough to rouse Leviathan; who then can stand against me?
Job41:11 Who confronted me
Job41:12 I cannot be silent about his limbs
Job41:13 Who can strip off his outer covering? Who can penetrate his double layer of armor?
Job41:14 Who can open his jaws
Job41:15 His pride is in his rows of scales
Job41:16 One scale is so close to another that no air can pass between them.
Job41:17 They are joined to one another
Job41:18 His snorting flashes with light
Job41:19 Flaming torches shoot from his mouth; fiery sparks fly out!
Job41:20 Smoke billows from his nostrils as from a boiling pot or burning reeds.
Job41:21 His breath sets coals ablaze
Job41:22 Strength resides in his neck
Job41:23 The folds of his flesh are joined together
Job41:24 His heart is as hard as a rock
Job41:25 When Leviathan rises
Job41:26 The sword that reaches him will have no effect
Job41:27 He regards iron as straw
Job41:28 No arrow can make him flee; slingstones become like stubble to him.
Job41:29 A club is regarded as stubble
Job41:30 His undersides are jagged potsherds
Job41:31 He makes the depths seethe like a cauldron; he makes the sea like an ointment jar.
Job41:32 He leaves a shining wake behind him; one would think the deep had gray hair!
Job41:33 He has no equal on earth — a creature devoid of fear!
Job41:34 He surveys everything that is haughty; he is king over all the proud beasts.
Job42:1 Then Job replied to the LORD:
Job42:2 I know that you can do anything and no plan of yours can be thwarted.
Job42:3 You asked
Job42:4 You said
Job42:5 I had heard reports about you
Job42:6 Therefore
Job42:7 After the LORD had finished speaking to Job
Job42:8 Now take seven bulls and seven rams
Job42:9 Then Eliphaz the Temanite
Job42:10 After Job had prayed for his friends
Job42:11 All his brothers
Job42:12 So the LORD blessed the last part of Job’s life more than the first. He owned fourteen thousand sheep and goats
Job42:13 He also had seven sons and three daughters.
Job42:14 He named his first daughter Jemimah
Job42:15 No women as beautiful as Job’s daughters could be found in all the land
Job42:16 Job lived 140 years after this and saw his children and their children to the fourth generation.
Job42:17 Then Job died
Psalms1:1 How happy is the one who does not walk in the advice of the wicked or stand in the pathway with sinners or sit in the company of mockers!
Psalms1:2 Instead
Psalms1:3 He is like a tree planted beside flowing streams that bears its fruit in its season and whose leaf does not wither. Whatever he does prospers.
Psalms1:4 The wicked are not like this; instead
Psalms1:5 Therefore the wicked will not stand up in the judgment
Psalms1:6 For the LORD watches over the way of the righteous
Psalms2:1 Why do the nations rage and the peoples plot in vain?
Psalms2:2 The kings of the earth take their stand
Psalms2:3 “Let’s tear off their chains and throw their ropes off of us.”
Psalms2:4 The one enthroned in heaven laughs; the Lord ridicules them.
Psalms2:5 Then he speaks to them in his anger and terrifies them in his wrath:
Psalms2:6 “I have installed my king on Zion
Psalms2:7 I will declare the LORD’s decree. He said to me
Psalms2:8 Ask of me
Psalms2:9 You will break them with an iron scepter; you will shatter them like pottery.”
Psalms2:10 So now
Psalms2:11 Serve the LORD with reverential awe and rejoice with trembling.
Psalms2:12 Pay homage to the Son or he will be angry and you will perish in your rebellion

Psalms6:2 Be gracious to me

Psalms6:7 My eyes are swollen from grief; they grow old because of all my enemies.
Psalms6:8 Depart from me
Psalms6:9 The LORD has heard my plea for help; the LORD accepts my prayer.
Psalms6:10 All my enemies will be ashamed and shake with terror; they will turn back and suddenly be disgraced.

Psalms7:7 Let the assembly of peoples gather around you; take your seat on high over it.

Psalms7:10 My shield is with God
Psalms7:11 God is a righteous judge and a God who shows his wrath every day.
Psalms7:12 If anyone does not repent

Psalms7:16 His trouble comes back on his own head; his own violence comes down on top of his head.

Psalms9:1 For the choir director: according to. A psalm of David. [1] I will thank the LORD with all my heart; I will declare all your wondrous works.
Psalms9:2 I will rejoice and boast about you; I will sing about your name
Psalms9:3 When my enemies retreat
Psalms9:4 For you have upheld my just cause; you are seated on your throne as a righteous judge.
Psalms9:5 You have rebuked the nations: You have destroyed the wicked; you have erased their name forever and ever.
Psalms9:6 The enemy has come to eternal ruin. You have uprooted the cities
Psalms9:7 But the LORD sits enthroned forever; he has established his throne for judgment.
Psalms9:8 And he judges the world with righteousness; he executes judgment on the nations with fairness.
Psalms9:9 The LORD is a refuge for the persecuted
Psalms9:10 Those who know your name trust in you because you have not abandoned those who seek you
Psalms9:11 Sing to the LORD
Psalms9:12 For the one who seeks an accounting for bloodshed remembers them; he does not forget the cry of the oppressed.
Psalms9:13 Be gracious to me
Psalms9:14 so that I may declare all your praises. I will rejoice in your salvation within the gates of Daughter Zion.
Psalms9:15 The nations have fallen into the pit they made; their foot is caught in the net they have concealed.
Psalms9:16 The LORD has made himself known; he has executed justice
Psalms9:17 The wicked will return to Sheol  — all the nations that forget God.
Psalms9:18 For the needy will not always be forgotten; the hope of the oppressed will not perish forever.

Psalms10:1 LORD
Psalms10:2 In arrogance the wicked relentlessly pursue their victims; let them be caught in the schemes they have devised.
Psalms10:3 For the wicked one boasts about his own cravings; the one who is greedy curses and despises the LORD.
Psalms10:4 In all his scheming
Psalms10:5 His ways are always secure; your lofty judgments have no effect on him; he scoffs at all his adversaries.
Psalms10:6 He says to himself
Psalms10:7 Cursing
Psalms10:8 He waits in ambush near settlements; he kills the innocent in secret places. His eyes are on the lookout for the helpless;
Psalms10:9 he lurks in secret like a lion in a thicket. He lurks in order to seize a victim; he seizes a victim and drags him in his net.
Psalms10:10 So he is oppressed and beaten down; helpless people fall because of the wicked one’s strength.
Psalms10:11 He says to himself
Psalms10:12 Rise up

Psalms10:14 But you yourself have seen trouble and grief

Psalms10:16 The LORD is King forever and ever; the nations will perish from his land.
Psalms10:17 LORD
Psalms10:18 doing justice for the fatherless and the oppressed so that mere humans from the earth may terrify them no more.

Psalms18:1 For the choir director. Of the servant of the LORD
Psalms18:2 The LORD is my rock
Psalms18:3 I called to the LORD
Psalms18:4 The ropes of death were wrapped around me; the torrents of destruction terrified me.
Psalms18:5 The ropes of Sheol entangled me; the snares of death confronted me.
Psalms18:6 I called to the LORD in my distress
Psalms18:7 Then the earth shook and quaked; the foundations of the mountains trembled; they shook because he burned with anger.
Psalms18:8 Smoke rose from his nostrils
Psalms18:9 He bent the heavens and came down
Psalms18:10 He rode on a cherub and flew
Psalms18:11 He made darkness his hiding place
Psalms18:12 From the radiance of his presence
Psalms18:13 The LORD thundered from heaven; the Most High made his voice heard.
Psalms18:14 He shot his arrows and scattered them; he hurled lightning bolts and routed them.
Psalms18:15 The depths of the sea became visible
Psalms18:16 He reached down from on high and took hold of me; he pulled me out of deep water.
Psalms18:17 He rescued me from my powerful enemy and from those who hated me
Psalms18:18 They confronted me in the day of my calamity
Psalms18:19 He brought me out to a spacious place; he rescued me because he delighted in me.
Psalms18:20 The LORD rewarded me according to my righteousness; he repaid me according to the cleanness of my hands.
Psalms18:21 For I have kept the ways of the LORD and have not turned from my God to wickedness.
Psalms18:22 Indeed
Psalms18:23 I was blameless toward him and kept myself from my iniquity.
Psalms18:24 So the LORD repaid me according to my righteousness
Psalms18:25 With the faithful you prove yourself faithful
Psalms18:26 with the pure you prove yourself pure; but with the crooked you prove yourself shrewd.
Psalms18:27 For you rescue an oppressed people
Psalms18:28 LORD
Psalms18:29 With you I can attack a barricade
Psalms18:30 God — his way is perfect; the word of the LORD is pure. He is a shield to all who take refuge in him.
Psalms18:31 For who is God besides the LORD? And who is a rock? Only our God.
Psalms18:32 God — he clothes me with strength and makes my way perfect.
Psalms18:33 He makes my feet like the feet of a deer and sets me securely on the heights.
Psalms18:34 He trains my hands for war; my arms can bend a bow of bronze.
Psalms18:35 You have given me the shield of your salvation; your right hand upholds me
Psalms18:36 You make a spacious place beneath me for my steps
Psalms18:37 I pursue my enemies and overtake them; I do not turn back until they are wiped out.
Psalms18:38 I crush them
Psalms18:39 You have clothed me with strength for battle; you subdue my adversaries beneath me.
Psalms18:40 You have made my enemies retreat before me; I annihilate those who hate me.
Psalms18:41 They cry for help
Psalms18:42 I pulverize them like dust before the wind; I trample them like mud in the streets.
Psalms18:43 You have freed me from the feuds among the people; you have appointed me the head of nations; a people I had not known serve me.
Psalms18:44 Foreigners submit to me cringing; as soon as they hear they obey me.
Psalms18:45 Foreigners lose heart and come trembling from their fortifications.
Psalms18:46 The LORD lives — blessed be my rock! The God of my salvation is exalted.
Psalms18:47 God — he grants me vengeance and subdues peoples under me.
Psalms18:48 He frees me from my enemies. You exalt me above my adversaries; you rescue me from violent men.
Psalms18:49 Therefore I will give thanks to you among the nations
Psalms18:50 He gives great victories to his king; he shows loyalty to his anointed
Psalms19:1 For the choir director. A psalm of David. [1] The heavens declare the glory of God
Psalms19:2 Day after day they pour out speech; night after night they communicate knowledge.
Psalms19:3 There is no speech; there are no words; their voice is not heard.
Psalms19:4 Their message has gone out to the whole earth
Psalms19:5 It is like a bridegroom coming from his home; it rejoices like an athlete running a course.
Psalms19:6 It rises from one end of the heavens and circles to their other end; nothing is hidden from its heat.
Psalms19:7 The instruction of the LORD is perfect
Psalms19:8 The precepts of the LORD are right
Psalms19:9 The fear of the LORD is pure
Psalms19:10 They are more desirable than gold — than an abundance of pure gold; and sweeter than honey dripping from a honeycomb.
Psalms19:11 In addition
Psalms19:12 Who perceives his unintentional sins? Cleanse me from my hidden faults.
Psalms19:13 Moreover
Psalms19:14 May the words of my mouth and the meditation of my heart be acceptable to you

Psalms22:2 My God
Psalms22:3 But you are holy
Psalms22:4 Our fathers trusted in you; they trusted
Psalms22:5 They cried to you and were set free; they trusted in you and were not disgraced.

Psalms22:7 Everyone who sees me mocks me; they sneer and shake their heads:
Psalms22:8 “He relies on the LORD; let him save him; let the LORD rescue him
Psalms22:9 It was you who brought me out of the womb
Psalms22:10 I was given over to you at birth; you have been my God from my mother’s womb.
Psalms22:11 Don’t be far from me
Psalms22:12 Many bulls surround me; strong ones of Bashan encircle me.
Psalms22:13 They open their mouths against me — lions
Psalms22:14 I am poured out like water
Psalms22:15 My strength is dried up like baked clay; my tongue sticks to the roof of my mouth. You put me into the dust of death.
Psalms22:16 For dogs have surrounded me; a gang of evildoers has closed in on me; they pierced my hands and my feet.
Psalms22:17 I can count all my bones; people look and stare at me.

Psalms22:19 But you

Psalms22:23 You who fear the LORD
Psalms22:24 For he has not despised or abhorred the torment of the oppressed. He did not hide his face from him but listened when he cried to him for help.
Psalms22:25 I will give praise in the great assembly because of you; I will fulfill my vows before those who fear you.
Psalms22:26 The humble will eat and be satisfied; those who seek the LORD will praise him. May your hearts live forever!
Psalms22:27 All the ends of the earth will remember and turn to the LORD. All the families of the nations will bow down before you
Psalms22:28 for kingship belongs to the LORD; he rules the nations.
Psalms22:29 All who prosper on earth will eat and bow down; all those who go down to the dust will kneel before him — even the one who cannot preserve his life.
Psalms22:30 Their descendants will serve him; the next generation will be told about the Lord.
Psalms22:31 They will come and declare his righteousness; to a people yet to be born they will declare what he has done.

Psalms37:1 Of David. [1] Do not be agitated by evildoers; do not envy those who do wrong.
Psalms37:2 For they wither quickly like grass and wilt like tender green plants.
Psalms37:3 Trust in the LORD and do what is good; dwell in the land and live securely.
Psalms37:4 Take delight in the LORD
Psalms37:5 Commit your way to the LORD; trust in him
Psalms37:6 making your righteousness shine like the dawn
Psalms37:7 Be silent before the LORD and wait expectantly for him; do not be agitated by one who prospers in his way
Psalms37:8 Refrain from anger and give up your rage; do not be agitated — it can only bring harm.
Psalms37:9 For evildoers will be destroyed
Psalms37:10 A little while
Psalms37:11 But the humble will inherit the land and will enjoy abundant prosperity.
Psalms37:12 The wicked person schemes against the righteous and gnashes his teeth at him.
Psalms37:13 The Lord laughs at him because he sees that his day is coming.
Psalms37:14 The wicked have drawn the sword and strung the bow to bring down the poor and needy and to slaughter those whose way is upright.
Psalms37:15 Their swords will enter their own hearts
Psalms37:16 The little that the righteous person has is better than the abundance of many wicked people.
Psalms37:17 For the arms of the wicked will be broken
Psalms37:18 The LORD watches over the blameless all their days
Psalms37:19 They will not be disgraced in times of adversity; they will be satisfied in days of hunger.
Psalms37:20 But the wicked will perish; the LORD’s enemies
Psalms37:21 The wicked person borrows and does not repay
Psalms37:22 Those who are blessed by the LORD will inherit the land
Psalms37:23 A person’s steps are established by the LORD
Psalms37:24 Though he falls
Psalms37:25 I have been young and now I am old
Psalms37:26 He is always generous
Psalms37:27 Turn away from evil
Psalms37:28 For the LORD loves justice and will not abandon his faithful ones. They are kept safe forever
Psalms37:29 The righteous will inherit the land and dwell in it permanently.
Psalms37:30 The mouth of the righteous utters wisdom; his tongue speaks what is just.
Psalms37:31 The instruction of his God is in his heart; his steps do not falter.
Psalms37:32 The wicked one lies in wait for the righteous and intends to kill him;
Psalms37:33 the LORD will not leave him in the power of the wicked one or allow him to be condemned when he is judged.
Psalms37:34 Wait for the LORD and keep his way
Psalms37:35 I have seen a wicked
Psalms37:36 Then I passed by and noticed he was gone; I searched for him
Psalms37:37 Watch the blameless and observe the upright
Psalms37:38 But transgressors will all be eliminated; the future of the wicked will be destroyed.
Psalms37:39 The salvation of the righteous is from the LORD
Psalms37:40 The LORD helps and delivers them; he will deliver them from the wicked and will save them because they take refuge in him.

Psalms40:9 I proclaim righteousness in the great assembly; see
Psalms40:10 I did not hide your righteousness in my heart; I spoke about your faithfulness and salvation; I did not conceal your constant love and truth from the great assembly.
Psalms40:11 LORD
Psalms40:12 For troubles without number have surrounded me; my iniquities have overtaken me; I am unable to see. They are more than the hairs of my head
Psalms40:13 LORD
Psalms40:14 Let those who intend to take my life be disgraced and confounded. Let those who wish me harm be turned back and humiliated.
Psalms40:15 Let those who say to me
Psalms40:16 Let all who seek you rejoice and be glad in you; let those who love your salvation continually say
Psalms40:17 I am oppressed and needy; may the Lord think of me. You are my helper and my deliverer; my God
Psalms41:1 For the choir director. A psalm of David. [1] Happy is one who is considerate of the poor; the LORD will save him in a day of adversity.
Psalms41:2 The LORD will keep him and preserve him; he will be blessed in the land. You will not give him over to the desire of his enemies.
Psalms41:3 The LORD will sustain him on his sickbed; you will heal him on the bed where he lies.
Psalms41:4 I said
Psalms41:5 My enemies speak maliciously about me: “When will he die and be forgotten?”
Psalms41:6 When one of them comes to visit
Psalms41:7 All who hate me whisper together about me; they plan to harm me.
Psalms41:8 “Something awful has overwhelmed him
Psalms41:9 Even my friend in whom I trusted
Psalms41:10 But you
Psalms41:11 By this I know that you delight in me: my enemy does not shout in triumph over me.
Psalms41:12 You supported me because of my integrity and set me in your presence forever.
Psalms41:13 Blessed be the LORD God of Israel
Psalms42:1 For the choir director. A of the sons of Korah. [1] As a deer longs for flowing streams
Psalms42:2 I thirst for God
Psalms42:3 My tears have been my food day and night
Psalms42:4 I remember this as I pour out my heart: how I walked with many
Psalms42:5 Why
Psalms42:6 I am deeply depressed; therefore I remember you from the land of Jordan and the peaks of Hermon
Psalms42:7 Deep calls to deep in the roar of your waterfalls; all your breakers and your billows have swept over me.
Psalms42:8 The LORD will send his faithful love by day; his song will be with me in the night — a prayer to the God of my life.
Psalms42:9 I will say to God
Psalms42:10 My adversaries taunt me

Psalms44:1 For the choir director. A of the sons of Korah. [1] God
Psalms44:2 In order to plant them
Psalms44:3 For they did not take the land by their sword — their arm did not bring them victory — but by your right hand
Psalms44:4 You are my King
Psalms44:5 Through you we drive back our foes; through your name we trample our enemies.
Psalms44:6 For I do not trust in my bow
Psalms44:7 But you give us victory over our foes and let those who hate us be disgraced.
Psalms44:8 We boast in God all day long; we will praise your name forever. Selah
Psalms44:9 But you have rejected and humiliated us; you do not march out with our armies.
Psalms44:10 You make us retreat from the foe
Psalms44:11 You hand us over to be eaten like sheep and scatter us among the nations.
Psalms44:12 You sell your people for nothing; you make no profit from selling them.
Psalms44:13 You make us an object of reproach to our neighbors
Psalms44:14 You make us a joke among the nations
Psalms44:15 My disgrace is before me all day long
Psalms44:16 because of the taunts of the scorner and reviler
Psalms44:17 All this has happened to us
Psalms44:18 Our hearts have not turned back; our steps have not strayed from your path.
Psalms44:19 But you have crushed us in a haunt of jackals and have covered us with deepest darkness.
Psalms44:20 If we had forgotten the name of our God and spread out our hands to a foreign god
Psalms44:21 wouldn’t God have found this out
Psalms44:22 Because of you we are being put to death all day long; we are counted as sheep to be slaughtered.
Psalms44:23 Wake up
Psalms44:24 Why do you hide and forget our affliction and oppression?
Psalms44:25 For we have sunk down to the dust; our bodies cling to the ground.
Psalms44:26 Rise up! Help us! Redeem us because of your faithful love.

Psalms68:28 Your God has decreed your strength. Show your strength
Psalms68:29 Because of your temple at Jerusalem
Psalms68:30 Rebuke the beast in the reeds
Psalms68:31 Ambassadors will come from Egypt; Cush will stretch out its hands to God.
Psalms68:32 Sing to God
Psalms68:33 to him who rides in the ancient
Psalms68:34 Ascribe power to God. His majesty is over Israel
Psalms68:35 God
Psalms69:1 For the choir director: according to “The Lilies.” Of David. [1] Save me
Psalms69:2 I have sunk in deep mud
Psalms69:3 I am weary from my crying; my throat is parched. My eyes fail
Psalms69:4 Those who hate me without cause are more numerous than the hairs of my head; my deceitful enemies
Psalms69:5 God
Psalms69:6 Do not let those who put their hope in you be disgraced because of me
Psalms69:7 For I have endured insults because of you
Psalms69:8 I have become a stranger to my brothers and a foreigner to my mother’s sons
Psalms69:9 because zeal for your house has consumed me
Psalms69:10 I mourned and fasted
Psalms69:11 I wore sackcloth as my clothing
Psalms69:12 Those who sit at the city gate talk about me
Psalms69:13 But as for me
Psalms69:14 Rescue me from the miry mud; don’t let me sink. Let me be rescued from those who hate me and from the deep water.
Psalms69:15 Don’t let the floodwaters sweep over me or the deep swallow me up; don’t let the Pit close its mouth over me.
Psalms69:16 Answer me
Psalms69:17 Don’t hide your face from your servant
Psalms69:18 Come near to me and redeem me; ransom me because of my enemies.
Psalms69:19 You know the insults I endure — my shame and disgrace. You are aware of all my adversaries.
Psalms69:20 Insults have broken my heart
Psalms69:21 Instead
Psalms69:22 Let their table set before them be a snare
Psalms69:23 Let their eyes grow too dim to see
Psalms69:24 Pour out your rage on them
Psalms69:25 Make their fortification desolate; may no one live in their tents.
Psalms69:26 For they persecute the one you struck and talk about the pain of those you wounded.
Psalms69:27 Charge them with crime on top of crime; do not let them share in your righteousness.
Psalms69:28 Let them be erased from the book of life and not be recorded with the righteous.
Psalms69:29 But as for me — poor and in pain — let your salvation protect me
Psalms69:30 I will praise God’s name with song and exalt him with thanksgiving.
Psalms69:31 That will please the LORD more than an ox
Psalms69:32 The humble will see it and rejoice. You who seek God
Psalms69:33 For the LORD listens to the needy and does not despise his own who are prisoners.
Psalms69:34 Let heaven and earth praise him
Psalms69:35 for God will save Zion and build up the cities of Judah. They will live there and possess it.
Psalms69:36 The descendants of his servants will inherit it
Psalms70:1 For the choir director. Of David. To bring remembrance. [1] God
Psalms70:2 Let those who seek to kill me be disgraced and confounded; let those who wish me harm be turned back and humiliated.
Psalms70:3 Let those who say
Psalms70:4 Let all who seek you rejoice and be glad in you; let those who love your salvation continually say
Psalms70:5 I am oppressed and needy; hurry to me
Psalms71:1 LORD
Psalms71:2 In your justice
Psalms71:3 Be a rock of refuge for me
Psalms71:4 Deliver me
Psalms71:5 For you are my hope
Psalms71:6 I have leaned on you from birth; you took me from my mother’s womb. My praise is always about you.
Psalms71:7 I am like a miraculous sign to many
Psalms71:8 My mouth is full of praise and honor to you all day long.
Psalms71:9 Don’t discard me in my old age. As my strength fails
Psalms71:10 For my enemies talk about me
Psalms71:11 saying
Psalms71:12 God
Psalms71:13 May my adversaries be disgraced and destroyed; may those who intend to harm me be covered with disgrace and humiliation.
Psalms71:14 But I will hope continually and will praise you more and more.
Psalms71:15 My mouth will tell about your righteousness and your salvation all day long
Psalms71:16 I come because of the mighty acts of the Lord GOD; I will proclaim your righteousness
Psalms71:17 God
Psalms71:18 Even while I am old and gray
Psalms71:19 Your righteousness reaches the heights
Psalms71:20 You caused me to experience many troubles and misfortunes
Psalms71:21 You will increase my honor and comfort me once again.
Psalms71:22 Therefore
Psalms71:23 My lips will shout for joy when I sing praise to you because you have redeemed me.
Psalms71:24 Therefore
Psalms72:1 Of Solomon. [1] God
Psalms72:2 He will judge your people with righteousness and your afflicted ones with justice.
Psalms72:3 May the mountains bring well-being to the people and the hills
Psalms72:4 May he vindicate the afflicted among the people
Psalms72:5 May they fear you while the sun endures and as long as the moon
Psalms72:6 May the king be like rain that falls on the cut grass
Psalms72:7 May the righteous flourish in his days and well-being abound until the moon is no more.
Psalms72:8 May he rule from sea to sea and from the Euphrates to the ends of the earth.
Psalms72:9 May desert tribes kneel before him and his enemies lick the dust.
Psalms72:10 May the kings of Tarshish and the coasts and islands bring tribute
Psalms72:11 Let all kings bow in homage to him
Psalms72:12 For he will rescue the poor who cry out and the afflicted who have no helper.
Psalms72:13 He will have pity on the poor and helpless and save the lives of the poor.
Psalms72:14 He will redeem them from oppression and violence
Psalms72:15 May he live long! May gold from Sheba be given to him. May prayer be offered for him continually
Psalms72:16 May there be plenty of grain in the land; may it wave on the tops of the mountains. May its crops be like Lebanon. May people flourish in the cities like the grass of the field.
Psalms72:17 May his name endure forever; as long as the sun shines
Psalms72:18 Blessed be the LORD God
Psalms72:19 Blessed be his glorious name forever; the whole earth is filled with his glory. Amen and amen.
Psalms72:20 The prayers of David son of Jesse are concluded.
Psalms73:1 A psalm of Asaph. [1] God is indeed good to Israel
Psalms73:2 But as for me
Psalms73:3 For I envied the arrogant; I saw the prosperity of the wicked.
Psalms73:4 They have an easy time until they die
Psalms73:5 They are not in trouble like others; they are not afflicted like most people.
Psalms73:6 Therefore
Psalms73:7 Their eyes bulge out from fatness; the imaginations of their hearts run wild.
Psalms73:8 They mock
Psalms73:9 They set their mouths against heaven
Psalms73:10 Therefore his people turn to them and drink in their overflowing words.
Psalms73:11 The wicked say
Psalms73:12 Look at them — the wicked! They are always at ease
Psalms73:13 Did I purify my heart and wash my hands in innocence for nothing?
Psalms73:14 For I am afflicted all day long and punished every morning.
Psalms73:15 If I had decided to say these things aloud
Psalms73:16 When I tried to understand all this
Psalms73:17 until I entered God’s sanctuary. Then I understood their destiny.
Psalms73:18 Indeed
Psalms73:19 How suddenly they become a desolation! They come to an end
Psalms73:20 Like one waking from a dream
Psalms73:21 When I became embittered and my innermost being was wounded
Psalms73:22 I was stupid and didn’t understand; I was an unthinking animal toward you.
Psalms73:23 Yet I am always with you; you hold my right hand.
Psalms73:24 You guide me with your counsel
Psalms73:25 Who do I have in heaven but you? And I desire nothing on earth but you.
Psalms73:26 My flesh and my heart may fail
Psalms73:27 Those far from you will certainly perish; you destroy all who are unfaithful to you.
Psalms73:28 But as for me
Psalms74:1 A of Asaph. [1] Why have you rejected us forever
Psalms74:2 Remember your congregation
Psalms74:3 Make your way to the perpetual ruins
Psalms74:4 Your adversaries roared in the meeting place where you met with us. They set up their emblems as signs.
Psalms74:5 It was like men in a thicket of trees
Psalms74:6 then smashing all the carvings with hatchets and picks.
Psalms74:7 They set your sanctuary on fire; they utterly desecrated the dwelling place of your name.
Psalms74:8 They said in their hearts
Psalms74:9 There are no signs for us to see. There is no longer a prophet. And none of us knows how long this will last.
Psalms74:10 God
Psalms74:11 Why do you hold back your hand? Stretch out your right hand and destroy them!
Psalms74:12 God my King is from ancient times
Psalms74:13 You divided the sea with your strength; you smashed the heads of the sea monsters in the water;
Psalms74:14 you crushed the heads of Leviathan; you fed him to the creatures of the desert.
Psalms74:15 You opened up springs and streams; you dried up ever-flowing rivers.
Psalms74:16 The day is yours
Psalms74:17 You set all the boundaries of the earth; you made summer and winter.
Psalms74:18 Remember this: the enemy has mocked the LORD
Psalms74:19 Do not give to beasts the life of your dove; do not forget the lives of your poor people forever.
Psalms74:20 Consider the covenant
Psalms74:21 Do not let the oppressed turn away in shame; let the poor and needy praise your name.
Psalms74:22 Rise up
Psalms74:23 Do not forget the clamor of your adversaries

Psalms76:1 For the choir director: with stringed instruments. A psalm of Asaph. A song. [1] God is known in Judah; his name is great in Israel.
Psalms76:2 His tent is in Salem
Psalms76:3 There he shatters the bow’s flaming arrows
Psalms76:4 You are resplendent and majestic coming down from the mountains of prey.
Psalms76:5 The brave-hearted have been plundered; they have slipped into their final sleep. None of the warriors was able to lift a hand.
Psalms76:6 At your rebuke
Psalms76:7 And you — you are to be feared. When you are angry
Psalms76:8 From heaven you pronounced judgment. The earth feared and grew quiet
Psalms76:9 when God rose up to judge and to save all the lowly of the earth. Selah
Psalms76:10 Even human wrath will praise you; you will clothe yourself with the wrath that remains.
Psalms76:11 Make and keep your vows to the LORD your God; let all who are around him bring tribute to the awe-inspiring one.
Psalms76:12 He humbles the spirit of leaders; he is feared by the kings of the earth.
Psalms77:1 For the choir director: according to Jeduthun. Of Asaph. A psalm. [1] I cry aloud to God
Psalms77:2 I sought the Lord in my day of trouble. My hands were continually lifted up all night long; I refused to be comforted.
Psalms77:3 I think of God; I groan; I meditate; my spirit becomes weak. Selah
Psalms77:4 You have kept me from closing my eyes; I am troubled and cannot speak.
Psalms77:5 I consider days of old
Psalms77:6 At night I remember my music; I meditate in my heart
Psalms77:7 “Will the Lord reject forever and never again show favor?
Psalms77:8 Has his faithful love ceased forever? Is his promise at an end for all generations?
Psalms77:9 Has God forgotten to be gracious? Has he in anger withheld his compassion?” Selah
Psalms77:10 So I say
Psalms77:11 I will remember the LORD’s works; yes
Psalms77:12 I will reflect on all you have done and meditate on your actions.
Psalms77:13 God
Psalms77:14 You are the God who works wonders; you revealed your strength among the peoples.
Psalms77:15 With power you redeemed your people
Psalms77:16 The water saw you
Psalms77:17 The clouds poured down water. The storm clouds thundered; your arrows flashed back and forth.
Psalms77:18 The sound of your thunder was in the whirlwind; lightning lit up the world. The earth shook and quaked.
Psalms77:19 Your way went through the sea and your path through the vast water
Psalms77:20 You led your people like a flock by the hand of Moses and Aaron.
Psalms78:1 A of Asaph. [1] My people
Psalms78:2 I will declare wise sayings; I will speak mysteries from the past  —
Psalms78:3 things we have heard and known and that our fathers have passed down to us.
Psalms78:4 We will not hide them from their children
Psalms78:5 He established a testimony in Jacob and set up a law in Israel
Psalms78:6 so that a future generation — children yet to be born — might know. They were to rise and tell their children
Psalms78:7 so that they might put their confidence in God and not forget God’s works
Psalms78:8 Then they would not be like their fathers
Psalms78:9 The Ephraimite archers turned back on the day of battle.
Psalms78:10 They did not keep God’s covenant and refused to live by his law.
Psalms78:11 They forgot what he had done
Psalms78:12 He worked wonders in the sight of their fathers in the land of Egypt
Psalms78:13 He split the sea and brought them across; the water stood firm like a wall.
Psalms78:14 He led them with a cloud by day and with a fiery light throughout the night.
Psalms78:15 He split rocks in the wilderness and gave them drink as abundant as the depths.
Psalms78:16 He brought streams out of the stone and made water flow down like rivers.
Psalms78:17 But they continued to sin against him
Psalms78:18 They deliberately tested God
Psalms78:19 They spoke against God
Psalms78:20 Look! He struck the rock and water gushed out; torrents overflowed. But can he also provide bread or furnish meat for his people?”
Psalms78:21 Therefore
Psalms78:22 because they did not believe God or rely on his salvation.
Psalms78:23 He gave a command to the clouds above and opened the doors of heaven.
Psalms78:24 He rained manna for them to eat; he gave them grain from heaven.
Psalms78:25 People ate the bread of angels. He sent them an abundant supply of food.
Psalms78:26 He made the east wind blow in the skies and drove the south wind by his might.
Psalms78:27 He rained meat on them like dust
Psalms78:28 He made them fall in the camp
Psalms78:29 The people ate and were completely satisfied
Psalms78:30 Before they had turned from what they craved
Psalms78:31 God’s anger flared up against them
Psalms78:32 Despite all this
Psalms78:33 He made their days end in futility
Psalms78:34 When he killed some of them
Psalms78:35 They remembered that God was their rock
Psalms78:36 But they deceived him with their mouths
Psalms78:37 their hearts were insincere toward him
Psalms78:38 Yet he was compassionate; he atoned for their iniquity and did not destroy them. He often turned his anger aside and did not unleash all his wrath.
Psalms78:39 He remembered that they were only flesh
Psalms78:40 How often they rebelled against him in the wilderness and grieved him in the desert.
Psalms78:41 They constantly tested God and provoked the Holy One of Israel.
Psalms78:42 They did not remember his power shown on the day he redeemed them from the foe
Psalms78:43 when he performed his miraculous signs in Egypt and his wonders in the territory of Zoan.
Psalms78:44 He turned their rivers into blood
Psalms78:45 He sent among them swarms of flies
Psalms78:46 He gave their crops to the caterpillar and the fruit of their labor to the locust.
Psalms78:47 He killed their vines with hail and their sycamore fig trees with a flood.
Psalms78:48 He handed over their livestock to hail and their cattle to lightning bolts.
Psalms78:49 He sent his burning anger against them: fury
Psalms78:50 He cleared a path for his anger. He did not spare them from death but delivered their lives to the plague.
Psalms78:51 He struck all the firstborn in Egypt
Psalms78:52 He led his people out like sheep and guided them like a flock in the wilderness.
Psalms78:53 He led them safely
Psalms78:54 He brought them to his holy territory
Psalms78:55 He drove out nations before them. He apportioned their inheritance by lot and settled the tribes of Israel in their tents.
Psalms78:56 But they rebelliously tested the Most High God
Psalms78:57 They treacherously turned away like their fathers; they became warped like a faulty bow.
Psalms78:58 They enraged him with their high places and provoked his jealousy with their carved images.
Psalms78:59 God heard and became furious; he completely rejected Israel.
Psalms78:60 He abandoned the tabernacle at Shiloh
Psalms78:61 He gave up his strength to captivity and his splendor to the hand of a foe.
Psalms78:62 He surrendered his people to the sword because he was enraged with his heritage.
Psalms78:63 Fire consumed his chosen young men
Psalms78:64 His priests fell by the sword
Psalms78:65 The Lord awoke as if from sleep
Psalms78:66 He beat back his foes; he gave them lasting disgrace.
Psalms78:67 He rejected the tent of Joseph and did not choose the tribe of Ephraim.
Psalms78:68 He chose instead the tribe of Judah
Psalms78:69 He built his sanctuary like the heights
Psalms78:70 He chose David his servant and took him from the sheep pens;
Psalms78:71 he brought him from tending ewes to be shepherd over his people Jacob — over Israel
Psalms78:72 He shepherded them with a pure heart and guided them with his skillful hands.

Psalms81:6 “I relieved his shoulder from the burden; his hands were freed from carrying the basket.
Psalms81:7 You called out in distress
Psalms81:8 Listen

Psalms81:16 But he would feed Israel with the best wheat. “I would satisfy you with honey from the rock.”
