Psalms88:1 A song. A psalm of the sons of Korah. For the choir director: according to A of Heman the Ezrahite. [1] LORD
Psalms88:2 May my prayer reach your presence; listen to my cry.
Psalms88:3 For I have had enough troubles
Psalms88:4 I am counted among those going down to the Pit. I am like a man without strength
Psalms88:5 abandoned among the dead. I am like the slain lying in the grave
Psalms88:6 You have put me in the lowest part of the Pit
Psalms88:7 Your wrath weighs heavily on me; you have overwhelmed me with all your waves. Selah
Psalms88:8 You have distanced my friends from me; you have made me repulsive to them. I am shut in and cannot go out.
Psalms88:9 My eyes are worn out from crying. LORD
Psalms88:10 Do you work wonders for the dead? Do departed spirits rise up to praise you? Selah
Psalms88:11 Will your faithful love be declared in the grave
Psalms88:12 Will your wonders be known in the darkness or your righteousness in the land of oblivion?
Psalms88:13 But I call to you for help
Psalms88:14 LORD
Psalms88:15 From my youth
Psalms88:16 Your wrath sweeps over me; your terrors destroy me.
Psalms88:17 They surround me like water all day long; they close in on me from every side.
Psalms88:18 You have distanced loved one and neighbor from me; darkness is my only friend.

Psalms107:2 Let the redeemed of the LORD proclaim that he has redeemed them from the power of the foe
Psalms107:3 and has gathered them from the lands — from the east and the west
Psalms107:4 Some wandered in the desolate wilderness
Psalms107:5 They were hungry and thirsty; their spirits failed within them.
Psalms107:6 Then they cried out to the LORD in their trouble; he rescued them from their distress.
Psalms107:7 He led them by the right path to go to a city where they could live.
Psalms107:8 Let them give thanks to the LORD for his faithful love and his wondrous works for all humanity.
Psalms107:9 For he has satisfied the thirsty and filled the hungry with good things.
Psalms107:10 Others sat in darkness and gloom  — prisoners in cruel chains  —
Psalms107:11 because they rebelled against God’s commands and despised the counsel of the Most High.
Psalms107:12 He broke their spirits with hard labor; they stumbled
Psalms107:13 Then they cried out to the LORD in their trouble; he saved them from their distress.
Psalms107:14 He brought them out of darkness and gloom and broke their chains apart.
Psalms107:15 Let them give thanks to the LORD for his faithful love and his wondrous works for all humanity.
Psalms107:16 For he has broken down the bronze gates and cut through the iron bars.
Psalms107:17 Fools suffered affliction because of their rebellious ways and their iniquities.
Psalms107:18 They loathed all food and came near the gates of death.
Psalms107:19 Then they cried out to the LORD in their trouble; he saved them from their distress.
Psalms107:20 He sent his word and healed them; he rescued them from the Pit.
Psalms107:21 Let them give thanks to the LORD for his faithful love and his wondrous works for all humanity.
Psalms107:22 Let them offer sacrifices of thanksgiving and announce his works with shouts of joy.
Psalms107:23 Others went to sea in ships
Psalms107:24 They saw the LORD’s works
Psalms107:25 He spoke and raised a stormy wind that stirred up the waves of the sea.
Psalms107:26 Rising up to the sky
Psalms107:27 they reeled and staggered like a drunkard
Psalms107:28 Then they cried out to the LORD in their trouble
Psalms107:29 He stilled the storm to a whisper
Psalms107:30 They rejoiced when the waves grew quiet. Then he guided them to the harbor they longed for.
Psalms107:31 Let them give thanks to the LORD for his faithful love and his wondrous works for all humanity.
Psalms107:32 Let them exalt him in the assembly of the people and praise him in the council of the elders.
Psalms107:33 He turns rivers into desert
Psalms107:34 and fruitful land into salty wasteland
Psalms107:35 He turns a desert into a pool
Psalms107:36 He causes the hungry to settle there
Psalms107:37 They sow fields and plant vineyards that yield a fruitful harvest.
Psalms107:38 He blesses them
Psalms107:39 When they are diminished and are humbled by cruel oppression and sorrow
Psalms107:40 he pours contempt on nobles and makes them wander in a trackless wasteland.
Psalms107:41 But he lifts the needy out of their suffering and makes their families multiply like flocks.
Psalms107:42 The upright see it and rejoice
Psalms107:43 Let whoever is wise pay attention to these things and consider the LORD’s acts of faithful love.

Psalms109:16 For he did not think to show kindness

Proverbs1:1 The proverbs of Solomon son of David
Proverbs1:2 For learning wisdom and discipline; for understanding insightful sayings;
Proverbs1:3 for receiving prudent instruction in righteousness
Proverbs1:4 for teaching shrewdness to the inexperienced
Proverbs1:5 let a wise person listen and increase learning
Proverbs1:6 for understanding a proverb or a parable
Proverbs1:7 The fear of the LORD is the beginning of knowledge; fools despise wisdom and discipline.
Proverbs1:8 Listen
Proverbs1:9 for they will be a garland of favor on your head and pendants around your neck.
Proverbs1:10 My son
Proverbs1:11 If they say — “Come with us! Let’s set an ambush and kill someone. Let’s attack some innocent person just for fun!
Proverbs1:12 Let’s swallow them alive
Proverbs1:13 We’ll find all kinds of valuable property and fill our houses with plunder.
Proverbs1:14 Throw in your lot with us
Proverbs1:15 my son
Proverbs1:16 because their feet run toward evil and they hurry to shed blood.
Proverbs1:17 It is useless to spread a net where any bird can see it
Proverbs1:18 but they set an ambush to kill themselves; they attack their own lives.
Proverbs1:19 Such are the paths of all who make profit dishonestly; it takes the lives of those who receive it.
Proverbs1:20 Wisdom calls out in the street; she makes her voice heard in the public squares.
Proverbs1:21 She cries out above the commotion; she speaks at the entrance of the city gates:
Proverbs1:22 “How long
Proverbs1:23 If you respond to my warning
Proverbs1:24 Since I called out and you refused
Proverbs1:25 since you neglected all my counsel and did not accept my correction
Proverbs1:26 I
Proverbs1:27 when terror strikes you like a storm and your calamity comes like a whirlwind
Proverbs1:28 Then they will call me
Proverbs1:29 Because they hated knowledge
Proverbs1:30 were not interested in my counsel
Proverbs1:31 they will eat the fruit of their way and be glutted with their own schemes.
Proverbs1:32 For the apostasy of the inexperienced will kill them
Proverbs1:33 But whoever listens to me will live securely and be undisturbed by the dread of danger.”
Proverbs2:1 My son
Proverbs2:2 listening closely to wisdom and directing your heart to understanding;
Proverbs2:3 furthermore
Proverbs2:4 if you seek it like silver and search for it like hidden treasure
Proverbs2:5 then you will understand the fear of the LORD and discover the knowledge of God.
Proverbs2:6 For the LORD gives wisdom; from his mouth come knowledge and understanding.
Proverbs2:7 He stores up success for the upright; He is a shield for those who live with integrity
Proverbs2:8 so that he may guard the paths of justice and protect the way of his faithful followers.
Proverbs2:9 Then you will understand righteousness
Proverbs2:10 For wisdom will enter your heart
Proverbs2:11 Discretion will watch over you
Proverbs2:12 It will rescue you from the way of evil — from anyone who says perverse things
Proverbs2:13 from those who abandon the right paths to walk in ways of darkness
Proverbs2:14 from those who enjoy doing evil and celebrate perversion
Proverbs2:15 whose paths are crooked
Proverbs2:16 It will rescue you from a forbidden woman
Proverbs2:17 who abandons the companion of her youth and forgets the covenant of her God;
Proverbs2:18 for her house sinks down to death and her ways to the land of the departed spirits.
Proverbs2:19 None return who go to her; none reach the paths of life.
Proverbs2:20 So follow the way of the good
Proverbs2:21 For the upright will inhabit the land
Proverbs2:22 but the wicked will be cut off from the land
Proverbs3:1 My son
Proverbs3:2 for they will bring you many days
Proverbs3:3 Never let loyalty and faithfulness leave you. Tie them around your neck; write them on the tablet of your heart.
Proverbs3:4 Then you will find favor and high regard with God and people.
Proverbs3:5 Trust in the LORD with all your heart
Proverbs3:6 in all your ways know him
Proverbs3:7 Don’t be wise in your own eyes; fear the LORD and turn away from evil.
Proverbs3:8 This will be healing for your body and strengthening for your bones.
Proverbs3:9 Honor the LORD with your possessions and with the first produce of your entire harvest;
Proverbs3:10 then your barns will be completely filled
Proverbs3:11 Do not despise the LORD’s instruction
Proverbs3:12 for the LORD disciplines the one he loves
Proverbs3:13 Happy is a man who finds wisdom and who acquires understanding
Proverbs3:14 for she is more profitable than silver
Proverbs3:15 She is more precious than jewels; nothing you desire can equal her.
Proverbs3:16 Long life is in her right hand; in her left
Proverbs3:17 Her ways are pleasant
Proverbs3:18 She is a tree of life to those who embrace her
Proverbs3:19 The LORD founded the earth by wisdom and established the heavens by understanding.
Proverbs3:20 By his knowledge the watery depths broke open
Proverbs3:21 Maintain sound wisdom and discretion. My son
Proverbs3:22 They will be life for you and adornment for your neck.
Proverbs3:23 Then you will go safely on your way; your foot will not stumble.
Proverbs3:24 When you lie down
Proverbs3:25 Don’t fear sudden danger or the ruin of the wicked when it comes
Proverbs3:26 for the LORD will be your confidence and will keep your foot from a snare.
Proverbs3:27 When it is in your power
Proverbs3:28 Don’t say to your neighbor
Proverbs3:29 Don’t plan any harm against your neighbor
Proverbs3:30 Don’t accuse anyone without cause
Proverbs3:31 Don’t envy a violent man or choose any of his ways;
Proverbs3:32 for the devious are detestable to the LORD
Proverbs3:33 The LORD’s curse is on the household of the wicked
Proverbs3:34 He mocks those who mock
Proverbs3:35 The wise will inherit honor
Proverbs4:1 Listen
Proverbs4:2 for I am giving you good instruction. Don’t abandon my teaching.
Proverbs4:3 When I was a son with my father
Proverbs4:4 he taught me and said: “Your heart must hold on to my words. Keep my commands and live.
Proverbs4:5 Get wisdom
Proverbs4:6 Don’t abandon wisdom
Proverbs4:7 Wisdom is supreme — so get wisdom. And whatever else you get
Proverbs4:8 Cherish her
Proverbs4:9 She will place a garland of favor on your head; she will give you a crown of beauty.”
Proverbs4:10 Listen
Proverbs4:11 I am teaching you the way of wisdom; I am guiding you on straight paths.
Proverbs4:12 When you walk
Proverbs4:13 Hold on to instruction; don’t let go. Guard it
Proverbs4:14 Keep off the path of the wicked; don’t proceed on the way of evil ones.
Proverbs4:15 Avoid it; don’t travel on it. Turn away from it
Proverbs4:16 For they can’t sleep unless they have done what is evil; they are robbed of sleep unless they make someone stumble.
Proverbs4:17 They eat the bread of wickedness and drink the wine of violence.
Proverbs4:18 The path of the righteous is like the light of dawn
Proverbs4:19 But the way of the wicked is like the darkest gloom; they don’t know what makes them stumble.
Proverbs4:20 My son
Proverbs4:21 Don’t lose sight of them; keep them within your heart.
Proverbs4:22 For they are life to those who find them
Proverbs4:23 Guard your heart above all else
Proverbs4:24 Don’t let your mouth speak dishonestly
Proverbs4:25 Let your eyes look forward; fix your gaze straight ahead.
Proverbs4:26 Carefully consider the path for your feet
Proverbs4:27 Don’t turn to the right or to the left; keep your feet away from evil.
Proverbs5:1 My son
Proverbs5:2 so that you may maintain discretion and your lips safeguard knowledge.
Proverbs5:3 Though the lips of the forbidden woman drip honey and her words are smoother than oil
Proverbs5:4 in the end she’s as bitter as wormwood and as sharp as a double-edged sword.
Proverbs5:5 Her feet go down to death; her steps head straight for Sheol.
Proverbs5:6 She doesn’t consider the path of life; she doesn’t know that her ways are unstable.
Proverbs5:7 So now
Proverbs5:8 Keep your way far from her. Don’t go near the door of her house.
Proverbs5:9 Otherwise
Proverbs5:10 strangers will drain your resources
Proverbs5:11 At the end of your life
Proverbs5:12 and you will say
Proverbs5:13 I didn’t obey my teachers or listen closely to my instructors.
Proverbs5:14 I am on the verge of complete ruin before the entire community.”
Proverbs5:15 Drink water from your own cistern
Proverbs5:16 Should your springs flow in the streets
Proverbs5:17 They should be for you alone and not for you to share with strangers.
Proverbs5:18 Let your fountain be blessed
Proverbs5:19 A loving deer
Proverbs5:20 Why
Proverbs5:21 For a man’s ways are before the LORD’s eyes
Proverbs5:22 A wicked man’s iniquities will trap him; he will become tangled in the ropes of his own sin.
Proverbs5:23 He will die because there is no discipline
Proverbs6:1 My son
Proverbs6:2 you have been snared by the words of your mouth trapped by the words from your mouth.
Proverbs6:3 Do this
Proverbs6:4 Don’t give sleep to your eyes or slumber to your eyelids.
Proverbs6:5 Escape like a gazelle from a hunter
Proverbs6:6 Go to the ant

Ecclesiastes1:1 The words of the Teacher
Ecclesiastes1:2 “Absolute futility
Ecclesiastes1:3 What does a person gain for all his efforts that he labors at under the sun?
Ecclesiastes1:4 A generation goes and a generation comes
Ecclesiastes1:5 The sun rises and the sun sets; panting
Ecclesiastes1:6 Gusting to the south
Ecclesiastes1:7 All the streams flow to the sea
Ecclesiastes1:8 All things are wearisome
Ecclesiastes1:9 What has been is what will be
Ecclesiastes1:10 Can one say about anything
Ecclesiastes1:11 There is no remembrance of those who came before; and of those who will come after there will also be no remembrance by those who follow them.
Ecclesiastes1:12 I
Ecclesiastes1:13 I applied my mind to examine and explore through wisdom all that is done under heaven. God has given people this miserable task to keep them occupied.
Ecclesiastes1:14 I have seen all the things that are done under the sun and have found everything to be futile
Ecclesiastes1:15 What is crooked cannot be straightened; what is lacking cannot be counted.
Ecclesiastes1:16 I said to myself
Ecclesiastes1:17 I applied my mind to know wisdom and knowledge
Ecclesiastes1:18 For with much wisdom is much sorrow; as knowledge increases
Ecclesiastes2:1 I said to myself
Ecclesiastes2:2 I said about laughter
Ecclesiastes2:3 I explored with my mind the pull of wine on my body — my mind still guiding me with wisdom — and how to grasp folly
Ecclesiastes2:4 I increased my achievements. I built houses and planted vineyards for myself.
Ecclesiastes2:5 I made gardens and parks for myself and planted every kind of fruit tree in them.
Ecclesiastes2:6 I constructed reservoirs for myself from which to irrigate a grove of flourishing trees.
Ecclesiastes2:7 I acquired male and female servants and had slaves who were born in my house. I also owned livestock — large herds and flocks — more than all who were before me in Jerusalem.
Ecclesiastes2:8 I also amassed silver and gold for myself
Ecclesiastes2:9 So I became great and surpassed all who were before me in Jerusalem; my wisdom also remained with me.
Ecclesiastes2:10 All that my eyes desired
Ecclesiastes2:11 When I considered all that I had accomplished and what I had labored to achieve
Ecclesiastes2:12 Then I turned to consider wisdom
Ecclesiastes2:13 And I realized that there is an advantage to wisdom over folly
Ecclesiastes2:14 The wise person has eyes in his head
Ecclesiastes2:15 So I said to myself
Ecclesiastes2:16 For
Ecclesiastes2:17 Therefore
Ecclesiastes2:18 I hated all my work that I labored at under the sun because I must leave it to the one who comes after me.
Ecclesiastes2:19 And who knows whether he will be wise or a fool? Yet he will take over all my work that I labored at skillfully under the sun. This too is futile.
Ecclesiastes2:20 So I began to give myself over to despair concerning all my work that I had labored at under the sun.
Ecclesiastes2:21 When there is a person whose work was done with wisdom
Ecclesiastes2:22 For what does a person get with all his work and all his efforts that he labors at under the sun?
Ecclesiastes2:23 For all his days are filled with grief
Ecclesiastes2:24 There is nothing better for a person than to eat
Ecclesiastes2:25 because who can eat and who can enjoy life apart from him?
Ecclesiastes2:26 For to the person who is pleasing in his sight
Ecclesiastes3:1 There is an occasion for everything
Ecclesiastes3:2 a time to give birth and a time to die; a time to plant and a time to uproot;
Ecclesiastes3:3 a time to kill and a time to heal; a time to tear down and a time to build;
Ecclesiastes3:4 a time to weep and a time to laugh; a time to mourn and a time to dance;
Ecclesiastes3:5 a time to throw stones and a time to gather stones; a time to embrace and a time to avoid embracing;
Ecclesiastes3:6 a time to search and a time to count as lost; a time to keep and a time to throw away;
Ecclesiastes3:7 a time to tear and a time to sew; a time to be silent and a time to speak;
Ecclesiastes3:8 a time to love and a time to hate; a time for war and a time for peace.
Ecclesiastes3:9 What does the worker gain from his struggles?
Ecclesiastes3:10 I have seen the task that God has given the children of Adam to keep them occupied.
Ecclesiastes3:11 He has made everything appropriate in its time. He has also put eternity in their hearts
Ecclesiastes3:12 I know that there is nothing better for them than to rejoice and enjoy the good life.
Ecclesiastes3:13 It is also the gift of God whenever anyone eats
Ecclesiastes3:14 I know that everything God does will last forever; there is no adding to it or taking from it. God works so that people will be in awe of him.
Ecclesiastes3:15 Whatever is
Ecclesiastes3:16 I also observed under the sun: there is wickedness at the place of judgment and there is wickedness at the place of righteousness.
Ecclesiastes3:17 I said to myself
Ecclesiastes3:18 I said to myself
Ecclesiastes3:19 For the fate of the children of Adam and the fate of animals is the same. As one dies
Ecclesiastes3:20 All are going to the same place; all come from dust
Ecclesiastes3:21 Who knows if the spirits of the children of Adam go upward and the spirits of animals go downward to the earth?
Ecclesiastes3:22 I have seen that there is nothing better than for a person to enjoy his activities because that is his reward. For who can enable him to see what will happen after he dies?
Ecclesiastes4:1 Again
Ecclesiastes4:2 So I commended the dead
Ecclesiastes4:3 But better than either of them is the one who has not yet existed
Ecclesiastes4:4 I saw that all labor and all skillful work is due to one person’s jealousy of another. This too is futile and a pursuit of the wind.
Ecclesiastes4:5 The fool folds his arms and consumes his own flesh.
Ecclesiastes4:6 Better one handful with rest than two handfuls with effort and a pursuit of the wind.
Ecclesiastes4:7 Again
Ecclesiastes4:8 There is a person without a companion
Ecclesiastes4:9 Two are better than one because they have a good reward for their efforts.
Ecclesiastes4:10 For if either falls
Ecclesiastes4:11 Also
Ecclesiastes4:12 And if someone overpowers one person
Ecclesiastes4:13 Better is a poor but wise youth than an old but foolish king who no longer pays attention to warnings.
Ecclesiastes4:14 For he came from prison to be king
Ecclesiastes4:15 I saw all the living
Ecclesiastes4:16 There is no limit to all the people who were before them
Ecclesiastes5:1 Guard your steps when you go to the house of God. Better to approach in obedience than to offer the sacrifice as fools do
Ecclesiastes5:2 Do not be hasty to speak
Ecclesiastes5:3 Just as dreams accompany much labor
Ecclesiastes5:4 When you make a vow to God
Ecclesiastes5:5 Better that you do not vow than that you vow and not fulfill it.
Ecclesiastes5:6 Do not let your mouth bring guilt on you
Ecclesiastes5:7 For many dreams bring futility
Ecclesiastes5:8 If you see oppression of the poor and perversion of justice and righteousness in the province
Ecclesiastes5:9 The profit from the land is taken by all; the king is served by the field.
Ecclesiastes5:10 The one who loves silver is never satisfied with silver
Ecclesiastes5:11 When good things increase
Ecclesiastes5:12 The sleep of the worker is sweet
Ecclesiastes5:13 There is a sickening tragedy I have seen under the sun: wealth kept by its owner to his harm.
Ecclesiastes5:14 That wealth was lost in a bad venture
Ecclesiastes5:15 As he came from his mother’s womb
Ecclesiastes5:16 This too is a sickening tragedy: exactly as he comes
Ecclesiastes5:17 What is more
Ecclesiastes5:18 Here is what I have seen to be good: It is appropriate to eat
Ecclesiastes5:19 Furthermore
Ecclesiastes5:20 for he does not often consider the days of his life because God keeps him occupied with the joy of his heart.
Ecclesiastes6:1 Here is a tragedy I have observed under the sun
Ecclesiastes6:2 God gives a person riches
Ecclesiastes6:3 A man may father a hundred children and live many years. No matter how long he lives
Ecclesiastes6:4 For he comes in futility and he goes in darkness
Ecclesiastes6:5 Though a stillborn child does not see the sun and is not conscious
Ecclesiastes6:6 And if a person lives a thousand years twice
Ecclesiastes6:7 All of a person’s labor is for his stomach
Ecclesiastes6:8 What advantage then does the wise person have over the fool? What advantage is there for the poor person who knows how to conduct himself before others?
Ecclesiastes6:9 Better what the eyes see than wandering desire. This too is futile and a pursuit of the wind.
Ecclesiastes6:10 Whatever exists was given its name long ago
Ecclesiastes6:11 For when there are many words
Ecclesiastes6:12 For who knows what is good for anyone in life
Ecclesiastes7:1 A good name is better than fine perfume
Ecclesiastes7:2 It is better to go to a house of mourning than to go to a house of feasting
Ecclesiastes7:3 Grief is better than laughter
Ecclesiastes7:4 The heart of the wise is in a house of mourning
Ecclesiastes7:5 It is better to listen to rebuke from a wise person than to listen to the song of fools
Ecclesiastes7:6 for like the crackling of burning thorns under the pot
Ecclesiastes7:7 Surely
Ecclesiastes7:8 The end of a matter is better than its beginning; a patient spirit is better than a proud spirit.
Ecclesiastes7:9 Don’t let your spirit rush to be angry
Ecclesiastes7:10 Don’t say
Ecclesiastes7:11 Wisdom is as good as an inheritance and an advantage to those who see the sun
Ecclesiastes7:12 because wisdom is protection as silver is protection; but the advantage of knowledge is that wisdom preserves the life of its owner.
Ecclesiastes7:13 Consider the work of God
Ecclesiastes7:14 In the day of prosperity be joyful
Ecclesiastes7:15 In my futile life I have seen everything: someone righteous perishes in spite of his righteousness
Ecclesiastes7:16 Don’t be excessively righteous
Ecclesiastes7:17 Don’t be excessively wicked
Ecclesiastes7:18 It is good that you grasp the one and do not let the other slip from your hand. For the one who fears God will end up with both of them.
Ecclesiastes7:19 Wisdom makes the wise person stronger than ten rulers of a city.
Ecclesiastes7:20 There is certainly no one righteous on the earth who does good and never sins.
Ecclesiastes7:21 Don’t pay attention to everything people say
Ecclesiastes7:22 for in your heart you know that many times you yourself have cursed others.
Ecclesiastes7:23 I have tested all this by wisdom. I resolved
Ecclesiastes7:24 What exists is beyond reach and very deep. Who can discover it?
Ecclesiastes7:25 I turned my thoughts to know
Ecclesiastes7:26 And I find more bitter than death the woman who is a trap: her heart a net and her hands chains. The one who pleases God will escape her
Ecclesiastes7:27 “Look
Ecclesiastes7:28 which my soul continually searches for but does not find: I found one person in a thousand
Ecclesiastes7:29 Only see this: I have discovered that God made people upright
Ecclesiastes8:1 Who is like the wise person
Ecclesiastes8:2 Keep the king’s command because of your oath made before God.
Ecclesiastes8:3 Do not be in a hurry; leave his presence
Ecclesiastes8:4 For the king’s word is authoritative
Ecclesiastes8:5 The one who keeps a command will not experience anything harmful
Ecclesiastes8:6 For every activity there is a right time and procedure
Ecclesiastes8:7 Yet no one knows what will happen because who can tell him what will happen?
Ecclesiastes8:8 No one has authority over the wind to restrain it
Ecclesiastes8:9 All this I have seen
Ecclesiastes8:10 In such circumstances
Ecclesiastes8:11 Because the sentence against an evil act is not carried out quickly
Ecclesiastes8:12 Although a sinner does evil a hundred times and prolongs his life
Ecclesiastes8:13 However
Ecclesiastes8:14 There is a futility that is done on the earth: there are righteous people who get what the actions of the wicked deserve
Ecclesiastes8:15 So I commended enjoyment because there is nothing better for a person under the sun than to eat
Ecclesiastes8:16 When I applied my mind to know wisdom and to observe the activity that is done on the earth (even though one’s eyes do not close in sleep day or night)
Ecclesiastes8:17 I observed all the work of God and concluded that a person is unable to discover the work that is done under the sun. Even though a person labors hard to explore it
Ecclesiastes9:1 Indeed
Ecclesiastes9:2 Everything is the same for everyone: There is one fate for the righteous and the wicked
Ecclesiastes9:3 This is an evil in all that is done under the sun: there is one fate for everyone. In addition
Ecclesiastes9:4 But there is hope for whoever is joined with all the living
Ecclesiastes9:5 For the living know that they will die
Ecclesiastes9:6 Their love
Ecclesiastes9:7 Go
Ecclesiastes9:8 Let your clothes be white all the time
Ecclesiastes9:9 Enjoy life with the wife you love all the days of your fleeting life
Ecclesiastes9:10 Whatever your hands find to do
Ecclesiastes9:11 Again I saw under the sun that the race is not to the swift
Ecclesiastes9:12 For certainly no one knows his time: like fish caught in a cruel net or like birds caught in a trap
Ecclesiastes9:13 I have observed that this also is wisdom under the sun
Ecclesiastes9:14 There was a small city with few men in it. A great king came against it
Ecclesiastes9:15 Now a poor wise man was found in the city
Ecclesiastes9:16 And I said
Ecclesiastes9:17 The calm words of the wise are heeded more than the shouts of a ruler over fools.
Ecclesiastes9:18 Wisdom is better than weapons of war
Ecclesiastes10:1 Dead flies make a perfumer’s oil ferment and stink; so a little folly outweighs wisdom and honor.
Ecclesiastes10:2 A wise person’s heart goes to the right
Ecclesiastes10:3 Even when the fool walks along the road
Ecclesiastes10:4 If the ruler’s anger rises against you
Ecclesiastes10:5 There is an evil I have seen under the sun
Ecclesiastes10:6 The fool is appointed to great heights
Ecclesiastes10:7 I have seen slaves on horses
Ecclesiastes10:8 The one who digs a pit may fall into it
Ecclesiastes10:9 The one who quarries stones may be hurt by them; the one who splits logs may be endangered by them.
Ecclesiastes10:10 If the ax is dull
Ecclesiastes10:11 If the snake bites before it is charmed
Ecclesiastes10:12 The words from the mouth of a wise person are gracious
Ecclesiastes10:13 The beginning of the words from his mouth is folly
Ecclesiastes10:14 yet the fool multiplies words. No one knows what will happen
Ecclesiastes10:15 The struggles of fools weary them
Ecclesiastes10:16 Woe to you
Ecclesiastes10:17 Blessed are you
Ecclesiastes10:18 Because of laziness the roof caves in
Ecclesiastes10:19 A feast is prepared for laughter
Ecclesiastes10:20 Do not curse the king even in your thoughts
Ecclesiastes11:1 Send your bread on the surface of the water
Ecclesiastes11:2 Give a portion to seven or even to eight
Ecclesiastes11:3 If the clouds are full
Ecclesiastes11:4 One who watches the wind will not sow
Ecclesiastes11:5 Just as you don’t know the path of the wind
Ecclesiastes11:6 In the morning sow your seed
Ecclesiastes11:7 Light is sweet
Ecclesiastes11:8 Indeed
Ecclesiastes11:9 Rejoice
Ecclesiastes11:10 Remove sorrow from your heart
Ecclesiastes12:1 So remember your Creator in the days of your youth: Before the days of adversity come
Ecclesiastes12:2 before the sun and the light are darkened
Ecclesiastes12:3 on the day when the guardians of the house tremble
Ecclesiastes12:4 the doors at the street are shut while the sound of the mill fades; when one rises at the sound of a bird
Ecclesiastes12:5 Also
Ecclesiastes12:6 before the silver cord is snapped
Ecclesiastes12:7 and the dust returns to the earth as it once was
Ecclesiastes12:8 “Absolute futility
Ecclesiastes12:9 In addition to the Teacher being a wise man
Ecclesiastes12:10 The Teacher sought to find delightful sayings and write words of truth accurately.
Ecclesiastes12:11 The sayings of the wise are like cattle prods
Ecclesiastes12:12 But beyond these
Ecclesiastes12:13 When all has been heard
Ecclesiastes12:14 For God will bring every act to judgment

Isaiah1:1 The vision concerning Judah and Jerusalem that Isaiah son of Amoz saw during the reigns of Kings Uzziah
Isaiah1:2 Listen
Isaiah1:3 The ox knows its owner
Isaiah1:4 Oh sinful nation
Isaiah1:5 Why do you want more beatings? Why do you keep on rebelling? The whole head is hurt
Isaiah1:6 From the sole of the foot even to the head
Isaiah1:7 Your land is desolate
Isaiah1:8 Daughter Zion is abandoned like a shelter in a vineyard
Isaiah1:9 If the LORD of Armies had not left us a few survivors
Isaiah1:10 Hear the word of the LORD
Isaiah1:11 “What are all your sacrifices to me?” asks the LORD. “I have had enough of burnt offerings and rams and the fat of well-fed cattle; I have no desire for the blood of bulls
Isaiah1:12 When you come to appear before me
Isaiah1:13 Stop bringing useless offerings. Your incense is detestable to me. New Moons and Sabbaths
Isaiah1:14 I hate your New Moons and prescribed festivals. They have become a burden to me; I am tired of putting up with them.
Isaiah1:15 When you spread out your hands in prayer
Isaiah1:16 “Wash yourselves. Cleanse yourselves. Remove your evil deeds from my sight. Stop doing evil.
Isaiah1:17 Learn to do what is good. Pursue justice. Correct the oppressor. Defend the rights of the fatherless. Plead the widow’s cause.
Isaiah1:18 “Come
Isaiah1:19 If you are willing and obedient
Isaiah1:20 But if you refuse and rebel
Isaiah1:21 The faithful town — what an adulteress she has become! She was once full of justice. Righteousness once dwelt in her
Isaiah1:22 Your silver has become dross to be discarded
Isaiah1:23 Your rulers are rebels
Isaiah1:24 Therefore the Lord GOD of Armies
Isaiah1:25 I will turn my hand against you and will burn away your dross completely; I will remove all your impurities.
Isaiah1:26 I will restore your judges to what they were at first
Isaiah1:27 Zion will be redeemed by justice
Isaiah1:28 At the same time both rebels and sinners will be broken
Isaiah1:29 Indeed
Isaiah1:30 For you will become like an oak whose leaves are withered
Isaiah1:31 The strong one will become tinder
Isaiah2:1 The vision that Isaiah son of Amoz saw concerning Judah and Jerusalem:
Isaiah2:2 In the last days the mountain of the LORD’s house will be established at the top of the mountains and will be raised above the hills. All nations will stream to it
Isaiah2:3 and many peoples will come and say
Isaiah2:4 He will settle disputes among the nations and provide arbitration for many peoples. They will beat their swords into plows and their spears into pruning knives. Nation will not take up the sword against nation
Isaiah2:5 House of Jacob
Isaiah2:6 For you have abandoned your people
Isaiah2:7 Their land is full of silver and gold
Isaiah2:8 Their land is full of idols; they worship the work of their hands
Isaiah2:9 So humanity is brought low
Isaiah2:10 Go into the rocks and hide in the dust from the terror of the LORD and from his majestic splendor.
Isaiah2:11 The pride of mankind will be humbled
Isaiah2:12 For a day belonging to the LORD of Armies is coming against all that is proud and lofty
Isaiah2:13 against all the cedars of Lebanon
Isaiah2:14 against all the high mountains
Isaiah2:15 against every high tower
Isaiah2:16 against every ship of Tarshish
Isaiah2:17 The pride of mankind will be brought low
Isaiah2:18 The idols will vanish completely.
Isaiah2:19 People will go into caves in the rocks and holes in the ground
Isaiah2:20 On that day people will throw their silver and gold idols
Isaiah2:21 They will go into the caves of the rocks and the crevices in the cliffs
Isaiah2:22 Put no more trust in a mere human
Isaiah3:1 Note this: The Lord GOD of Armies is about to remove from Jerusalem and from Judah every kind of security: the entire supply of bread and water
Isaiah3:2 heroes and warriors
Isaiah3:3 commanders of fifty and dignitaries
Isaiah3:4 “I will make youths their leaders
Isaiah3:5 The people will oppress one another
Isaiah3:6 A man will even seize his brother in his father’s house
Isaiah3:7 On that day he will cry out
Isaiah3:8 For Jerusalem has stumbled and Judah has fallen because they have spoken and acted against the LORD
Isaiah3:9 The look on their faces testifies against them
Isaiah3:10 Tell the righteous that it will go well for them
Isaiah3:11 Woe to the wicked — it will go badly for them
Isaiah3:12 Youths oppress my people
Isaiah3:13 The LORD rises to argue the case and stands to judge the people.
Isaiah3:14 The LORD brings this charge against the elders and leaders of his people: “You have devastated the vineyard. The plunder from the poor is in your houses.
Isaiah3:15 Why do you crush my people and grind the faces of the poor?” This is the declaration of the Lord GOD of Armies.

Isaiah4:2 On that day the Branch of the LORD will be beautiful and glorious

Isaiah4:4 when the Lord has washed away the filth of the daughters of Zion and cleansed the bloodguilt from the heart of Jerusalem by a spirit of judgment and a spirit of burning.

Isaiah5:1 I will sing about the one I love
Isaiah5:2 He broke up the soil
Isaiah5:3 So now
Isaiah5:4 What more could I have done for my vineyard than I did? Why
Isaiah5:5 Now I will tell you what I am about to do to my vineyard: I will remove its hedge
Isaiah5:6 I will make it a wasteland. It will not be pruned or weeded; thorns and briers will grow up. I will also give orders to the clouds that rain should not fall on it.
Isaiah5:7 For the vineyard of the LORD of Armies is the house of Israel
Isaiah5:8 Woe to those who add house to house and join field to field until there is no more room and you alone are left in the land.
Isaiah5:9 I heard the LORD of Armies say: Indeed
Isaiah5:10 For a ten-acre vineyard will yield only six gallons of wine
Isaiah5:11 Woe to those who rise early in the morning in pursuit of beer
Isaiah5:12 At their feasts they have lyre
Isaiah5:13 Therefore my people will go into exile because they lack knowledge; her dignitaries are starving
Isaiah5:14 Therefore Sheol enlarges its throat and opens wide its enormous jaws
Isaiah5:15 Humanity is brought low
Isaiah5:16 But the LORD of Armies is exalted by his justice
Isaiah5:17 Lambs will graze as if in their own pastures
Isaiah5:18 Woe to those who drag iniquity with cords of deceit and pull sin along with cart ropes
Isaiah5:19 to those who say: “Let him hurry up and do his work quickly so that we can see it! Let the plan of the Holy One of Israel take place so that we can know it!”
Isaiah5:20 Woe to those who call evil good and good evil
Isaiah5:21 Woe to those who consider themselves wise and judge themselves clever.
Isaiah5:22 Woe to those who are heroes at drinking wine
Isaiah5:23 who acquit the guilty for a bribe and deprive the innocent of justice.
Isaiah5:24 Therefore
Isaiah5:25 Therefore the LORD’s anger burned against his people. He raised his hand against them and struck them; the mountains quaked
Isaiah5:26 He raises a signal flag for the distant nations and whistles for them from the ends of the earth. Look — how quickly and swiftly they come!
Isaiah5:27 None of them grows weary or stumbles; no one slumbers or sleeps. No belt is loose and no sandal strap broken.
Isaiah5:28 Their arrows are sharpened
Isaiah5:29 Their roaring is like a lion’s; they roar like young lions; they growl and seize their prey and carry it off
Isaiah5:30 On that day they will roar over it
Isaiah6:1 In the year that King Uzziah died
Isaiah6:2 Seraphim were standing above him; they each had six wings: with two they covered their faces
Isaiah6:3 And one called to another: Holy
Isaiah6:4 The foundations of the doorways shook at the sound of their voices
Isaiah6:5 Then I said: Woe is me for I am ruined because I am a man of unclean lips and live among a people of unclean lips
Isaiah6:6 Then one of the seraphim flew to me
Isaiah6:7 He touched my mouth with it and said: Now that this has touched your lips
Isaiah6:8 Then I heard the voice of the Lord asking: Who should I send? Who will go for us? I said: Here I am. Send me.
Isaiah6:9 And he replied: Go! Say to these people: Keep listening
Isaiah6:10 Make the minds of these people dull; deafen their ears and blind their eyes; otherwise they might see with their eyes and hear with their ears
Isaiah6:11 Then I said
Isaiah6:12 and the LORD drives the people far away
Isaiah6:13 Though a tenth will remain in the land
Isaiah7:1 This took place during the reign of Ahaz
Isaiah7:2 When it became known to the house of David that Aram had occupied Ephraim
Isaiah7:3 The LORD said to Isaiah
Isaiah7:4 Say to him: Calm down and be quiet. Don’t be afraid or cowardly because of these two smoldering sticks
Isaiah7:5 For Aram
Isaiah7:6 ‘Let us go up against Judah
Isaiah7:7 This is what the Lord GOD says: It will not happen; it will not occur.
Isaiah7:8 The chief city of Aram is Damascus
Isaiah7:9 the chief city of Ephraim is Samaria
Isaiah7:10 Then the LORD spoke again to Ahaz:
Isaiah7:11 “Ask for a sign from the LORD your God — it can be as deep as Sheol or as high as heaven.”
Isaiah7:12 But Ahaz replied
Isaiah7:13 Isaiah said
Isaiah7:14 Therefore
Isaiah7:15 By the time he learns to reject what is bad and choose what is good
Isaiah7:16 For before the boy knows to reject what is bad and choose what is good
Isaiah7:17 The LORD will bring on you
Isaiah7:18 On that day the LORD will whistle to flies at the farthest streams of the Nile and to bees in the land of Assyria.
Isaiah7:19 All of them will come and settle in the steep ravines
Isaiah7:20 On that day the Lord will use a razor hired from beyond the Euphrates River — the king of Assyria  — to shave the hair on your heads
Isaiah7:21 On that day a man will raise a young cow and two sheep
Isaiah7:22 and from the abundant milk they give he will eat curds
Isaiah7:23 And on that day every place where there were a thousand vines
Isaiah7:24 A man will go there with bow and arrows because the whole land will be thorns and briers.

Isaiah8:10 Devise a plan; it will fail. Make a prediction; it will not happen. For God is with us.
Isaiah8:11 For this is what the LORD said to me with great power
Isaiah8:12 Do not call everything a conspiracy these people say is a conspiracy. Do not fear what they fear; do not be terrified.

Isaiah8:14 He will be a sanctuary; but for the two houses of Israel
Isaiah8:15 Many will stumble over these; they will fall and be broken; they will be snared and captured.

Isaiah9:1 Nevertheless
Isaiah9:2 The people walking in darkness have seen a great light; a light has dawned on those living in the land of darkness.

Isaiah9:4 For you have shattered their oppressive yoke and the rod on their shoulders
Isaiah9:5 For every trampling boot of battle and the bloodied garments of war will be burned as fuel for the fire.
Isaiah9:6 For a child will be born for us
Isaiah9:7 The dominion will be vast

Isaiah9:9 All the people — Ephraim and the inhabitants of Samaria  — will know it. They will say with pride and arrogance:

Isaiah9:13 The people did not turn to him who struck them; they did not seek the LORD of Armies.

Isaiah9:15 The head is the elder
Isaiah9:16 The leaders of the people mislead them

Isaiah10:2 to keep the poor from getting a fair trial and to deprive the needy among my people of justice
Isaiah10:3 What will you do on the day of punishment when devastation comes from far away? Who will you run to for help? Where will you leave your wealth?

Isaiah10:15 Does an ax exalt itself above the one who chops with it? Does a saw magnify itself above the one who saws with it? It would be like a rod waving the one who lifts it! It would be like a staff lifting the one who isn’t wood!

Isaiah10:18 He will completely destroy the glory of its forests and orchards as a sickness consumes a person.
Isaiah10:19 The remaining trees of its forest will be so few in number that a child could count them.

Isaiah10:22 Israel

Isaiah11:7 The cow and the bear will graze

Jeremiah1:1 The words of Jeremiah
Jeremiah1:2 The word of the LORD came to him in the thirteenth year of the reign of Josiah son of Amon
Jeremiah1:3 It also came throughout the days of Jehoiakim son of Josiah
Jeremiah1:4 The word of the LORD came to me:
Jeremiah1:5 I chose you before I formed you in the womb; I set you apart before you were born. I appointed you a prophet to the nations.
Jeremiah1:6 But I protested
Jeremiah1:7 Then the LORD said to me: Do not say
Jeremiah1:8 Do not be afraid of anyone
Jeremiah1:9 Then the LORD reached out his hand
Jeremiah1:10 See
Jeremiah1:11 Then the word of the LORD came to me
Jeremiah1:12 The LORD said to me
Jeremiah1:13 Again the word of the LORD came to me asking
Jeremiah1:14 Then the LORD said to me
Jeremiah1:15 Indeed
Jeremiah1:16 “I will pronounce my judgments against them for all the evil they did when they abandoned me to burn incense to other gods and to worship the works of their own hands.
Jeremiah1:17 “Now
Jeremiah1:18 Today
Jeremiah1:19 They will fight against you but never prevail over you
Jeremiah2:1 The word of the LORD came to me:
Jeremiah2:2 “Go and announce directly to Jerusalem that this is what the LORD says: I remember the loyalty of your youth
Jeremiah2:3 Israel was holy to the LORD
Jeremiah2:4 Hear the word of the LORD
Jeremiah2:5 This is what the LORD says: What fault did your fathers find in me that they went so far from me
Jeremiah2:6 They stopped asking
Jeremiah2:7 I brought you to a fertile land to eat its fruit and bounty
Jeremiah2:8 The priests quit asking
Jeremiah2:9 Therefore
Jeremiah2:10 Cross over to the coasts of Cyprus and take a look. Send someone to Kedar and consider carefully; see if there has ever been anything like this:
Jeremiah2:11 Has a nation ever exchanged its gods? (But they were not gods! ) Yet my people have exchanged their Glory for useless idols.
Jeremiah2:12 Be appalled at this
Jeremiah2:13 For my people have committed a double evil: They have abandoned me
Jeremiah2:14 Is Israel a slave? Was he born into slavery? Why else has he become a prey?
Jeremiah2:15 The young lions have roared at him; they have roared loudly. They have laid waste his land. His cities are in ruins

Jeremiah16:1 The word of the LORD came to me:
Jeremiah16:2 “Do not marry or have sons or daughters in this place.
Jeremiah16:3 For this is what the LORD says concerning sons and daughters born in this place as well as concerning the mothers who bear them and the fathers who father them in this land:
Jeremiah16:4 They will die from deadly diseases. They will not be mourned or buried but will be like manure on the soil’s surface. They will be finished off by sword and famine. Their corpses will become food for the birds of the sky and for the wild animals of the land.
Jeremiah16:5 “For this is what the LORD says: Don’t enter a house where a mourning feast is taking place. Don’t go to lament or sympathize with them
Jeremiah16:6 “Both great and small will die in this land without burial. No lament will be made for them
Jeremiah16:7 Food won’t be provided for the mourner to comfort him because of the dead. A consoling drink won’t be given him for the loss of his father or mother.
Jeremiah16:8 Do not enter the house where feasting is taking place to sit with them to eat and drink.
Jeremiah16:9 For this is what the LORD of Armies
Jeremiah16:10 “When you tell these people all these things
Jeremiah16:11 Then you will answer them
Jeremiah16:12 You did more evil than your fathers. Look
Jeremiah16:13 So I will hurl you from this land into a land that you and your fathers are not familiar with. There you will worship other gods both day and night
Jeremiah16:14 “However
Jeremiah16:15 but rather
Jeremiah16:16 “I am about to send for many fishermen”  — this is the LORD’s declaration — “and they will fish for them. Then I will send for many hunters
Jeremiah16:17 for my gaze takes in all their ways. They are not concealed from me
Jeremiah16:18 I will first repay them double for their iniquity and sin because they have polluted my land. They have filled my inheritance with the carcasses of their abhorrent and detestable idols.”
Jeremiah16:19 LORD
Jeremiah16:20 Can one make gods for himself? But they are not gods.
Jeremiah16:21 “Therefore
Jeremiah17:1 The sin of Judah is inscribed with an iron stylus. With a diamond point it is engraved on the tablet of their hearts and on the horns of their altars
Jeremiah17:2 while their children remember their altars and their Asherah poles
Jeremiah17:3 my mountains in the countryside. I will give up your wealth and all your treasures as plunder because of the sin of your high places in all your borders.
Jeremiah17:4 You will
Jeremiah17:5 This is what the LORD says: Cursed is the person who trusts in mankind. He makes human flesh his strength
Jeremiah17:6 He will be like a juniper in the Arabah; he cannot see when good comes but dwells in the parched places in the wilderness
Jeremiah17:7 The person who trusts in the LORD
Jeremiah17:8 He will be like a tree planted by water: it sends its roots out toward a stream
Jeremiah17:9 The heart is more deceitful than anything else
Jeremiah17:10 I
Jeremiah17:11 He who makes a fortune unjustly is like a partridge that hatches eggs it didn’t lay. In the middle of his life his riches will abandon him
Jeremiah17:12 A glorious throne on high from the beginning is the place of our sanctuary.
Jeremiah17:13 LORD
Jeremiah17:14 Heal me
Jeremiah17:15 Hear how they keep challenging me
Jeremiah17:16 But I have not run away from being your shepherd
Jeremiah17:17 Don’t become a terror to me. You are my refuge in the day of disaster.
Jeremiah17:18 Let my persecutors be put to shame
Jeremiah17:19 This is what the LORD said to me
Jeremiah17:20 Announce to them
Jeremiah17:21 This is what the LORD says: Watch yourselves; do not pick up a load and bring it in through Jerusalem’s gates on the Sabbath day.
Jeremiah17:22 Do not carry a load out of your houses on the Sabbath day or do any work
Jeremiah17:23 They wouldn’t listen or pay attention but became obstinate
Jeremiah17:24 “‘However
Jeremiah17:25 kings and princes will enter through the gates of this city. They will sit on the throne of David; they will ride in chariots and on horses with their officials
Jeremiah17:26 Then people will come from the cities of Judah and from the area around Jerusalem
Jeremiah17:27 But if you do not listen to me to keep the Sabbath day holy by not carrying a load while entering the gates of Jerusalem on the Sabbath day

Jeremiah18:2 “Go down at once to the potter’s house; there I will reveal my words to you.”
Jeremiah18:3 So I went down to the potter’s house
Jeremiah18:4 But the jar that he was making from the clay became flawed in the potter’s hand
Jeremiah18:5 The word of the LORD came to me:
Jeremiah18:6 “House of Israel
Jeremiah18:7 At one moment I might announce concerning a nation or a kingdom that I will uproot
Jeremiah18:8 However
Jeremiah18:9 At another time I might announce concerning a nation or a kingdom that I will build and plant it.
Jeremiah18:10 However
Jeremiah18:11 So now
Jeremiah18:12 But they will say
Jeremiah18:13 Therefore
Jeremiah18:14 Does the snow of Lebanon ever leave the highland crags? Or does cold water flowing from a distance ever fail?
Jeremiah18:15 Yet my people have forgotten me. They burn incense to worthless idols that make them stumble in their ways on the ancient roads
Jeremiah18:16 They have made their land a horror
Jeremiah18:17 I will scatter them before the enemy like the east wind. I will show them my back and not my face on the day of their calamity.
Jeremiah18:18 Then certain ones said
Jeremiah18:19 Pay attention to me
Jeremiah18:20 Should good be repaid with evil? Yet they have dug a pit for me. Remember how I stood before you to speak good on their behalf
Jeremiah18:21 Therefore
Jeremiah18:22 Let a cry be heard from their houses when you suddenly bring raiders against them
Jeremiah18:23 But you

Jeremiah19:10 “Then you are to shatter the jar in the presence of the people going with you

Jeremiah19:13 The houses of Jerusalem and the houses of the kings of Judah will become impure like that place Topheth — all the houses on whose rooftops they have burned incense to all the stars in the sky and poured out drink offerings to other gods.’”

Jeremiah22:1 This is what the LORD says: “Go down to the palace of the king of Judah and announce this word there.
Jeremiah22:2 You are to say
Jeremiah22:3 This is what the LORD says: Administer justice and righteousness. Rescue the victim of robbery from his oppressor. Don’t exploit or brutalize the resident alien
Jeremiah22:4 For if you conscientiously carry out this word
Jeremiah22:5 But if you do not obey these words

Jeremiah22:9 They will answer
Jeremiah22:10 Do not weep for the dead; do not mourn for him. Weep bitterly for the one who has gone away
Jeremiah22:11 For this is what the LORD says concerning Shallum son of Josiah
Jeremiah22:12 but he will die in the place where they deported him
Jeremiah22:13 Woe for the one who builds his palace through unrighteousness
Jeremiah22:14 who says
Jeremiah22:15 Are you a king because you excel in cedar? Didn’t your father eat and drink and administer justice and righteousness? Then it went well with him.
Jeremiah22:16 He took up the case of the poor and needy; then it went well. Is this not what it means to know me? This is the LORD’s declaration.
Jeremiah22:17 But you have eyes and a heart for nothing except your own dishonest profit
Jeremiah22:18 Therefore
Jeremiah22:19 He will be buried like a donkey

Jeremiah22:25 In fact

Jeremiah23:1 “Woe to the shepherds who destroy and scatter the sheep of my pasture!” This is the LORD’s declaration.
Jeremiah23:2 “Therefore
Jeremiah23:3 “I will gather the remnant of my flock from all the lands where I have banished them
Jeremiah23:4 I will raise up shepherds over them who will tend them. They will no longer be afraid or discouraged
Jeremiah23:5 “Look
Jeremiah23:6 In his days Judah will be saved
Jeremiah23:7 “Look
Jeremiah23:8 but
Jeremiah23:9 Concerning the prophets: My heart is broken within me
Jeremiah23:10 For the land is full of adulterers; the land mourns because of the curse
Jeremiah23:11 because both prophet and priest are ungodly
Jeremiah23:12 Therefore
Jeremiah23:13 Among the prophets of Samaria I saw something disgusting: They prophesied by Baal and led my people Israel astray.
Jeremiah23:14 Among the prophets of Jerusalem also I saw a horrible thing: They commit adultery and walk in lies. They strengthen the hands of evildoers
Jeremiah23:15 Therefore
Jeremiah23:16 This is what the LORD of Armies says: “Do not listen to the words of the prophets who prophesy to you. They are deluding you. They speak visions from their own minds
Jeremiah23:17 They keep on saying to those who despise me
Jeremiah23:18 For who has stood in the council of the LORD to see and hear his word? Who has paid attention to his word and obeyed?
Jeremiah23:19 Look
Jeremiah23:20 The LORD’s anger will not turn away until he has completely fulfilled the purposes of his heart. In time to come you will understand it clearly.
Jeremiah23:21 I did not send out these prophets
Jeremiah23:22 If they had really stood in my council
Jeremiah23:23 “Am I a God who is only near”  — this is the LORD’s declaration — “and not a God who is far away?
Jeremiah23:24 Can a person hide in secret places where I cannot see him?”  — the LORD’s declaration. “Do I not fill the heavens and the earth?”  — the LORD’s declaration.
Jeremiah23:25 “I have heard what the prophets who prophesy a lie in my name have said: ‘I had a dream! I had a dream! ’
Jeremiah23:26 How long will this continue in the minds of the prophets prophesying lies
Jeremiah23:27 Through their dreams that they tell one another
Jeremiah23:28 The prophet who has only a dream should recount the dream
Jeremiah23:29 “Is not my word like fire” — this is the LORD’s declaration — “and like a hammer that pulverizes rock?
Jeremiah23:30 Therefore
Jeremiah23:31 I am against the prophets” — the LORD’s declaration — “who use their own tongues to make a declaration.
Jeremiah23:32 I am against those who prophesy false dreams”  — the LORD’s declaration — “telling them and leading my people astray with their reckless lies. It was not I who sent or commanded them
Jeremiah23:33 “Now when these people or a prophet or a priest asks you
Jeremiah23:34 As for the prophet
Jeremiah23:35 This is what each man is to say to his friend and to his brother: ‘What has the LORD answered? ’ or ‘What has the LORD spoken?’
Jeremiah23:36 But no longer refer to the burden of the LORD
Jeremiah23:37 Say to the prophet
Jeremiah23:38 But if you say
Jeremiah23:39 I will surely forget you. I will throw you away from my presence — both you and the city that I gave you and your fathers.
Jeremiah23:40 I will bring on you everlasting disgrace and humiliation that will never be forgotten.”

Jeremiah25:5 He announced

Jeremiah25:7 “‘But you have not obeyed me’ — this is the LORD’s declaration — ‘with the result that you have angered me by the work of your hands and brought disaster on yourselves.’
Jeremiah25:8 “Therefore
Jeremiah25:9 I am going to send for all the families of the north’  — this is the LORD’s declaration — ‘and send for my servant Nebuchadnezzar king of Babylon
Jeremiah25:10 I will eliminate the sound of joy and gladness from them — the voice of the groom and the bride
Jeremiah25:11 This whole land will become a desolate ruin
Jeremiah25:12 When the seventy years are completed
Jeremiah25:13 I will bring on that land all my words I have spoken against it
Jeremiah25:14 For many nations and great kings will enslave them
Jeremiah25:15 This is what the LORD
Jeremiah25:16 They will drink
Jeremiah25:17 So I took the cup from the LORD’s hand and made all the nations to whom the LORD sent me drink from it.

Jeremiah25:20 and all the mixed peoples; all the kings of the land of Uz; all the kings of the land of the Philistines — Ashkelon

Jeremiah25:30 “As for you
Jeremiah25:31 The tumult reaches to the ends of the earth because the LORD brings a case against the nations. He enters into judgment with all people. As for the wicked
Jeremiah25:32 “This is what the LORD of Armies says: Pay attention! Disaster spreads from nation to nation. A huge storm is stirred up from the ends of the earth.”

Jeremiah25:34 Wail
Jeremiah25:35 Flight will be impossible for the shepherds

Jeremiah25:37 Peaceful grazing land will become lifeless because of the LORD’s burning anger.
Jeremiah25:38 He has left his den like a lion

Jeremiah26:2 “This is what the LORD says: Stand in the courtyard of the LORD’s temple and speak all the words I have commanded you to speak to all Judah’s cities that are coming to worship there. Do not hold back a word.
Jeremiah26:3 Perhaps they will listen and turn — each from his evil way of life — so that I might relent concerning the disaster that I plan to do to them because of the evil of their deeds.

Jeremiah27:21 Yes
Jeremiah27:22 ‘They will be taken to Babylon and will remain there until I attend to them again.’ This is the LORD’s declaration. ‘Then I will bring them up and restore them to this place.’”

Jeremiah29:3 He sent the letter with Elasah son of Shaphan and Gemariah son of Hilkiah
Jeremiah29:4 This is what the LORD of Armies
Jeremiah29:5 “Build houses and live in them. Plant gardens and eat their produce.
Jeremiah29:6 Find wives for yourselves
Jeremiah29:7 Pursue the well-being of the city I have deported you to. Pray to the LORD on its behalf
Jeremiah29:8 For this is what the LORD of Armies

Jeremiah29:10 For this is what the LORD says: “When seventy years for Babylon are complete

Jeremiah29:12 You will call to me and come and pray to me
Jeremiah29:13 You will seek me and find me when you search for me with all your heart.

Jeremiah29:19 I will do this because they have not listened to my words” — this is the LORD’s declaration — “the words that I sent to them with my servants the prophets time and time again. And you too have not listened.” This is the LORD’s declaration.

Jeremiah29:27 So now

Jeremiah29:31 “Send a message to all the exiles
Jeremiah29:32 this is what the LORD says: I am about to punish Shemaiah the Nehelamite and his descendants. There will not be even one of his descendants living among these people
Jeremiah30:1 This is the word that came to Jeremiah from the LORD.
Jeremiah30:2 “This is what the LORD
Jeremiah30:3 for look
Jeremiah30:4 These are the words the LORD spoke to Israel and Judah.
Jeremiah30:5 This is what the LORD says: We have heard a cry of terror
Jeremiah30:6 Ask and see whether a male can give birth. Why then do I see every man with his hands on his stomach like a woman in labor and every face turned pale?
Jeremiah30:7 How awful that day will be! There will be no other like it! It will be a time of trouble for Jacob
Jeremiah30:8 On that day — this is the declaration of the LORD of Armies — I will break his yoke from your neck and tear off your chains
Jeremiah30:9 They will serve the LORD their God and David their king
Jeremiah30:10 As for you
Jeremiah30:11 For I will be with you — this is the LORD’s declaration — to save you! I will bring destruction on all the nations where I have scattered you; however
Jeremiah30:12 For this is what the LORD says: Your injury is incurable; your wound most severe.
Jeremiah30:13 You have no defender for your case. There is no remedy for your sores
Jeremiah30:14 All your lovers have forgotten you; they no longer look for you
Jeremiah30:15 Why do you cry out about your injury? Your pain has no cure! I have done these things to you because of your enormous guilt and your innumerable sins.
Jeremiah30:16 Nevertheless
Jeremiah30:17 But I will bring you health and will heal you of your wounds — this is the LORD’s declaration — for they call you Outcast
Jeremiah30:18 This is what the LORD says: I will certainly restore the fortunes of Jacob’s tents and show compassion on his dwellings. Every city will be rebuilt on its mound; every citadel will stand on its proper site.
Jeremiah30:19 Thanksgiving will come out of them
Jeremiah30:20 His children will be as in past days; his congregation will be established in my presence. I will punish all his oppressors.
Jeremiah30:21 Jacob’s leader will be one of them; his ruler will issue from him. I will invite him to me
Jeremiah30:22 You will be my people
Jeremiah30:23 Look
Jeremiah30:24 The LORD’s burning anger will not turn back until he has completely fulfilled the purposes of his heart. In time to come you will understand it.
Jeremiah31:1 “At that time” — this is the LORD’s declaration — “I will be the God of all the families of Israel

Jeremiah31:14 I will refresh the priests with an abundance

Jeremiah31:16 This is what the LORD says: Keep your voice from weeping and your eyes from tears

Jeremiah50:1 This is the word the LORD spoke about Babylon
Jeremiah50:2 Announce to the nations; proclaim and raise up a signal flag; proclaim
Jeremiah50:3 For a nation from the north will attack her; it will make her land desolate. No one will be living in it — both people and animals will escape.
Jeremiah50:4 In those days and at that time — this is the LORD’s declaration — the Israelites and Judeans will come together
Jeremiah50:5 They will ask about Zion
Jeremiah50:6 My people were lost sheep; their shepherds led them astray
Jeremiah50:7 Whoever found them devoured them. Their adversaries said
Jeremiah50:8 Escape from Babylon; depart from the Chaldeans’ land. Be like the rams that lead the flock.
Jeremiah50:9 For I will soon stir up and bring against Babylon an assembly of great nations from the north country. They will line up in battle formation against her; from there she will be captured. Their arrows will be like a skilled warrior who does not return empty-handed.
Jeremiah50:10 The Chaldeans will become plunder; all Babylon’s plunderers will be fully satisfied. This is the LORD’s declaration.
Jeremiah50:11 Because you rejoice
Jeremiah50:12 your mother will be utterly humiliated; she who bore you will be put to shame. Look! She will lag behind all the nations — an arid wilderness
Jeremiah50:13 Because of the LORD’s wrath
Jeremiah50:14 Line up in battle formation around Babylon
Jeremiah50:15 Raise a war cry against her on every side! She has thrown up her hands in surrender; her defense towers have fallen; her walls are demolished. Since this is the LORD’s vengeance
Jeremiah50:16 Cut off the sower from Babylon as well as him who wields the sickle at harvest time. Because of the oppressor’s sword
Jeremiah50:17 Israel is a stray lamb
Jeremiah50:18 Therefore
Jeremiah50:19 I will return Israel to his grazing land
Jeremiah50:20 In those days and at that time — this is the LORD’s declaration — one will search for Israel’s iniquity
Jeremiah50:21 Attack the land of Merathaim
Jeremiah50:22 The sound of war is in the land  — a crushing blow!
Jeremiah50:23 How the hammer of the whole earth is cut down and smashed! What a horror Babylon has become among the nations!
Jeremiah50:24 Babylon
Jeremiah50:25 The LORD opened his armory and brought out his weapons of wrath
Jeremiah50:26 Come against her from the most distant places. Open her granaries; pile her up like mounds of grain and completely destroy her. Leave her no survivors.
Jeremiah50:27 Put all her young bulls to the sword; let them go down to the slaughter. Woe to them because their day has come
Jeremiah50:28 There is a voice of fugitives and refugees from the land of Babylon. The voice announces in Zion the vengeance of the LORD our God
Jeremiah50:29 Summon the archers to Babylon
Jeremiah50:30 Therefore
Jeremiah50:31 Look
Jeremiah50:32 The arrogant will stumble and fall with no one to pick him up. I will set fire to his cities
Jeremiah50:33 This is what the LORD of Armies says: Israelites and Judeans alike have been oppressed. All their captors hold them fast; they refuse to release them.
Jeremiah50:34 Their Redeemer is strong; the LORD of Armies is his name. He will fervently champion their cause so that he might bring rest to the earth but turmoil to those who live in Babylon.
Jeremiah50:35 A sword is over the Chaldeans — this is the LORD’s declaration — against those who live in Babylon
Jeremiah50:36 A sword is against the diviners
Jeremiah50:37 A sword is against his horses and chariots and against all the foreigners among them
Jeremiah50:38 A drought will come on her waters
Jeremiah50:39 Therefore
Jeremiah50:40 Just as God demolished Sodom and Gomorrah and their neighboring towns  — this is the LORD’s declaration — so no one will live there; no human being will stay in it even temporarily as a temporary resident.
Jeremiah50:41 Look! A people comes from the north. A great nation and many kings will be stirred up from the remote regions of the earth.
Jeremiah50:42 They grasp bow and javelin. They are cruel and show no mercy. Their voice roars like the sea
Jeremiah50:43 The king of Babylon has heard about them; his hands have become weak. Distress has seized him — pain

Jeremiah51:3 Don’t let the archer string his bow; don’t let him put on his armor. Don’t spare her young men; completely destroy her entire army!
Jeremiah51:4 Those who were slain will fall in the land of the Chaldeans
Jeremiah51:5 For Israel and Judah are not left widowed by their God
Jeremiah51:6 Leave Babylon; save your lives
Jeremiah51:7 Babylon was a gold cup in the LORD’s hand
Jeremiah51:8 Suddenly Babylon fell and was shattered. Wail for her; get balm for her wound — perhaps she can be healed.
Jeremiah51:9 We tried to heal Babylon
Jeremiah51:10 The LORD has brought about our vindication; come
Jeremiah51:11 Sharpen the arrows! Fill the quivers! The LORD has roused the spirit of the kings of the Medes because his plan is aimed at Babylon to destroy her
Jeremiah51:12 Raise up a signal flag against the walls of Babylon; fortify the watch post; set the watchmen in place; prepare the ambush. For the LORD has both planned and accomplished what he has threatened against those who live in Babylon.
Jeremiah51:13 You who reside by abundant water
Jeremiah51:14 The LORD of Armies has sworn by himself: I will fill you up with men as with locusts
Jeremiah51:15 He made the earth by his power
Jeremiah51:16 When he thunders
Jeremiah51:17 Everyone is stupid and ignorant. Every goldsmith is put to shame by his carved image
Jeremiah51:18 They are worthless
Jeremiah51:19 Jacob’s Portion is not like these because he is the one who formed all things. Israel is the tribe of his inheritance; the LORD of Armies is his name.
Jeremiah51:20 You are my war club
Jeremiah51:21 With you I will smash the horse and its rider; with you I will smash the chariot and its rider.
Jeremiah51:22 With you I will smash man and woman; with you I will smash the old man and the youth; with you I will smash the young man and the young woman.
Jeremiah51:23 With you I will smash the shepherd and his flock; with you I will smash the farmer and his ox-team. With you I will smash governors and officials.
Jeremiah51:24 “Before your very eyes
Jeremiah51:25 Look
Jeremiah51:26 No one will be able to retrieve a cornerstone or a foundation stone from you

Jeremiah51:28 Set apart the nations for battle against her — the kings of Media
Jeremiah51:29 The earth quakes and trembles because the LORD’s intentions against Babylon stand: to make the land of Babylon a desolation
Jeremiah51:30 Babylon’s warriors have stopped fighting; they sit in their strongholds. Their might is exhausted; they have become like women. Babylon’s homes have been set ablaze
Jeremiah51:31 Messenger races to meet messenger
Jeremiah51:32 The fords have been seized
Jeremiah51:33 For this is what the LORD of Armies

Jeremiah51:36 Therefore
Jeremiah51:37 Babylon will become a heap of rubble

Jeremiah51:40 I will bring them down like lambs to the slaughter
Jeremiah51:41 How Sheshach has been captured
Jeremiah51:42 The sea has risen over Babylon; she is covered with its tumultuous waves.
Jeremiah51:43 Her cities have become a desolation
Jeremiah51:44 I will punish Bel in Babylon. I will make him vomit what he swallowed. The nations will no longer stream to him; even Babylon’s wall will fall.
Jeremiah51:45 Come out from among her
Jeremiah51:46 May you not become cowardly and fearful when the report is proclaimed in the land
Jeremiah51:47 Therefore

Jeremiah51:49 Babylon must fall because of the slain of Israel
Jeremiah51:50 You who have escaped the sword

Jeremiah51:55 For the LORD is going to devastate Babylon; he will silence her mighty voice. Their waves roar like a huge torrent; the tumult of their voice resounds
Jeremiah51:56 for a destroyer is coming against her
