Jeremiah52:1 Zedekiah was twenty-one years old when he became king
Jeremiah52:2 Zedekiah did what was evil in the LORD’s sight just as Jehoiakim had done.
Jeremiah52:3 Because of the LORD’s anger
Jeremiah52:4 In the ninth year of Zedekiah’s reign
Jeremiah52:5 The city was under siege until King Zedekiah’s eleventh year.
Jeremiah52:6 By the ninth day of the fourth month the famine was so severe in the city that the common people had no food.
Jeremiah52:7 Then the city was broken into
Jeremiah52:8 The Chaldean army pursued the king and overtook Zedekiah in the plains of Jericho. Zedekiah’s entire army left him and scattered.
Jeremiah52:9 The Chaldeans seized the king and brought him to the king of Babylon at Riblah in the land of Hamath
Jeremiah52:10 At Riblah the king of Babylon slaughtered Zedekiah’s sons before his eyes
Jeremiah52:11 Then he blinded Zedekiah and bound him with bronze chains. The king of Babylon brought Zedekiah to Babylon
Jeremiah52:12 On the tenth day of the fifth month — which was the nineteenth year of King Nebuchadnezzar
Jeremiah52:13 He burned the LORD’s temple
Jeremiah52:14 The whole Chaldean army with the captain of the guards tore down all the walls surrounding Jerusalem.
Jeremiah52:15 Nebuzaradan
Jeremiah52:16 But Nebuzaradan
Jeremiah52:17 Now the Chaldeans broke into pieces the bronze pillars for the LORD’s temple and the water carts and the bronze basin that were in the LORD’s temple
Jeremiah52:18 They also took the pots
Jeremiah52:19 The captain of the guards took away the bowls
Jeremiah52:20 As for the two pillars
Jeremiah52:21 One pillar was 27 feet tall
Jeremiah52:22 and had a bronze capital on top of it. One capital
Jeremiah52:23 Each capital had ninety-six pomegranates all around it. All the pomegranates around the grating numbered one hundred.
Jeremiah52:24 The captain of the guards also took away Seraiah the chief priest
Jeremiah52:25 From the city he took a court official who had been appointed over the warriors; seven trusted royal aides found in the city; the secretary of the commander of the army
Jeremiah52:26 Nebuzaradan
Jeremiah52:27 The king of Babylon put them to death at Riblah in the land of Hamath. So Judah went into exile from its land.
Jeremiah52:28 These are the people Nebuchadnezzar deported: in the seventh year
Jeremiah52:29 in his eighteenth year
Jeremiah52:30 in Nebuchadnezzar’s twenty-third year
Jeremiah52:31 On the twenty-fifth day of the twelfth month of the thirty-seventh year of the exile of Judah’s King Jehoiachin
Jeremiah52:32 He spoke kindly to him and set his throne above the thrones of the kings who were with him in Babylon.
Jeremiah52:33 So Jehoiachin changed his prison clothes
Jeremiah52:34 As for his allowance
Lamentations1:1 How she sits alone
Lamentations1:2 She weeps bitterly during the night
Lamentations1:3 Judah has gone into exile following affliction and harsh slavery; she lives among the nations but finds no place to rest. All her pursuers have overtaken her in narrow places.
Lamentations1:4 The roads to Zion mourn
Lamentations1:5 Her adversaries have become her masters; her enemies are at ease
Lamentations1:6 All the splendor has vanished from Daughter Zion. Her leaders are like stags that find no pasture; they stumble away exhausted before the hunter.
Lamentations1:7 During the days of her affliction and homelessness Jerusalem remembers all her precious belongings that were hers in days of old. When her people fell into the adversary’s hand
Lamentations1:8 Jerusalem has sinned grievously; therefore
Lamentations1:9 Her uncleanness stains her skirts. She never considered her end. Her downfall was astonishing; there was no one to comfort her. LORD
Lamentations1:10 The adversary has seized all her precious belongings. She has even seen the nations enter her sanctuary — those you had forbidden to enter your assembly.
Lamentations1:11 All her people groan while they search for bread. They have traded their precious belongings for food in order to stay alive. LORD
Lamentations1:12 Is this nothing to you
Lamentations1:13 He sent fire from on high into my bones; he made it descend. He spread a net for my feet and turned me back. He made me desolate
Lamentations1:14 My transgressions have been formed into a yoke
Lamentations1:15 The Lord has rejected all the mighty men within me. He has summoned an army against me to crush my young warriors. The Lord has trampled Virgin Daughter Judah like grapes in a winepress.
Lamentations1:16 I weep because of these things; my eyes flow with tears. For there is no one nearby to comfort me
Lamentations1:17 Zion stretches out her hands; there is no one to comfort her. The LORD has issued a decree against Jacob that his neighbors should be his adversaries. Jerusalem has become something impure among them.
Lamentations1:18 The LORD is just
Lamentations1:19 I called to my lovers
Lamentations1:20 LORD
Lamentations1:21 People have heard me groaning
Lamentations1:22 Let all their wickedness come before you
Lamentations2:1 How the Lord has overshadowed Daughter Zion with his anger! He has thrown down Israel’s glory from heaven to earth. He did not acknowledge his footstool in the day of his anger.
Lamentations2:2 Without compassion the Lord has swallowed up all the dwellings of Jacob. In his wrath he has demolished the fortified cities of Daughter Judah. He brought them to the ground and defiled the kingdom and its leaders.
Lamentations2:3 He has cut off every horn of Israel in his burning anger and withdrawn his right hand in the presence of the enemy. He has blazed against Jacob like a flaming fire that consumes everything.
Lamentations2:4 He has strung his bow like an enemy; his right hand is positioned like an adversary. He has killed everyone who was the delight to the eye
Lamentations2:5 The Lord is like an enemy; he has swallowed up Israel. He swallowed up all its palaces and destroyed its fortified cities. He has multiplied mourning and lamentation within Daughter Judah.
Lamentations2:6 He has wrecked his temple as if it were merely a shack in a field
Lamentations2:7 The Lord has rejected his altar
Lamentations2:8 The LORD determined to destroy the wall of Daughter Zion. He stretched out a measuring line and did not restrain himself from destroying. He made the ramparts and walls grieve; together they waste away.
Lamentations2:9 Zion’s gates have fallen to the ground; he has destroyed and shattered the bars on her gates. Her king and her leaders live among the nations
Lamentations2:10 The elders of Daughter Zion sit on the ground in silence. They have thrown dust on their heads and put on sackcloth. The young women of Jerusalem have bowed their heads to the ground.
Lamentations2:11 My eyes are worn out from weeping; I am churning within. My heart is poured out in grief because of the destruction of my dear people
Lamentations2:12 They cry out to their mothers
Lamentations2:13 What can I say on your behalf? What can I compare you to
Lamentations2:14 Your prophets saw visions for you that were empty and deceptive; they did not reveal your iniquity and so restore your fortunes. They saw pronouncements for you that were empty and misleading.
Lamentations2:15 All who pass by scornfully clap their hands at you. They hiss and shake their heads at Daughter Jerusalem: Is this the city that was called the perfection of beauty
Lamentations2:16 All your enemies open their mouths against you. They hiss and gnash their teeth
Lamentations2:17 The LORD has done what he planned; he has accomplished his decree
Lamentations2:18 The hearts of the people cry out to the Lord. Wall of Daughter Zion
Lamentations2:19 Arise
Lamentations2:20 LORD
Lamentations2:21 Both young and old are lying on the ground in the streets. My young women and young men have fallen by the sword. You have killed them in the day of your anger
Lamentations2:22 You summon those who terrorize me on every side
Lamentations3:1 I am the man who has seen affliction under the rod of God’s wrath.
Lamentations3:2 He has driven me away and forced me to walk in darkness instead of light.
Lamentations3:3 Yes
Lamentations3:4 He has worn away my flesh and skin; he has broken my bones.
Lamentations3:5 He has laid siege against me
Lamentations3:6 He has made me dwell in darkness like those who have been dead for ages.
Lamentations3:7 He has walled me in so I cannot get out; he has weighed me down with chains.
Lamentations3:8 Even when I cry out and plead for help
Lamentations3:9 He has walled in my ways with blocks of stone; he has made my paths crooked.
Lamentations3:10 He is a bear waiting in ambush
Lamentations3:11 He forced me off my way and tore me to pieces; he left me desolate.
Lamentations3:12 He strung his bow and set me as the target for his arrow.
Lamentations3:13 He pierced my kidneys with shafts from his quiver.
Lamentations3:14 I am a laughingstock to all my people
Lamentations3:15 He filled me with bitterness
Lamentations3:16 He ground my teeth with gravel and made me cower in the dust.
Lamentations3:17 I have been deprived of peace; I have forgotten what prosperity is.
Lamentations3:18 Then I thought
Lamentations3:19 Remember my affliction and my homelessness
Lamentations3:20 I continually remember them and have become depressed.
Lamentations3:21 Yet I call this to mind
Lamentations3:22 Because of the LORD’s faithful love we do not perish
Lamentations3:23 They are new every morning; great is your faithfulness!
Lamentations3:24 I say
Lamentations3:25 The LORD is good to those who wait for him
Lamentations3:26 It is good to wait quietly for salvation from the LORD.
Lamentations3:27 It is good for a man to bear the yoke while he is still young.
Lamentations3:28 Let him sit alone and be silent
Lamentations3:29 Let him put his mouth in the dust  — perhaps there is still hope.
Lamentations3:30 Let him offer his cheek to the one who would strike him; let him be filled with disgrace.
Lamentations3:31 For the Lord will not reject us forever.
Lamentations3:32 Even if he causes suffering
Lamentations3:33 For he does not enjoy bringing affliction or suffering on mankind.
Lamentations3:34 Crushing all the prisoners of the land beneath one’s feet
Lamentations3:35 denying justice to a man in the presence of the Most High
Lamentations3:36 or subverting a person in his lawsuit  — the Lord does not approve of these things.
Lamentations3:37 Who is there who speaks and it happens
Lamentations3:38 Do not both adversity and good come from the mouth of the Most High?
Lamentations3:39 Why should any living person complain
Lamentations3:40 Let us examine and probe our ways
Lamentations3:41 Let us lift up our hearts and our hands to God in heaven:
Lamentations3:42 “We have sinned and rebelled; you have not forgiven.
Lamentations3:43 “You have covered yourself in anger and pursued us; you have killed without compassion.
Lamentations3:44 You have covered yourself with a cloud so that no prayer can get through.
Lamentations3:45 You have made us disgusting filth among the peoples.
Lamentations3:46 “All our enemies open their mouths against us.
Lamentations3:47 We have experienced panic and pitfall
Lamentations3:48 My eyes flow with streams of tears because of the destruction of my dear people.
Lamentations3:49 My eyes overflow unceasingly
Lamentations3:50 until the LORD looks down from heaven and sees.
Lamentations3:51 My eyes bring me grief because of the fate of all the women in my city.
Lamentations3:52 For no reason
Lamentations3:53 They smothered my life in a pit and threw stones on me.
Lamentations3:54 Water flooded over my head
Lamentations3:55 I called on your name
Lamentations3:56 You heard my plea: Do not ignore my cry for relief.
Lamentations3:57 You came near whenever I called you; you said
Lamentations3:58 You championed my cause
Lamentations3:59 LORD
Lamentations3:60 You saw all their vengefulness
Lamentations3:61 LORD
Lamentations3:62 The slander and murmuring of my opponents attack me all day long.
Lamentations3:63 When they sit and when they rise
Lamentations3:64 You will pay them back what they deserve
Lamentations3:65 You will give them a heart filled with anguish. May your curse be on them!
Lamentations3:66 You will pursue them in anger and destroy them under your heavens.
Lamentations4:1 How the gold has become tarnished
Lamentations4:2 Zion’s precious children — once worth their weight in pure gold  — how they are regarded as clay jars
Lamentations4:3 Even jackals offer their breasts to nurse their young
Lamentations4:4 The nursing baby’s tongue clings to the roof of his mouth from thirst. Infants beg for food
Lamentations4:5 Those who used to eat delicacies are destitute in the streets; those who were reared in purple garments huddle in trash heaps.
Lamentations4:6 The punishment of my dear people is greater than that of Sodom
Lamentations4:7 Her dignitaries were brighter than snow
Lamentations4:8 Now they appear darker than soot; they are not recognized in the streets. Their skin has shriveled on their bones; it has become dry like wood.
Lamentations4:9 Those slain by the sword are better off than those slain by hunger
Lamentations4:10 The hands of compassionate women have cooked their own children; they became their food during the destruction of my dear people.
Lamentations4:11 The LORD has exhausted his wrath
Lamentations4:12 The kings of the earth and all the world’s inhabitants did not believe that an enemy or adversary could enter Jerusalem’s gates.
Lamentations4:13 Yet it happened because of the sins of her prophets and the iniquities of her priests
Lamentations4:14 Blind
Lamentations4:15 “Stay away! Unclean!” people shouted at them. “Away
Lamentations4:16 The LORD himself has scattered them; he no longer watches over them. The priests are not respected; the elders find no favor.
Lamentations4:17 All the while our eyes were failing as we looked in vain for help; we watched from our towers for a nation that would not save us.
Lamentations4:18 Our steps were closely followed so that we could not walk in our streets. Our end approached; our time ran out. Our end had come!
Lamentations4:19 Those who chased us were swifter than eagles in the sky; they relentlessly pursued us over the mountains and ambushed us in the wilderness.
Lamentations4:20 The LORD’s anointed
Lamentations4:21 So rejoice and be glad
Lamentations4:22 Daughter Zion
Lamentations5:1 LORD
Lamentations5:2 Our inheritance has been turned over to strangers
Lamentations5:3 We have become orphans
Lamentations5:4 We must pay for the water we drink; our wood comes at a price.
Lamentations5:5 We are closely pursued; we are tired
Lamentations5:6 We made a treaty with Egypt and with Assyria
Lamentations5:7 Our fathers sinned; they no longer exist
Lamentations5:8 Slaves rule over us; no one rescues us from them.
Lamentations5:9 We secure our food at the risk of our lives because of the sword in the wilderness.
Lamentations5:10 Our skin is as hot as an oven from the ravages of hunger.
Lamentations5:11 Women have been raped in Zion
Lamentations5:12 Princes have been hung up by their hands; elders are shown no respect.
Lamentations5:13 Young men labor at millstones; boys stumble under loads of wood.
Lamentations5:14 The elders have left the city gate
Lamentations5:15 Joy has left our hearts; our dancing has turned to mourning.
Lamentations5:16 The crown has fallen from our head. Woe to us
Lamentations5:17 Because of this
Lamentations5:18 because of Mount Zion
Lamentations5:19 You
Lamentations5:20 Why do you continually forget us
Lamentations5:21 LORD
Lamentations5:22 unless you have completely rejected us and are intensely angry with us.
Ezekiel1:1 In the thirtieth year
Ezekiel1:2 On the fifth day of the month — it was the fifth year of King Jehoiachin’s exile  —
Ezekiel1:3 the word of the LORD came directly to the priest Ezekiel son of Buzi
Ezekiel1:4 I looked
Ezekiel1:5 The likeness of four living creatures came from it
Ezekiel1:6 but each of them had four faces and four wings.
Ezekiel1:7 Their legs were straight
Ezekiel1:8 They had human hands under their wings on their four sides. All four of them had faces and wings.
Ezekiel1:9 Their wings were touching. The creatures did not turn as they moved; each one went straight ahead.
Ezekiel1:10 Their faces looked something like the face of a human
Ezekiel1:11 That is what their faces were like. Their wings were spread upward; each had two wings touching that of another and two wings covering its body.
Ezekiel1:12 Each creature went straight ahead. Wherever the Spirit wanted to go
Ezekiel1:13 The likeness of the living creatures was like the appearance of blazing coals of fire or like torches. Fire was moving back and forth between the living creatures; it was bright
Ezekiel1:14 The creatures were darting back and forth like flashes of lightning.
Ezekiel1:15 When I looked at the living creatures
Ezekiel1:16 The appearance of the wheels and their craftsmanship was like the gleam of beryl
Ezekiel1:17 When they moved
Ezekiel1:18 Their rims were tall and awe-inspiring. Each of their four rims were full of eyes all around.
Ezekiel1:19 When the living creatures moved
Ezekiel1:20 Wherever the Spirit wanted to go
Ezekiel1:21 When the creatures moved
Ezekiel1:22 Over the heads of the living creatures the likeness of an expanse was spread out. It gleamed like awe-inspiring crystal
Ezekiel1:23 and under the expanse their wings extended one toward another. They each also had two wings covering their bodies.
Ezekiel1:24 When they moved
Ezekiel1:25 A voice came from above the expanse over their heads; when they stopped
Ezekiel1:26 Something like a throne with the appearance of lapis lazuli was above the expanse over their heads. On the throne
Ezekiel1:27 From what seemed to be his waist up
Ezekiel1:28 The appearance of the brilliant light all around was like that of a rainbow in a cloud on a rainy day. This was the appearance of the likeness of the LORD’s glory. When I saw it

Ezekiel4:1 “Now you

Ezekiel4:7 Face the siege of Jerusalem with your arm bared

Ezekiel7:1 The word of the LORD came to me:
Ezekiel7:2 “Son of man
Ezekiel7:3 The end is now upon you; I will send my anger against you and judge you according to your ways. I will punish you for all your detestable practices.
Ezekiel7:4 I will not look on you with pity or spare you
Ezekiel7:5 This is what the Lord GOD says: Look
Ezekiel7:6 An end has come; the end has come! It has awakened against you. Look
Ezekiel7:7 Doom has come on you
Ezekiel7:8 I will pour out my wrath on you very soon; I will exhaust my anger against you and judge you according to your ways. I will punish you for all your detestable practices.
Ezekiel7:9 I will not look on you with pity or spare you. I will punish you for your ways and for your detestable practices within you. Then you will know that it is I
Ezekiel7:10 Here is the day! Here it comes! Doom is on its way. The rod has blossomed; arrogance has bloomed.
Ezekiel7:11 Violence has grown into a rod of wickedness. None of them will remain: none of that crowd
Ezekiel7:12 The time has come; the day has arrived. Let the buyer not rejoice and the seller not mourn
Ezekiel7:13 The seller will certainly not return to what was sold as long as he and the buyer remain alive. For the vision concerning her whole crowd will not be revoked
Ezekiel7:14 They have blown the trumpet and prepared everything
Ezekiel7:15 The sword is on the outside; plague and famine are on the inside. Whoever is in the field will die by the sword
Ezekiel7:16 The survivors among them will escape and live on the mountains. Like doves of the valley
Ezekiel7:17 All their hands will become weak
Ezekiel7:18 They will put on sackcloth
Ezekiel7:19 They will throw their silver into the streets
Ezekiel7:20 He appointed his beautiful ornaments for majesty
Ezekiel7:21 I will hand these things over to foreigners as plunder and to the wicked of the earth as spoil
Ezekiel7:22 I will turn my face from them as they profane my treasured place. Violent men will enter it and profane it.
Ezekiel7:23 Forge the chain
Ezekiel7:24 So I will bring the most evil of nations to take possession of their houses. I will put an end to the pride of the strong
Ezekiel7:25 Anguish is coming! They will look for peace
Ezekiel7:26 Disaster after disaster will come
Ezekiel7:27 The king will mourn; the prince will be clothed in grief; and the hands of the people of the land will tremble. I will deal with them according to their own conduct

Ezekiel13:2 “Son of man

Ezekiel13:11 therefore

Ezekiel13:14 I will demolish the wall you plastered with whitewash and knock it to the ground so that its foundation is exposed. The city will fall
Ezekiel13:15 After I exhaust my wrath against the wall and against those who plaster it with whitewash
Ezekiel13:16 those prophets of Israel who prophesied to Jerusalem and saw a vision of peace for her when there was no peace.” This is the declaration of the Lord GOD.’

Ezekiel13:18 Say

Ezekiel13:20 “‘Therefore
Ezekiel13:21 I will also tear off your veils and rescue my people from your hands
Ezekiel13:22 Because you have disheartened the righteous person with lies (when I intended no distress)
Ezekiel13:23 therefore you will no longer see false visions or practice divination. I will rescue my people from your hands. Then you will know that I am the LORD.’”

Ezekiel15:3 Can wood be taken from it to make something useful? Or can anyone make a peg from it to hang things on?
Ezekiel15:4 In fact
Ezekiel15:5 Even when it was whole it could not be made into a useful object. How much less can it ever be made into anything useful when the fire has devoured it and it is charred!”
Ezekiel15:6 Therefore
Ezekiel15:7 I will turn against them. They may have escaped from the fire
Ezekiel15:8 I will make the land desolate because they have acted unfaithfully.” This is the declaration of the Lord GOD.
Ezekiel16:1 The word of the LORD came to me again:
Ezekiel16:2 “Son of man
Ezekiel16:3 You are to say
Ezekiel16:4 As for your birth
Ezekiel16:5 No one cared enough about you to do even one of these things out of compassion for you. But you were thrown out into the open field because you were despised on the day you were born.
Ezekiel16:6 “‘I passed by you and saw you thrashing around in your blood
Ezekiel16:7 I made you thrive like plants of the field. You grew up and matured and became very beautiful. Your breasts were formed and your hair grew
Ezekiel16:8 “‘Then I passed by you and saw you
Ezekiel16:9 I washed you with water
Ezekiel16:10 I clothed you in embroidered cloth and provided you with fine leather sandals. I also wrapped you in fine linen and covered you with silk.
Ezekiel16:11 I adorned you with jewelry
Ezekiel16:12 I put a ring in your nose
Ezekiel16:13 So you were adorned with gold and silver
Ezekiel16:14 Your fame spread among the nations because of your beauty
Ezekiel16:15 “‘But you trusted in your beauty and acted like a prostitute because of your fame. You lavished your sexual favors on everyone who passed by. Your beauty became his.
Ezekiel16:16 You took some of your clothing and made colorful high places for yourself
Ezekiel16:17 You also took your beautiful jewelry made from the gold and silver I had given you
Ezekiel16:18 Then you took your embroidered clothing to cover them and set my oil and incense before them.
Ezekiel16:19 The food that I gave you — the fine flour
Ezekiel16:20 “‘You even took your sons and daughters you bore to me and sacrificed them to these images as food. Wasn’t your prostitution enough?
Ezekiel16:21 You slaughtered my children and gave them up when you passed them through the fire to the images.
Ezekiel16:22 In all your detestable practices and acts of prostitution
Ezekiel16:23 “‘Then after all your evil — Woe
Ezekiel16:24 you built yourself a mound and made yourself an elevated place in every square.
Ezekiel16:25 You built your elevated place at the head of every street and turned your beauty into a detestable thing. You spread your legs to everyone who passed by and increased your prostitution.
Ezekiel16:26 You engaged in promiscuous acts with Egyptian men
Ezekiel16:27 “‘Therefore
Ezekiel16:28 Then you engaged in prostitution with the Assyrian men because you were not satisfied. Even though you did this with them
Ezekiel16:29 So you extended your prostitution to Chaldea
Ezekiel16:30 “‘How your heart was inflamed with lust  — the declaration of the Lord GOD  — when you did all these things
Ezekiel16:31 building your mound at the head of every street and making your elevated place in every square. But you were unlike a prostitute because you scorned payment.
Ezekiel16:32 You adulterous wife
Ezekiel16:33 Men give gifts to all prostitutes
Ezekiel16:34 So you were the opposite of other women in your acts of prostitution; no one solicited you. When you paid a fee instead of one being paid to you
Ezekiel16:35 “‘Therefore
Ezekiel16:36 This is what the Lord GOD says: Because your lust was poured out and your nakedness exposed by your acts of prostitution with your lovers
Ezekiel16:37 I am therefore going to gather all the lovers you pleased  — all those you loved as well as all those you hated. I will gather them against you from all around and expose your nakedness to them so they see you completely naked.
Ezekiel16:38 I will judge you the way adulteresses and those who shed blood are judged. Then I will bring about the shedding of your blood in jealous wrath.
Ezekiel16:39 I will hand you over to them
Ezekiel16:40 They will bring a mob against you to stone you and to cut you to pieces with their swords.
Ezekiel16:41 They will burn your houses and execute judgments against you in the sight of many women. I will stop you from being a prostitute
Ezekiel16:42 So I will satisfy my wrath against you
Ezekiel16:43 Because you did not remember the days of your youth but enraged me with all these things
Ezekiel16:44 “‘Look
Ezekiel16:45 You are the daughter of your mother
Ezekiel16:46 Your older sister was Samaria
Ezekiel16:47 Didn’t you walk in their ways and do their detestable practices? It was only a short time before all your ways were more corrupt than theirs.
Ezekiel16:48 “‘As I live — the declaration of the Lord GOD  — your sister Sodom and her daughters have not behaved as you and your daughters have.
Ezekiel16:49 Now this was the iniquity of your sister Sodom: She and her daughters had pride
Ezekiel16:50 They were haughty and did detestable acts before me
Ezekiel16:51 But Samaria did not commit even half your sins. You have multiplied your detestable practices beyond theirs and made your sisters appear righteous by all the detestable acts you have committed.
Ezekiel16:52 You must also bear your disgrace
Ezekiel16:53 “‘I will restore their fortunes
Ezekiel16:54 so you will bear your disgrace and be ashamed of all you did when you comforted them.
Ezekiel16:55 As for your sisters
Ezekiel16:56 Didn’t you treat your sister Sodom as an object of scorn when you were proud
Ezekiel16:57 before your wickedness was exposed? It was like the time you were scorned by the daughters of Aram and all those around her
Ezekiel16:58 You yourself must bear the consequences of your depravity and detestable practices  — this is the LORD’s declaration.
Ezekiel16:59 “‘For this is what the Lord GOD says: I will deal with you according to what you have done
Ezekiel16:60 But I will remember the covenant I made with you in the days of your youth
Ezekiel16:61 Then you will remember your ways and be ashamed when you receive your older and younger sisters. I will give them to you as daughters
Ezekiel16:62 I will establish my covenant with you
Ezekiel16:63 so that when I make atonement for all you have done

Ezekiel18:1 The word of the LORD came to me:
Ezekiel18:2 “What do you mean by using this proverb concerning the land of Israel: ‘The fathers eat sour grapes
Ezekiel18:3 As I live” — this is the declaration of the Lord GOD  — “you will no longer use this proverb in Israel.
Ezekiel18:4 Look
Ezekiel18:5 “Suppose a man is righteous and does what is just and right:
Ezekiel18:6 He does not eat at the mountain shrines or look to the idols of the house of Israel. He does not defile his neighbor’s wife or approach a woman during her menstrual impurity.
Ezekiel18:7 He doesn’t oppress anyone but returns his collateral to the debtor. He does not commit robbery
Ezekiel18:8 He doesn’t lend at interest or for profit but keeps his hand from injustice and carries out true justice between men.
Ezekiel18:9 He follows my statutes and keeps my ordinances
Ezekiel18:10 “But suppose the man has a violent son
Ezekiel18:11 though the father has done none of them. Indeed
Ezekiel18:12 and when he oppresses the poor and needy
Ezekiel18:13 and lends at interest or for profit
Ezekiel18:14 “Now suppose he has a son who sees all the sins his father has committed
Ezekiel18:15 He does not eat at the mountain shrines or look to the idols of the house of Israel. He does not defile his neighbor’s wife.
Ezekiel18:16 He doesn’t oppress anyone
Ezekiel18:17 He keeps his hand from harming the poor
Ezekiel18:18 “As for his father
Ezekiel18:19 But you may ask
Ezekiel18:20 The person who sins is the one who will die. A son won’t suffer punishment for the father’s iniquity
Ezekiel18:21 “But if the wicked person turns from all the sins he has committed
Ezekiel18:22 None of the transgressions he has committed will be held against him. He will live because of the righteousness he has practiced.
Ezekiel18:23 Do I take any pleasure in the death of the wicked?” This is the declaration of the Lord GOD. “Instead
Ezekiel18:24 But when a righteous person turns from his righteousness and acts unjustly
Ezekiel18:25 “But you say
Ezekiel18:26 When a righteous person turns from his righteousness and acts unjustly
Ezekiel18:27 But if a wicked person turns from the wickedness he has committed and does what is just and right
Ezekiel18:28 He will certainly live because he thought it over and turned from all the transgressions he had committed; he will not die.
Ezekiel18:29 But the house of Israel says
Ezekiel18:30 “Therefore
Ezekiel18:31 Throw off all the transgressions you have committed
Ezekiel18:32 For I take no pleasure in anyone’s death.” This is the declaration of the Lord GOD. “So repent and live!

Ezekiel26:2 “Son of man

Ezekiel26:11 He will trample all your streets with the hooves of his horses. He will slaughter your people with the sword
Ezekiel26:12 They will take your wealth as spoil and plunder your merchandise. They will also demolish your walls and tear down your beautiful homes. Then they will throw your stones
Ezekiel26:13 I will put an end to the noise of your songs
Ezekiel26:14 I will turn you into a bare rock
Ezekiel26:15 This is what the Lord GOD says to Tyre: “Won’t the coasts and islands quake at the sound of your downfall

Ezekiel27:14 Those from Beth-togarmah exchanged horses
Ezekiel27:15 Men of Dedan were also your merchants; many coasts and islands were your regular markets. They brought back ivory tusks and ebony as your payment.

Ezekiel27:17 Judah and the land of Israel were your merchants. They exchanged wheat from Minnith
Ezekiel27:18 Damascus was also your trading partner because of your numerous products and your abundant wealth of every kind

Ezekiel27:34 Now you are wrecked by the sea in the depths of the waters; your goods and the people within you have gone down.

Ezekiel40:4 He spoke to me: “Son of man
Ezekiel40:5 Now there was a wall surrounding the outside of the temple. The measuring rod in the man’s hand was six units of twenty-one inches; each unit was the standard length plus three inches. He measured the thickness of the wall structure; it was 10½ feet
Ezekiel40:6 Then he came to the gate that faced east and climbed its steps. He measured the threshold of the gate; it was 10½ feet deep — one threshold was 10½ feet deep.
Ezekiel40:7 Each recess was 10½ feet long and 10½ feet deep

Ezekiel40:13 Then he measured the gate from the roof of one recess to the roof of the opposite one; the distance was 43¾ feet. The openings of the recesses faced each other.
Ezekiel40:14 Next
Ezekiel40:15 The distance from the front of the gate at the entrance to the front of the gate’s portico on the inside was 87½ feet.
Ezekiel40:16 The recesses and their jambs had beveled windows all around the inside of the gate. The porticoes also had windows all around on the inside. Each jamb was decorated with palm trees.

Ezekiel41:1 Next he brought me into the great hall and measured the jambs; on each side the width of the jamb was 10½ feet.
Ezekiel41:2 The width of the entrance was 17½ feet
Ezekiel41:3 He went inside the next room and measured the jambs at the entrance; they were 3½ feet wide. The entrance was 10½ feet wide
Ezekiel41:4 He then measured the length of the room adjacent to the great hall
Ezekiel41:5 Then he measured the wall of the temple; it was 10½ feet thick. The width of the side rooms all around the temple was 7 feet.
Ezekiel41:6 The side rooms were arranged one above another in three stories of thirty rooms each. There were ledges on the wall of the temple all around to serve as supports for the side rooms
Ezekiel41:7 The side rooms surrounding the temple widened at each successive story
Ezekiel41:8 I saw that the temple had a raised platform surrounding it; this foundation for the side rooms was 10½ feet high.
Ezekiel41:9 The thickness of the outer wall of the side rooms was 8¾ feet. The free space between the side rooms of the temple

Ezekiel41:22 The altar was made of wood

Ezekiel44:8 You have not kept charge of my holy things but have appointed others to keep charge of my sanctuary for you.’
Ezekiel44:9 “This is what the Lord GOD says: No foreigner
Ezekiel44:10 Surely the Levites who wandered away from me when Israel went astray
Ezekiel44:11 Yet they will occupy my sanctuary
Ezekiel44:12 Because they ministered to the house of Israel before their idols and became a sinful stumbling block to them
Ezekiel44:13 They must not approach me to serve me as priests or come near any of my holy things or the most holy things. They will bear their disgrace and the consequences of the detestable acts they committed.
Ezekiel44:14 Yet I will make them responsible for the duties of the temple  — for all its work and everything done in it.
Ezekiel44:15 “But the Levitical priests descended from Zadok
Ezekiel44:16 “They are the ones who may enter my sanctuary and approach my table to serve me. They will keep my mandate.
Ezekiel44:17 When they enter the gates of the inner court they are to wear linen garments; they must not have on them anything made of wool when they minister at the gates of the inner court and within it.
Ezekiel44:18 They are to wear linen turbans on their heads and linen undergarments around their waists. They are not to put on anything that makes them sweat.
Ezekiel44:19 Before they go out to the outer court
Ezekiel44:20 “They may not shave their heads or let their hair grow long
Ezekiel44:21 No priest may drink wine before he enters the inner court.
Ezekiel44:22 He is not to marry a widow or a divorced woman
Ezekiel44:23 They are to teach my people the difference between the holy and the common
Ezekiel44:24 “In a dispute
Ezekiel44:25 A priest may not come near a dead person so that he becomes defiled. However
Ezekiel44:26 After he is cleansed
Ezekiel44:27 On the day he goes into the sanctuary
Ezekiel44:28 “This will be their inheritance: I am their inheritance. You are to give them no possession in Israel: I am their possession.
Ezekiel44:29 They will eat the grain offering
Ezekiel44:30 The best of all the firstfruits of every kind and contribution of every kind from all your gifts will belong to the priests. You are to give your first batch of dough to the priest so that a blessing may rest on your homes.
Ezekiel44:31 The priests may not eat any bird or animal that died naturally or was mauled by wild beasts.

Daniel1:1 In the third year of the reign of King Jehoiakim of Judah
Daniel1:2 The Lord handed King Jehoiakim of Judah over to him
Daniel1:3 The king ordered Ashpenaz
Daniel1:4 young men without any physical defect
Daniel1:5 The king assigned them daily provisions from the royal food and from the wine that he drank. They were to be trained for three years
Daniel1:6 Among them
Daniel1:7 The chief eunuch gave them names; he gave the name Belteshazzar to Daniel
Daniel1:8 Daniel determined that he would not defile himself with the king’s food or with the wine he drank. So he asked permission from the chief eunuch not to defile himself.
Daniel1:9 God had granted Daniel kindness and compassion from the chief eunuch
Daniel1:10 yet he said to Daniel
Daniel1:11 So Daniel said to the guard whom the chief eunuch had assigned to Daniel
Daniel1:12 “Please test your servants for ten days. Let us be given vegetables to eat and water to drink.
Daniel1:13 Then examine our appearance and the appearance of the young men who are eating the king’s food
Daniel1:14 He agreed with them about this and tested them for ten days.
Daniel1:15 At the end of ten days they looked better and healthier than all the young men who were eating the king’s food.
Daniel1:16 So the guard continued to remove their food and the wine they were to drink and gave them vegetables.
Daniel1:17 God gave these four young men knowledge and understanding in every kind of literature and wisdom. Daniel also understood visions and dreams of every kind.
Daniel1:18 At the end of the time that the king had said to present them
Daniel1:19 The king interviewed them
Daniel1:20 In every matter of wisdom and understanding that the king consulted them about
Daniel1:21 Daniel remained there until the first year of King Cyrus.

Daniel4:1 King Nebuchadnezzar
Daniel4:2 I am pleased to tell you about the miracles and wonders the Most High God has done for me.

Daniel4:15 But leave the stump with its roots in the ground and with a band of iron and bronze around it in the tender grass of the field. Let him be drenched with dew from the sky and share the plants of the earth with the animals.

Daniel4:17 This word is by decree of the watchers

Daniel5:1 King Belshazzar held a great feast for a thousand of his nobles and drank wine in their presence.
Daniel5:2 Under the influence of the wine
Daniel5:3 So they brought in the gold vessels that had been taken from the temple
Daniel5:4 They drank the wine and praised their gods made of gold and silver
Daniel5:5 At that moment the fingers of a man’s hand appeared and began writing on the plaster of the king’s palace wall next to the lampstand. As the king watched the hand that was writing
Daniel5:6 his face turned pale
Daniel5:7 The king shouted to bring in the mediums
Daniel5:8 So all the king’s wise men came in
Daniel5:9 Then King Belshazzar became even more terrified
Daniel5:10 Because of the outcry of the king and his nobles
Daniel5:11 There is a man in your kingdom who has a spirit of the holy gods in him. In the days of your predecessor he was found to have insight
Daniel5:12 did this because Daniel
Daniel5:13 Then Daniel was brought before the king. The king said to him
Daniel5:14 I’ve heard that you have a spirit of the gods in you
Daniel5:15 Now the wise men and mediums were brought before me to read this inscription and make its interpretation known to me
Daniel5:16 However
Daniel5:17 Then Daniel answered the king
Daniel5:18 Your Majesty
Daniel5:19 Because of the greatness he gave him
Daniel5:20 But when his heart was exalted and his spirit became arrogant
Daniel5:21 He was driven away from people
Daniel5:22 “But you his successor
Daniel5:23 Instead
Daniel5:24 Therefore
Daniel5:25 “This is the writing that was inscribed: MENE
Daniel5:26 This is the interpretation of the message: ‘Mene’ means that God has numbered the days of your kingdom and brought it to an end.
Daniel5:27 ‘Tekel’ means that you have been weighed on the balance and found deficient.
Daniel5:28 ‘Peres’ means that your kingdom has been divided and given to the Medes and Persians.”
Daniel5:29 Then Belshazzar gave an order
Daniel5:30 That very night Belshazzar the king of the Chaldeans was killed
Daniel5:31 and Darius the Mede received the kingdom at the age of sixty-two.

Daniel6:14 As soon as the king heard this

Daniel6:18 Then the king went to his palace and spent the night fasting. No diversions were brought to him
Daniel6:19 At the first light of dawn the king got up and hurried to the lions’ den.
Daniel6:20 When he reached the den

Daniel6:27 He rescues and delivers; he performs signs and wonders in the heavens and on the earth
Daniel6:28 So Daniel prospered during the reign of Darius and the reign of Cyrus the Persian.

Daniel8:2 I saw the vision
Daniel8:3 I looked up
Daniel8:4 I saw the ram charging to the west
Daniel8:5 As I was observing
Daniel8:6 He came toward the two-horned ram I had seen standing beside the canal and rushed at him with savage fury.
Daniel8:7 I saw him approaching the ram
Daniel8:8 Then the male goat acted even more arrogantly
Daniel8:9 From one of them a little horn emerged and grew extensively toward the south and the east and toward the beautiful land.
Daniel8:10 It grew as high as the heavenly army
Daniel8:11 It acted arrogantly even against the Prince of the heavenly army; it revoked his regular sacrifice and overthrew the place of his sanctuary.

Daniel8:13 Then I heard a holy one speaking

Daniel8:18 While he was speaking to me

Daniel8:22 The four horns that took the place of the broken horn represent four kingdoms. They will rise from that nation
Daniel8:23 Near the end of their kingdoms
Daniel8:24 His power will be great
Daniel8:25 He will cause deceit to prosper through his cunning and by his influence
Daniel8:26 The vision of the evenings and the mornings that has been told is true. Now you are to seal up the vision because it refers to many days in the future.”

Daniel11:1 In the first year of Darius the Mede
Daniel11:2 Now I will tell you the truth. “Three more kings will arise in Persia
Daniel11:3 Then a warrior king will arise; he will rule a vast realm and do whatever he wants.
Daniel11:4 But as soon as he is established
Daniel11:5 “The king of the South will grow powerful
Daniel11:6 After some years they will form an alliance
Daniel11:7 In the place of the king of the South
Daniel11:8 He will take even their gods captive to Egypt
Daniel11:9 who will enter the kingdom of the king of the South and then return to his own land.
Daniel11:10 “His sons will mobilize for war and assemble a large number of armed forces. They will advance
Daniel11:11 Infuriated
Daniel11:12 When the army is carried off
Daniel11:13 The king of the North will again raise a multitude larger than the first. After some years he will advance with a great army and many supplies.
Daniel11:14 “In those times many will rise up against the king of the South. Violent ones among your own people will assert themselves to fulfill a vision
Daniel11:15 Then the king of the North will come
Daniel11:16 The king of the North who comes against him will do whatever he wants
Daniel11:17 He will resolve to come with the force of his whole kingdom and will reach an agreement with him. He will give him a daughter in marriage to destroy it
Daniel11:18 Then he will turn his attention to the coasts and islands and capture many. But a commander will put an end to his taunting; instead
Daniel11:19 He will turn his attention back to the fortresses of his own land
Daniel11:20 “In his place one will arise who will send out a tax collector for the glory of the kingdom; but within a few days he will be broken
Daniel11:21 “In his place a despised person will arise; royal honors will not be given to him
Daniel11:22 A flood of forces will be swept away before him; they will be broken
Daniel11:23 After an alliance is made with him
Daniel11:24 During a time of peace
Daniel11:25 “With a large army he will stir up his power and his courage against the king of the South. The king of the South will prepare for battle with an extremely large and powerful army
Daniel11:26 Those who eat his provisions will destroy him; his army will be swept away
Daniel11:27 The two kings
Daniel11:28 The king of the North will return to his land with great wealth
Daniel11:29 “At the appointed time he will come again to the South
Daniel11:30 Ships of Kittim will come against him
Daniel11:31 His forces will rise up and desecrate the temple fortress. They will abolish the regular sacrifice and set up the abomination of desolation.
Daniel11:32 With flattery he will corrupt those who act wickedly toward the covenant
Daniel11:33 Those who have insight among the people will give understanding to many
Daniel11:34 When they fall
Daniel11:35 Some of those who have insight will fall so that they may be refined
Daniel11:36 “Then the king will do whatever he wants. He will exalt and magnify himself above every god
Daniel11:37 He will not show regard for the gods of his fathers
Daniel11:38 Instead
Daniel11:39 He will deal with the strongest fortresses with the help of a foreign god. He will greatly honor those who acknowledge him
Daniel11:40 “At the time of the end
Daniel11:41 He will also invade the beautiful land
Daniel11:42 He will extend his power against the countries
Daniel11:43 He will get control over the hidden treasures of gold and silver and over all the riches of Egypt. The Libyans and Cushites will also be in submission.
Daniel11:44 But reports from the east and the north will terrify him
Daniel11:45 He will pitch his royal tents between the sea and the beautiful holy mountain

Hosea1:1 The word of the LORD that came to Hosea son of Beeri during the reigns of Uzziah
Hosea1:2 When the LORD first spoke to Hosea
Hosea1:3 So he went and married Gomer daughter of Diblaim
Hosea1:4 Then the LORD said to him: Name him Jezreel
Hosea1:5 On that day I will break the bow of Israel in the Valley of Jezreel.
Hosea1:6 She conceived again and gave birth to a daughter
Hosea1:7 But I will have compassion on the house of Judah
Hosea1:8 After Gomer had weaned Lo-ruhamah
Hosea1:9 Then the LORD said: Name him Lo-ammi
Hosea1:10 Yet the number of the Israelites will be like the sand of the sea
Hosea1:11 And the Judeans and the Israelites will be gathered together. They will appoint for themselves a single ruler and go up from the land. For the day of Jezreel will be great.
Hosea2:1 Call your brothers: My People and your sisters: Compassion.
Hosea2:2 Rebuke your mother; rebuke her. For she is not my wife and I am not her husband. Let her remove the promiscuous look from her face and her adultery from between her breasts.
Hosea2:3 Otherwise
Hosea2:4 I will have no compassion on her children because they are the children of promiscuity.
Hosea2:5 Yes
Hosea2:6 Therefore
Hosea2:7 She will pursue her lovers but not catch them; she will look for them but not find them. Then she will think
Hosea2:8 She does not recognize that it is I who gave her the grain
Hosea2:9 Therefore
Hosea2:10 Now I will expose her shame in the sight of her lovers
Hosea2:11 I will put an end to all her celebrations: her feasts
Hosea2:12 I will devastate her vines and fig trees. She thinks that these are her wages that her lovers have given her. I will turn them into a thicket
Hosea2:13 And I will punish her for the days of the Baals
Hosea2:14 Therefore
Hosea2:15 There I will give her vineyards back to her and make the Valley of Achor into a gateway of hope. There she will respond as she did in the days of her youth
Hosea2:16 In that day — this is the LORD’s declaration — you will call me
Hosea2:17 For I will remove the names of the Baals from her mouth; they will no longer be remembered by their names.
Hosea2:18 On that day I will make a covenant for them with the wild animals
Hosea2:19 I will take you to be my wife forever. I will take you to be my wife in righteousness
Hosea2:20 I will take you to be my wife in faithfulness
Hosea2:21 On that day I will respond  — this is the LORD’s declaration. I will respond to the sky
Hosea2:22 The earth will respond to the grain
Hosea2:23 I will sow her in the land for myself

Hosea5:2 Rebels are deeply involved in slaughter; I will be a punishment for all of them.

Hosea5:4 Their actions do not allow them to return to their God
Hosea5:5 Israel’s arrogance testifies against them. Both Israel and Ephraim stumble because of their iniquity; even Judah will stumble with them.

Hosea12:7 A merchant loves to extort with dishonest scales in his hands.

Hosea13:9 I will destroy you

Hosea13:11 I give you a king in my anger and take away a king in my wrath.

Hosea13:13 Labor pains come on him. He is not a wise son; when the time comes
Hosea13:14 I will ransom them from the power of Sheol. I will redeem them from death. Death

Joel1:1 The word of the LORD that came to Joel son of Pethuel:
Joel1:2 Hear this

Joel1:4 What the devouring locust has left
Joel1:5 Wake up
Joel1:6 For a nation has invaded my land
Joel1:7 It has devastated my grapevine and splintered my fig tree. It has stripped off its bark and thrown it away; its branches have turned white.
Joel1:8 Grieve like a young woman dressed in sackcloth
Joel1:9 Grain and drink offerings have been cut off from the house of the LORD; the priests
Joel1:10 The fields are destroyed; the land grieves; indeed
Joel1:11 Be ashamed
Joel1:12 The grapevine is dried up

Joel1:16 Hasn’t the food been cut off before our eyes
Joel1:17 The seeds lie shriveled in their casings. The storehouses are in ruin
Joel1:18 How the animals groan! The herds of cattle wander in confusion since they have no pasture. Even the flocks of sheep and goats suffer punishment.
Joel1:19 I call to you
Joel1:20 Even the wild animals cry out to you
Joel2:1 Blow the horn in Zion; sound the alarm on my holy mountain! Let all the residents of the land tremble

Joel2:3 A fire devours in front of them
Joel2:4 Their appearance is like that of horses
Joel2:5 They bound on the tops of the mountains. Their sound is like the sound of chariots
Joel2:6 Nations writhe in horror before them; all faces turn pale.
Joel2:7 They attack as warriors attack; they scale walls as men of war do. Each goes on his own path
Joel2:8 They do not push each other; each proceeds on his own path. They dodge the arrows
Joel2:9 They storm the city; they run on the wall; they climb into the houses; they enter through the windows like thieves.
Joel2:10 The earth quakes before them; the sky shakes. The sun and moon grow dark
Joel2:11 The LORD makes his voice heard in the presence of his army. His camp is very large; those who carry out his command are powerful. Indeed
Joel2:12 Even now — this is the LORD’s declaration — turn to me with all your heart
Joel2:13 Tear your hearts
Joel2:14 Who knows? He may turn and relent and leave a blessing behind him

Joel2:16 Gather the people; sanctify the congregation; assemble the aged; gather the infants

Joel2:22 Don’t be afraid
Joel2:23 Children of Zion

Joel2:27 You will know that I am present in Israel and that I am the LORD your God
Joel2:28 After this I will pour out my Spirit on all humanity; then your sons and your daughters will prophesy

Amos2:1 The LORD says: I will not relent from punishing Moab for three crimes
Amos2:2 Therefore
Amos2:3 I will cut off the judge from the land and kill all its officials with him. The LORD has spoken.
Amos2:4 The LORD says: I will not relent from punishing Judah for three crimes
Amos2:5 Therefore
Amos2:6 The LORD says: I will not relent from punishing Israel for three crimes
Amos2:7 They trample the heads of the poor on the dust of the ground and obstruct the path of the needy. A man and his father have sexual relations with the same girl
Amos2:8 They stretch out beside every altar on garments taken as collateral
Amos2:9 Yet I destroyed the Amorite as Israel advanced; his height was like the cedars
Amos2:10 And I brought you from the land of Egypt and led you forty years in the wilderness in order to possess the land of the Amorite.
Amos2:11 I raised up some of your sons as prophets and some of your young men as Nazirites. Is this not the case
Amos2:12 But you made the Nazirites drink wine and commanded the prophets
Amos2:13 Look

Amos2:15 The archer will not stand his ground

Amos3:1 Listen to this message that the LORD has spoken against you
Amos3:2 I have known only you out of all the clans of the earth; therefore
Amos3:3 Can two walk together without agreeing to meet?
Amos3:4 Does a lion roar in the forest when it has no prey? Does a young lion growl from its lair unless it has captured something?
Amos3:5 Does a bird land in a trap on the ground if there is no bait for it? Does a trap spring from the ground when it has caught nothing?
Amos3:6 If a ram’s horn is blown in a city
Amos3:7 Indeed
Amos3:8 A lion has roared; who will not fear? The Lord GOD has spoken; who will not prophesy?

Jonah1:1 The word of the LORD came to Jonah son of Amittai:
Jonah1:2 “Get up! Go to the great city of Nineveh and preach against it because their evil has come up before me.”
Jonah1:3 Jonah got up to flee to Tarshish from the LORD’s presence. He went down to Joppa and found a ship going to Tarshish. He paid the fare and went down into it to go with them to Tarshish from the LORD’s presence.
Jonah1:4 But the LORD threw a great wind onto the sea
Jonah1:5 The sailors were afraid
Jonah1:6 The captain approached him and said
Jonah1:7 “Come on!” the sailors said to each other. “Let’s cast lots. Then we’ll know who is to blame for this trouble we’re in.” So they cast lots
Jonah1:8 Then they said to him
Jonah1:9 He answered them
Jonah1:10 Then the men were seized by a great fear and said to him
Jonah1:11 So they said to him
Jonah1:12 He answered them
Jonah1:13 Nevertheless
Jonah1:14 So they called out to the LORD: “Please
Jonah1:15 Then they picked up Jonah and threw him into the sea
Jonah1:16 The men were seized by great fear of the LORD

Jonah2:3 You threw me into the depths
Jonah2:4 But I said
Jonah2:5 The water engulfed me up to the neck; the watery depths overcame me; seaweed was wrapped around my head.
Jonah2:6 I sank to the foundations of the mountains
Jonah2:7 As my life was fading away
Jonah2:8 Those who cherish worthless idols abandon their faithful love
Jonah2:9 but as for me
Jonah2:10 Then the LORD commanded the fish

Jonah3:2 “Get up! Go to the great city of Nineveh and preach the message that I tell you.”
Jonah3:3 Jonah got up and went to Nineveh according to the LORD’s command. Now Nineveh was an extremely great city
Jonah3:4 Jonah set out on the first day of his walk in the city and proclaimed
Jonah3:5 Then the people of Nineveh believed God. They proclaimed a fast and dressed in sackcloth — from the greatest of them to the least.
Jonah3:6 When word reached the king of Nineveh
Jonah3:7 Then he issued a decree in Nineveh: By order of the king and his nobles: No person or animal
Jonah3:8 Furthermore
Jonah3:9 Who knows? God may turn and relent; he may turn from his burning anger so that we will not perish.
Jonah3:10 God saw their actions — that they had turned from their evil ways  — so God relented from the disaster he had threatened them with. And he did not do it.

Micah1:3 Look

Micah1:6 Therefore
Micah1:7 All her carved images will be smashed to pieces; all her wages will be burned in the fire
Micah1:8 Because of this I will lament and wail; I will walk barefoot and naked. I will howl like the jackals and mourn like ostriches.
Micah1:9 For her wound is incurable and has reached even Judah; it has approached my people’s city gate

Micah1:12 Though the residents of Maroth anxiously wait for something good

Micah5:12 I will remove sorceries from your hands
Micah5:13 I will remove your carved images and sacred pillars from you so that you will no longer worship the work of your hands.
Micah5:14 I will pull up the Asherah poles from among you and demolish your cities.
Micah5:15 I will take vengeance in anger and wrath against the nations that have not obeyed me.

Micah7:1 How sad for me! For I am like one who — when the summer fruit has been gathered after the gleaning of the grape harvest  — finds no grape cluster to eat
Micah7:2 Faithful people have vanished from the land; there is no one upright among the people. All of them wait in ambush to shed blood; they hunt each other with a net.
Micah7:3 Both hands are good at accomplishing evil: the official and the judge demand a bribe; when the powerful man communicates his evil desire
Micah7:4 The best of them is like a brier; the most upright is worse than a hedge of thorns. The day of your watchmen
Micah7:5 Do not rely on a friend; don’t trust in a close companion. Seal your mouth from the woman who lies in your arms.

Micah7:9 Because I have sinned against him

Micah7:17 They will lick the dust like a snake; they will come trembling out of their hiding places like reptiles slithering on the ground. They will tremble in the presence of the LORD our God; they will stand in awe of you.

Nahum1:1 The pronouncement concerning Nineveh. The book of the vision of Nahum the Elkoshite.
Nahum1:2 The LORD is a jealous and avenging God; the LORD takes vengeance and is fierce in wrath. The LORD takes vengeance against his foes; he is furious with his enemies.
Nahum1:3 The LORD is slow to anger but great in power; the LORD will never leave the guilty unpunished. His path is in the whirlwind and storm
Nahum1:4 He rebukes the sea and dries it up
Nahum1:5 The mountains quake before him
Nahum1:6 Who can withstand his indignation? Who can endure his burning anger? His wrath is poured out like fire; even rocks are shattered before him.

Nahum2:4 The chariots dash madly through the streets; they rush around in the plazas. They look like torches; they dart back and forth like lightning.

Nahum2:6 The river gates are opened

Habakkuk1:7 They are fierce and terrifying; their views of justice and sovereignty stem from themselves.
Habakkuk1:8 Their horses are swifter than leopards and more fierce than wolves of the night. Their horsemen charge ahead; their horsemen come from distant lands. They fly like eagles
Habakkuk1:9 All of them come to do violence; their faces are set in determination. They gather prisoners like sand.
Habakkuk1:10 They mock kings
Habakkuk1:11 Then they sweep by like the wind and pass through. They are guilty; their strength is their god.

Zechariah8:7 The LORD of Armies says this: “I will save my people from the land of the east and the land of the west.
Zechariah8:8 I will bring them back to live in Jerusalem. They will be my people
Zechariah8:9 The LORD of Armies says this: “Let your hands be strong
Zechariah8:10 For prior to those days neither man nor animal had wages. There was no safety from the enemy for anyone who came or went
Zechariah8:11 But now
Zechariah8:12 “For they will sow in peace: the vine will yield its fruit
Zechariah8:13 As you have been a curse among the nations
Zechariah8:14 For the LORD of Armies says this: “As I resolved to treat you badly when your fathers provoked me to anger
Zechariah8:15 “so I have resolved again in these days to do what is good to Jerusalem and the house of Judah. Don’t be afraid.
Zechariah8:16 These are the things you must do: Speak truth to one another; make true and sound decisions within your city gates.
Zechariah8:17 Do not plot evil in your hearts against your neighbor
Zechariah8:18 Then the word of the LORD of Armies came to me:
Zechariah8:19 The LORD of Armies says this: “The fast of the fourth month
Zechariah8:20 The LORD of Armies says this: “Peoples will yet come
Zechariah8:21 the residents of one city will go to another
Zechariah8:22 Many peoples and strong nations will come to seek the LORD of Armies in Jerusalem and to plead for the LORD’s favor.”
Zechariah8:23 The LORD of Armies says this: “In those days
Zechariah9:1 A pronouncement: The word of the LORD is against the land of Hadrach
Zechariah9:2 and also against Hamath
Zechariah9:3 Tyre has built herself a fortress; she has heaped up silver like dust and gold like the dirt of the streets.
Zechariah9:4 Listen! The Lord will impoverish her and cast her wealth into the sea; she herself will be consumed by fire.
Zechariah9:5 Ashkelon will see it and be afraid; Gaza too
Zechariah9:6 A mongrel people will live in Ashdod
Zechariah9:7 I will remove the blood from their mouths and the abhorrent things from between their teeth. Then they too will become a remnant for our God; they will become like a clan in Judah and Ekron like the Jebusites.
Zechariah9:8 I will encamp at my house as a guard
Zechariah9:9 Rejoice greatly
Zechariah9:10 I will cut off the chariot from Ephraim and the horse from Jerusalem. The bow of war will be removed
Zechariah9:11 As for you
Zechariah9:12 Return to a stronghold
Zechariah9:13 For I will bend Judah as my bow; I will fill that bow with Ephraim. I will rouse your sons
Zechariah9:14 Then the LORD will appear over them
Zechariah9:15 The LORD of Armies will defend them. They will consume and conquer with slingstones; they will drink and be rowdy as if with wine. They will be as full as the sprinkling basin
Zechariah9:16 The LORD their God will save them on that day as the flock of his people; for they are like jewels in a crown
Zechariah9:17 How lovely and beautiful! Grain will make the young men flourish
Zechariah10:1 Ask the LORD for rain in the season of spring rain. The LORD makes the rain clouds
Zechariah10:2 For the idols speak falsehood
Zechariah10:3 My anger burns against the shepherds
Zechariah10:4 The cornerstone will come from Judah. The tent peg will come from them and also the battle bow and every ruler. Together
Zechariah10:5 they will be like warriors in battle trampling down the mud of the streets. They will fight because the LORD is with them
Zechariah10:6 I will strengthen the house of Judah and deliver the house of Joseph. I will restore them because I have compassion on them
Zechariah10:7 Ephraim will be like a warrior
Zechariah10:8 I will whistle and gather them because I have redeemed them; they will be as numerous as they once were.
Zechariah10:9 Though I sow them among the nations
Zechariah10:10 I will bring them back from the land of Egypt and gather them from Assyria. I will bring them to the land of Gilead and to Lebanon
Zechariah10:11 The LORD will pass through the sea of distress and strike the waves of the sea; all the depths of the Nile will dry up. The pride of Assyria will be brought down
Zechariah10:12 I will strengthen them in the LORD
Zechariah11:1 Open your gates
Zechariah11:2 Wail
Zechariah11:3 Listen to the wail of the shepherds
Zechariah11:4 The LORD my God says this: “Shepherd the flock intended for slaughter.
Zechariah11:5 Those who buy them slaughter them but are not punished. Those who sell them say: Blessed be the LORD because I have become rich! Even their own shepherds have no compassion for them.
Zechariah11:6 Indeed
Zechariah11:7 So I shepherded the flock intended for slaughter
Zechariah11:8 In one month I got rid of three shepherds. I became impatient with them
Zechariah11:9 Then I said
Zechariah11:10 Next I took my staff called Favor and cut it in two
Zechariah11:11 It was annulled on that day
Zechariah11:12 Then I said to them
Zechariah11:13 “Throw it to the potter
Zechariah11:14 Then I cut in two my second staff
Zechariah11:15 The LORD also said to me: “Take the equipment of a foolish shepherd.
Zechariah11:16 I am about to raise up a shepherd in the land who will not care for those who are perishing
Zechariah11:17 Woe to the worthless shepherd who deserts the flock! May a sword strike his arm and his right eye! May his arm wither away and his right eye go completely blind!”
Zechariah12:1 A pronouncement: The word of the LORD concerning Israel. A declaration of the LORD
Zechariah12:2 “Look
Zechariah12:3 On that day I will make Jerusalem a heavy stone for all the peoples; all who try to lift it will injure themselves severely when all the nations of the earth gather against her.
Zechariah12:4 On that day” — this is the LORD’s declaration — “I will strike every horse with panic and its rider with madness. I will keep a watchful eye on the house of Judah but strike all the horses of the nations with blindness.
Zechariah12:5 Then each of the leaders of Judah will think to himself: The residents of Jerusalem are my strength through the LORD of Armies
Zechariah12:6 On that day I will make the leaders of Judah like a firepot in a woodpile
Zechariah12:7 The LORD will save the tents of Judah first
Zechariah12:8 On that day the LORD will defend the inhabitants of Jerusalem
Zechariah12:9 On that day I will set out to destroy all the nations that come against Jerusalem.
Zechariah12:10 “Then I will pour out a spirit of grace and prayer on the house of David and the residents of Jerusalem
Zechariah12:11 On that day the mourning in Jerusalem will be as great as the mourning of Hadad-rimmon in the plain of Megiddo.
Zechariah12:12 The land will mourn
Zechariah12:13 the family of Levi’s house by itself and their women by themselves; the family of Shimei by itself and their women by themselves;
Zechariah12:14 all the remaining families
Zechariah13:1 “On that day a fountain will be opened for the house of David and for the residents of Jerusalem
Zechariah13:2 On that day” — this is the declaration of the LORD of Armies — “I will remove the names of the idols from the land
Zechariah13:3 If a man still prophesies
Zechariah13:4 On that day every prophet will be ashamed of his vision when he prophesies; they will not put on a hairy cloak in order to deceive.
Zechariah13:5 He will say: I am not a prophet; I work the land
Zechariah13:6 If someone asks him: What are these wounds on your chest?  — then he will answer: I received the wounds in the house of my friends.
Zechariah13:7 Sword
Zechariah13:8 In the whole land — this is the LORD’s declaration — two-thirds will be cut off and die
Zechariah13:9 I will put this third through the fire; I will refine them as silver is refined and test them as gold is tested. They will call on my name
Zechariah14:1 Look
Zechariah14:2 I will gather all the nations against Jerusalem for battle. The city will be captured
Zechariah14:3 Then the LORD will go out to fight against those nations as he fights on a day of battle.
Zechariah14:4 On that day his feet will stand on the Mount of Olives
Zechariah14:5 You will flee by my mountain valley
Zechariah14:6 On that day there will be no light; the sunlight and moonlight will diminish.
Zechariah14:7 It will be a unique day known only to the LORD
Zechariah14:8 On that day living water will flow out from Jerusalem
Zechariah14:9 On that day the LORD will become King over the whole earth  — the LORD alone
Zechariah14:10 All the land from Geba to Rimmon south of Jerusalem will be changed into a plain. But Jerusalem will be raised up and will remain on its site from the Benjamin Gate to the place of the First Gate
Zechariah14:11 People will live there
Zechariah14:12 This will be the plague with which the LORD strikes all the people who have warred against Jerusalem: their flesh will rot while they stand on their feet
Zechariah14:13 On that day a great panic from the LORD will be among them
Zechariah14:14 Judah will also fight at Jerusalem
Zechariah14:15 The same plague as the previous one will strike the horses
Zechariah14:16 Then all the survivors from the nations that came against Jerusalem will go up year after year to worship the King
Zechariah14:17 Should any of the families of the earth not go up to Jerusalem to worship the King
Zechariah14:18 And if the people of Egypt will not go up and enter
Zechariah14:19 This will be the punishment of Egypt and all the nations that do not go up to celebrate the Festival of Shelters.
Zechariah14:20 On that day
Zechariah14:21 Every pot in Jerusalem and in Judah will be holy to the LORD of Armies. All who sacrifice will come and use the pots to cook in. And on that day there will no longer be a Canaanite in the house of the LORD of Armies.
Malachi1:1 A pronouncement: The word of the LORD to Israel through Malachi.
Malachi1:2 “I have loved you
Malachi1:3 but I hated Esau. I turned his mountains into a wasteland
Malachi1:4 Though Edom says: “We have been devastated
Malachi1:5 Your own eyes will see this
Malachi1:6 “A son honors his father
Malachi1:7 “By presenting defiled food on my altar.” “How have we defiled you?” you ask. When you say: “The LORD’s table is contemptible.”
Malachi1:8 “When you present a blind animal for sacrifice
Malachi1:9 “And now plead for God’s favor. Will he be gracious to us? Since this has come from your hands
Malachi1:10 “I wish one of you would shut the temple doors
Malachi1:11 “My name will be great among the nations
Malachi1:12 “But you are profaning it when you say: ‘The Lord’s table is defiled
Malachi1:13 You also say: ‘Look
Malachi1:14 “The deceiver is cursed who has an acceptable male in his flock and makes a vow but sacrifices a defective animal to the Lord. For I am a great King
Malachi2:1 “Therefore
Malachi2:2 If you don’t listen
Malachi2:3 “Look
Malachi2:4 Then you will know that I sent you this decree
Malachi2:5 “My covenant with him was one of life and peace
Malachi2:6 True instruction was in his mouth
Malachi2:7 For the lips of a priest should guard knowledge
Malachi2:8 “You
Malachi2:9 “So I in turn have made you despised and humiliated before all the people because you are not keeping my ways but are showing partiality in your instruction.”
Malachi2:10 Don’t all of us have one Father? Didn’t one God create us? Why then do we act treacherously against one another
Malachi2:11 Judah has acted treacherously
Malachi2:12 May the LORD cut off from the tents of Jacob the man who does this
Malachi2:13 This is another thing you do. You are covering the LORD’s altar with tears
Malachi2:14 And you ask
Malachi2:15 Didn’t God make them one and give them a portion of spirit? What is the one seeking? Godly offspring. So watch yourselves carefully
Malachi2:16 “If he hates and divorces his wife
Malachi2:17 You have wearied the LORD with your words. Yet you ask
Malachi3:1 “See
Malachi3:2 But who can endure the day of his coming? And who will be able to stand when he appears? For he will be like a refiner’s fire and like launderer’s bleach.
Malachi3:3 He will be like a refiner and purifier of silver; he will purify the sons of Levi and refine them like gold and silver. Then they will present offerings to the LORD in righteousness.
Malachi3:4 And the offerings of Judah and Jerusalem will please the LORD as in days of old and years gone by.
Malachi3:5 “I will come to you in judgment
Malachi3:6 “Because I
Malachi3:7 “Since the days of your fathers
Malachi3:8 “Will a man rob God? Yet you are robbing me!” “How do we rob you?” you ask. “By not making the payments of the tenth and the contributions.
Malachi3:9 You are suffering under a curse
Malachi3:10 Bring the full tenth into the storehouse so that there may be food in my house. Test me in this way
Malachi3:11 I will rebuke the devourer for you
Malachi3:12 “Then all the nations will consider you fortunate
Malachi3:13 “Your words against me are harsh
Malachi3:14 You have said: “It is useless to serve God. What have we gained by keeping his requirements and walking mournfully before the LORD of Armies?
Malachi3:15 So now we consider the arrogant to be fortunate. Not only do those who commit wickedness prosper
Malachi3:16 At that time those who feared the LORD spoke to one another. The LORD took notice and listened. So a book of remembrance was written before him for those who feared the LORD and had high regard for his name.
Malachi3:17 “They will be mine
Malachi3:18 So you will again see the difference between the righteous and the wicked
Malachi4:1 “For look
Malachi4:2 But for you who fear my name
Malachi4:3 You will trample the wicked
Malachi4:4 “Remember the instruction of Moses my servant
Malachi4:5 Look
Malachi4:6 And he will turn the hearts of fathers to their children and the hearts of children to their fathers. Otherwise
Matthew1:1 An account of the genealogy of Jesus Christ
Matthew1:2 Abraham fathered Isaac
Matthew1:3 Judah fathered Perez and Zerah by Tamar
Matthew1:4 Aram fathered Amminadab
Matthew1:5 Salmon fathered Boaz by Rahab
Matthew1:6 and Jesse fathered King David. David fathered Solomon by Uriah’s wife
Matthew1:7 Solomon fathered Rehoboam
Matthew1:8 Asa fathered Jehoshaphat
Matthew1:9 Uzziah fathered Jotham
Matthew1:10 Hezekiah fathered Manasseh
Matthew1:11 and Josiah fathered Jeconiah and his brothers at the time of the exile to Babylon.
Matthew1:12 After the exile to Babylon Jeconiah fathered Shealtiel
Matthew1:13 Zerubbabel fathered Abiud
Matthew1:14 Azor fathered Zadok
Matthew1:15 Eliud fathered Eleazar
Matthew1:16 and Jacob fathered Joseph the husband of Mary
Matthew1:17 So all the generations from Abraham to David were fourteen generations; and from David until the exile to Babylon
Matthew1:18 The birth of Jesus Christ came about this way: After his mother Mary had been engaged to Joseph
Matthew1:19 So her husband Joseph
Matthew1:20 But after he had considered these things
Matthew1:21 She will give birth to a son
Matthew1:22 Now all this took place to fulfill what was spoken by the Lord through the prophet:
Matthew1:23 See
Matthew1:24 When Joseph woke up
Matthew1:25 but did not have sexual relations with her until she gave birth to a son. And he named him Jesus.

Matthew2:2 saying
Matthew2:3 When King Herod heard this
Matthew2:4 So he assembled all the chief priests and scribes of the people and asked them where the Christ would be born.
Matthew2:5 “In Bethlehem of Judea
Matthew2:6 And you
Matthew2:7 Then Herod secretly summoned the wise men and asked them the exact time the star appeared.
Matthew2:8 He sent them to Bethlehem and said
Matthew2:9 After hearing the king
Matthew2:10 When they saw the star
Matthew2:11 Entering the house
Matthew2:12 And being warned in a dream not to go back to Herod
Matthew2:13 After they were gone
Matthew2:14 So he got up
Matthew2:15 He stayed there until Herod’s death
Matthew2:16 Then Herod
Matthew2:17 Then what was spoken through Jeremiah the prophet was fulfilled:
Matthew2:18 A voice was heard in Ramah
Matthew2:19 After Herod died
Matthew2:20 saying
Matthew2:21 So he got up
Matthew2:22 But when he heard that Archelaus was ruling over Judea in place of his father Herod
Matthew2:23 Then he went and settled in a town called Nazareth to fulfill what was spoken through the prophets

Matthew3:2 and saying
Matthew3:3 For he is the one spoken of through the prophet Isaiah
Matthew3:4 Now John had a camel-hair garment with a leather belt around his waist

Matthew3:6 and they were baptized by him in the Jordan River
Matthew3:7 When he saw many of the Pharisees and Sadducees coming to his baptism

Matthew3:15 Jesus answered him
