Matthew5:1 When he saw the crowds
Matthew5:2 Then he began to teach them
Matthew5:3 “Blessed are the poor in spirit
Matthew5:4 Blessed are those who mourn
Matthew5:5 Blessed are the humble
Matthew5:6 Blessed are those who hunger and thirst for righteousness
Matthew5:7 Blessed are the merciful
Matthew5:8 Blessed are the pure in heart
Matthew5:9 Blessed are the peacemakers
Matthew5:10 Blessed are those who are persecuted because of righteousness
Matthew5:11 “You are blessed when they insult you and persecute you and falsely say every kind of evil against you because of me.
Matthew5:12 Be glad and rejoice
Matthew5:13 “You are the salt of the earth. But if the salt should lose its taste
Matthew5:14 “You are the light of the world. A city situated on a hill cannot be hidden.
Matthew5:15 No one lights a lamp and puts it under a basket
Matthew5:16 In the same way
Matthew5:17 “Don’t think that I came to abolish the Law or the Prophets. I did not come to abolish but to fulfill.
Matthew5:18 For truly I tell you
Matthew5:19 Therefore
Matthew5:20 For I tell you
Matthew5:21 “You have heard that it was said to our ancestors
Matthew5:22 But I tell you
Matthew5:23 So if you are offering your gift on the altar
Matthew5:24 leave your gift there in front of the altar. First go and be reconciled with your brother or sister
Matthew5:25 Reach a settlement quickly with your adversary while you’re on the way with him to the court
Matthew5:26 Truly I tell you
Matthew5:27 “You have heard that it was said
Matthew5:28 But I tell you
Matthew5:29 If your right eye causes you to sin
Matthew5:30 And if your right hand causes you to sin
Matthew5:31 “It was also said
Matthew5:32 But I tell you
Matthew5:33 “Again
Matthew5:34 But I tell you
Matthew5:35 or by the earth
Matthew5:36 Do not swear by your head
Matthew5:37 But let your ‘yes’ mean ‘yes
Matthew5:38 “You have heard that it was said
Matthew5:39 But I tell you
Matthew5:40 As for the one who wants to sue you and take away your shirt
Matthew5:41 And if anyone forces you to go one mile
Matthew5:42 Give to the one who asks you
Matthew5:43 “You have heard that it was said
Matthew5:44 But I tell you
Matthew5:45 so that you may be children of your Father in heaven. For he causes his sun to rise on the evil and the good
Matthew5:46 For if you love those who love you
Matthew5:47 And if you greet only your brothers and sisters
Matthew5:48 Be perfect
Matthew6:1 “Be careful not to practice your righteousness in front of others to be seen by them. Otherwise
Matthew6:2 So whenever you give to the poor
Matthew6:3 But when you give to the poor
Matthew6:4 so that your giving may be in secret. And your Father who sees in secret will reward you.
Matthew6:5 “Whenever you pray
Matthew6:6 But when you pray
Matthew6:7 When you pray
Matthew6:8 Don’t be like them
Matthew6:9 “Therefore
Matthew6:10 Your kingdom come. Your will be done on earth as it is in heaven.
Matthew6:11 Give us today our daily bread.
Matthew6:12 And forgive us our debts
Matthew6:13 And do not bring us into temptation
Matthew6:14 “For if you forgive others their offenses
Matthew6:15 But if you don’t forgive others
Matthew6:16 “Whenever you fast
Matthew6:17 But when you fast
Matthew6:18 so that your fasting isn’t obvious to others but to your Father who is in secret. And your Father who sees in secret will reward you.
Matthew6:19 “Don’t store up for yourselves treasures on earth
Matthew6:20 But store up for yourselves treasures in heaven
Matthew6:21 For where your treasure is
Matthew6:22 “The eye is the lamp of the body. If your eye is healthy
Matthew6:23 But if your eye is bad
Matthew6:24 “No one can serve two masters
Matthew6:25 “Therefore I tell you: Don’t worry about your life
Matthew6:26 Consider the birds of the sky: They don’t sow or reap or gather into barns
Matthew6:27 Can any of you add one moment to his life-span by worrying?
Matthew6:28 And why do you worry about clothes? Observe how the wildflowers of the field grow: They don’t labor or spin thread.
Matthew6:29 Yet I tell you that not even Solomon in all his splendor was adorned like one of these.
Matthew6:30 If that’s how God clothes the grass of the field
Matthew6:31 So don’t worry
Matthew6:32 For the Gentiles eagerly seek all these things
Matthew6:33 But seek first the kingdom of God and his righteousness
Matthew6:34 Therefore don’t worry about tomorrow
Matthew7:1 “Do not judge
Matthew7:2 For you will be judged by the same standard with which you judge others
Matthew7:3 Why do you look at the splinter in your brother’s eye but don’t notice the beam of wood in your own eye?
Matthew7:4 Or how can you say to your brother
Matthew7:5 Hypocrite! First take the beam of wood out of your eye
Matthew7:6 Don’t give what is holy to dogs or toss your pearls before pigs
Matthew7:7 “Ask
Matthew7:8 For everyone who asks receives
Matthew7:9 Who among you
Matthew7:10 Or if he asks for a fish
Matthew7:11 If you then
Matthew7:12 Therefore
Matthew7:13 “Enter through the narrow gate. For the gate is wide and the road broad that leads to destruction
Matthew7:14 How narrow is the gate and difficult the road that leads to life
Matthew7:15 “Be on your guard against false prophets who come to you in sheep’s clothing but inwardly are ravaging wolves.
Matthew7:16 You’ll recognize them by their fruit. Are grapes gathered from thornbushes or figs from thistles?
Matthew7:17 In the same way
Matthew7:18 A good tree can’t produce bad fruit; neither can a bad tree produce good fruit.
Matthew7:19 Every tree that doesn’t produce good fruit is cut down and thrown into the fire.
Matthew7:20 So you’ll recognize them by their fruit.
Matthew7:21 “Not everyone who says to me
Matthew7:22 On that day many will say to me
Matthew7:23 Then I will announce to them
Matthew7:24 “Therefore
Matthew7:25 The rain fell
Matthew7:26 But everyone who hears these words of mine and doesn’t act on them will be like a foolish man who built his house on the sand.
Matthew7:27 The rain fell
Matthew7:28 When Jesus had finished saying these things
Matthew7:29 because he was teaching them like one who had authority

Matthew10:1 Summoning his twelve disciples
Matthew10:2 These are the names of the twelve apostles: First
Matthew10:3 Philip and Bartholomew; Thomas and Matthew the tax collector; James the son of Alphaeus
Matthew10:4 Simon the Zealot
Matthew10:5 Jesus sent out these twelve after giving them instructions: “Don’t take the road that leads to the Gentiles
Matthew10:6 Instead
Matthew10:7 As you go
Matthew10:8 Heal the sick
Matthew10:9 Don’t acquire gold
Matthew10:10 Don’t take a traveling bag for the road
Matthew10:11 When you enter any town or village
Matthew10:12 Greet a household when you enter it

Matthew13:21 But he has no root and is short-lived. When distress or persecution comes because of the word

Matthew13:32 It’s the smallest of all the seeds
Matthew13:33 He told them another parable: “The kingdom of heaven is like leaven that a woman took and mixed into fifty pounds of flour until all of it was leavened.”

Matthew24:1 As Jesus left and was going out of the temple

Matthew24:6 You are going to hear of wars and rumors of wars. See that you are not alarmed
Matthew24:7 For nation will rise up against nation
Matthew24:8 All these events are the beginning of labor pains.

Matthew24:10 Then many will fall away
Matthew24:11 Many false prophets will rise up and deceive many.
Matthew24:12 Because lawlessness will multiply
Matthew24:13 But the one who endures to the end will be saved.
Matthew24:14 This good news of the kingdom will be proclaimed in all the world as a testimony to all nations
Matthew24:15 “So when you see the abomination of desolation
Matthew24:16 “then those in Judea must flee to the mountains.
Matthew24:17 A man on the housetop must not come down to get things out of his house
Matthew24:18 and a man in the field must not go back to get his coat.
Matthew24:19 Woe to pregnant women and nursing mothers in those days!
Matthew24:20 Pray that your escape may not be in winter or on a Sabbath.
Matthew24:21 For at that time there will be great distress
Matthew24:22 Unless those days were cut short
Matthew24:23 “If anyone tells you then
Matthew24:24 For false messiahs and false prophets will arise and perform great signs and wonders to lead astray

Matthew24:26 So if they tell you
Matthew24:27 For as the lightning comes from the east and flashes as far as the west
Matthew24:28 Wherever the carcass is
Matthew24:29 “Immediately after the distress of those days
Matthew24:30 Then the sign of the Son of Man will appear in the sky
Matthew24:31 He will send out his angels with a loud trumpet
Matthew24:32 “Learn this lesson from the fig tree: As soon as its branch becomes tender and sprouts leaves
Matthew24:33 In the same way
Matthew24:34 Truly I tell you
Matthew24:35 Heaven and earth will pass away
Matthew24:36 “Now concerning that day and hour no one knows — neither the angels of heaven nor the Son — except the Father alone.
Matthew24:37 As the days of Noah were
Matthew24:38 For in those days before the flood they were eating and drinking
Matthew24:39 They didn’t know until the flood came and swept them all away. This is the way the coming of the Son of Man will be.
Matthew24:40 Then two men will be in the field; one will be taken and one left.
Matthew24:41 Two women will be grinding grain with a hand mill; one will be taken and one left.
Matthew24:42 Therefore be alert
Matthew24:43 But know this: If the homeowner had known what time the thief was coming
Matthew24:44 This is why you are also to be ready
Matthew24:45 “Who then is a faithful and wise servant
Matthew24:46 Blessed is that servant whom the master finds doing his job when he comes.
Matthew24:47 Truly I tell you
Matthew24:48 But if that wicked servant says in his heart
Matthew24:49 and starts to beat his fellow servants
Matthew24:50 that servant’s master will come on a day he does not expect him and at an hour he does not know.
Matthew24:51 He will cut him to pieces and assign him a place with the hypocrites

Matthew27:44 In the same way even the criminals who were crucified with him taunted him.

Matthew27:47 When some of those standing there heard this
Matthew27:48 Immediately one of them ran and got a sponge
Matthew27:49 But the rest said
Matthew27:50 But Jesus cried out again with a loud voice and gave up his spirit.
Matthew27:51 Suddenly

Matthew27:56 Among them were Mary Magdalene
Matthew27:57 When it was evening

Mark1:1 The beginning of the gospel of Jesus Christ
Mark1:2 As it is written in Isaiah the prophet: See
Mark1:3 A voice of one crying out in the wilderness: Prepare the way for the Lord; make his paths straight!
Mark1:4 John came baptizing in the wilderness and proclaiming a baptism of repentance for the forgiveness of sins.
Mark1:5 The whole Judean countryside and all the people of Jerusalem were going out to him
Mark1:6 John wore a camel-hair garment with a leather belt around his waist and ate locusts and wild honey.
Mark1:7 He proclaimed
Mark1:8 I baptize you with water
Mark1:9 In those days Jesus came from Nazareth in Galilee and was baptized in the Jordan by John.
Mark1:10 As soon as he came up out of the water
Mark1:11 And a voice came from heaven: “You are my beloved Son; with you I am well-pleased.”
Mark1:12 Immediately the Spirit drove him into the wilderness.
Mark1:13 He was in the wilderness forty days
Mark1:14 After John was arrested
Mark1:15 “The time is fulfilled
Mark1:16 As he passed alongside the Sea of Galilee
Mark1:17 “Follow me
Mark1:18 Immediately they left their nets and followed him.
Mark1:19 Going on a little farther
Mark1:20 Immediately he called them
Mark1:21 They went into Capernaum
Mark1:22 They were astonished at his teaching because he was teaching them as one who had authority
Mark1:23 Just then a man with an unclean spirit was in their synagogue. He cried out
Mark1:24 “What do you have to do with us
Mark1:25 Jesus rebuked him saying
Mark1:26 And the unclean spirit threw him into convulsions
Mark1:27 They were all amazed
Mark1:28 At once the news about him spread throughout the entire vicinity of Galilee.
Mark1:29 As soon as they left the synagogue
Mark1:30 Simon’s mother-in-law was lying in bed with a fever
Mark1:31 So he went to her
Mark1:32 When evening came
Mark1:33 The whole town was assembled at the door
Mark1:34 and he healed many who were sick with various diseases and drove out many demons. And he would not permit the demons to speak

Mark1:39 He went into all of Galilee

Mark1:45 Yet he went out and began to proclaim it widely and to spread the news

Mark4:17 But they have no root; they are short-lived. When distress or persecution comes because of the word

Luke1:1 Many have undertaken to compile a narrative about the events that have been fulfilled among us
Luke1:2 just as the original eyewitnesses and servants of the word handed them down to us.
Luke1:3 It also seemed good to me
Luke1:4 so that you may know the certainty of the things about which you have been instructed.
Luke1:5 In the days of King Herod of Judea
Luke1:6 Both were righteous in God’s sight
Luke1:7 But they had no children because Elizabeth could not conceive
Luke1:8 When his division was on duty and he was serving as priest before God
Luke1:9 it happened that he was chosen by lot
Luke1:10 At the hour of incense the whole assembly of the people was praying outside.
Luke1:11 An angel of the Lord appeared to him
Luke1:12 When Zechariah saw him
Luke1:13 But the angel said to him: “Do not be afraid
Luke1:14 There will be joy and delight for you
Luke1:15 For he will be great in the sight of the Lord and will never drink wine or beer. He will be filled with the Holy Spirit while still in his mother’s womb.
Luke1:16 He will turn many of the children of Israel to the Lord their God.
Luke1:17 And he will go before him in the spirit and power of Elijah
Luke1:18 “How can I know this?” Zechariah asked the angel. “For I am an old man
Luke1:19 The angel answered him
Luke1:20 Now listen. You will become silent and unable to speak until the day these things take place
Luke1:21 Meanwhile
Luke1:22 When he did come out
Luke1:23 When the days of his ministry were completed
Luke1:24 After these days his wife Elizabeth conceived and kept herself in seclusion for five months. She said
Luke1:25 “The Lord has done this for me. He has looked with favor in these days to take away my disgrace among the people.”
Luke1:26 In the sixth month
Luke1:27 to a virgin engaged to a man named Joseph
Luke1:28 And the angel came to her and said
Luke1:29 But she was deeply troubled by this statement
Luke1:30 Then the angel told her: “Do not be afraid
Luke1:31 Now listen: You will conceive and give birth to a son
Luke1:32 He will be great and will be called the Son of the Most High
Luke1:33 He will reign over the house of Jacob forever
Luke1:34 Mary asked the angel
Luke1:35 The angel replied to her: “The Holy Spirit will come upon you
Luke1:36 And consider your relative Elizabeth — even she has conceived a son in her old age
Luke1:37 For nothing will be impossible with God.”
Luke1:38 “I am the Lord’s servant
Luke1:39 In those days Mary set out and hurried to a town in the hill country of Judah
Luke1:40 where she entered Zechariah’s house and greeted Elizabeth.
Luke1:41 When Elizabeth heard Mary’s greeting
Luke1:42 Then she exclaimed with a loud cry: “Blessed are you among women
Luke1:43 How could this happen to me
Luke1:44 For you see
Luke1:45 Blessed is she who has believed that the Lord would fulfill what he has spoken to her!”
Luke1:46 And Mary said: My soul praises the greatness of the Lord
Luke1:47 and my spirit rejoices in God my Savior
Luke1:48 because he has looked with favor on the humble condition of his servant. Surely
Luke1:49 because the Mighty One has done great things for me
Luke1:50 His mercy is from generation to generation on those who fear him.
Luke1:51 He has done a mighty deed with his arm; he has scattered the proud because of the thoughts of their hearts;
Luke1:52 he has toppled the mighty from their thrones and exalted the lowly.
Luke1:53 He has satisfied the hungry with good things and sent the rich away empty.
Luke1:54 He has helped his servant Israel
Luke1:55 to Abraham and his descendants forever
Luke1:56 And Mary stayed with her about three months; then she returned to her home.
Luke1:57 Now the time had come for Elizabeth to give birth
Luke1:58 Then her neighbors and relatives heard that the Lord had shown her his great mercy

Luke23:42 Then he said

Luke23:49 But all who knew him

John6:61 Jesus

Acts14:13 The priest of Zeus

Acts14:16 In past generations he allowed all the nations to go their own way
Acts14:17 although he did not leave himself without a witness
Acts14:18 Even though they said these things

Acts14:20 After the disciples gathered around him

Acts14:25 After they had spoken the word in Perga
Acts14:26 From there they sailed back to Antioch where they had been commended to the grace of God for the work they had now completed.
Acts14:27 After they arrived and gathered the church together
Acts14:28 And they spent a considerable time with the disciples.
Acts15:1 Some men came down from Judea and began to teach the brothers: “Unless you are circumcised according to the custom prescribed by Moses

Acts15:3 When they had been sent on their way by the church
Acts15:4 When they arrived at Jerusalem

Acts15:8 And God
Acts15:9 He made no distinction between us and them
Acts15:10 Now then
Acts15:11 On the contrary
Acts15:12 The whole assembly became silent and listened to Barnabas and Paul describe all the signs and wonders God had done through them among the Gentiles.
Acts15:13 After they stopped speaking
Acts15:14 Simeon has reported how God first intervened to take from the Gentiles a people for his name.
Acts15:15 And the words of the prophets agree with this
Acts15:16 After these things I will return and rebuild David’s fallen tent. I will rebuild its ruins and set it up again
Acts15:17 so the rest of humanity may seek the Lord — even all the Gentiles who are called by my name — declares the Lord who makes these things
Acts15:18 known from long ago.
Acts15:19 Therefore
Acts15:20 but instead we should write to them to abstain from things polluted by idols
Acts15:21 For since ancient times
Acts15:22 Then the apostles and the elders

Acts15:25 we have unanimously decided to select men and send them to you along with our dearly loved Barnabas and Paul
Acts15:26 who have risked their lives for the name of our Lord Jesus Christ.
Acts15:27 Therefore we have sent Judas and Silas
Acts15:28 For it was the Holy Spirit’s decision — and ours — not to place further burdens on you beyond these requirements:
Acts15:29 that you abstain from food offered to idols
Acts15:30 So they were sent off and went down to Antioch
Acts15:31 When they read it
Acts15:32 Both Judas and Silas

Acts15:39 They had such a sharp disagreement that they parted company
Acts15:40 But Paul chose Silas and departed
Acts15:41 He traveled through Syria and Cilicia
Acts16:1 Paul went on to Derbe and Lystra
Acts16:2 The brothers and sisters at Lystra and Iconium spoke highly of him.

Acts16:4 As they traveled through the towns
Acts16:5 So the churches were strengthened in the faith and grew daily in numbers.

Acts16:7 When they came to Mysia

Acts16:15 After she and her household were baptized

Acts16:18 She did this for many days. Paul was greatly annoyed. Turning to the spirit
Acts16:19 When her owners realized that their hope of profit was gone
Acts16:20 Bringing them before the chief magistrates
Acts16:21 and are promoting customs that are not legal for us as Romans to adopt or practice.”

Acts16:23 After they had severely flogged them

Acts17:6 When they did not find them
Acts17:7 and Jason has welcomed them. They are all acting contrary to Caesar’s decrees

Acts17:16 While Paul was waiting for them in Athens

Acts17:18 Some of the Epicurean and Stoic philosophers also debated with him. Some said
Acts17:19 They took him and brought him to the Areopagus
Acts17:20 Because what you say sounds strange to us
Acts17:21 Now all the Athenians and the foreigners residing there spent their time on nothing else but telling or hearing something new.
Acts17:22 Paul stood in the middle of the Areopagus and said: “People of Athens! I see that you are extremely religious in every respect.
Acts17:23 For as I was passing through and observing the objects of your worship
Acts17:24 The God who made the world and everything in it  — he is Lord of heaven and earth  — does not live in shrines made by hands.
Acts17:25 Neither is he served by human hands
Acts17:26 From one man he has made every nationality to live over the whole earth and has determined their appointed times and the boundaries of where they live.
Acts17:27 He did this so that they might seek God
Acts17:28 For in him we live and move and have our being
Acts17:29 Since we are God’s offspring then
Acts17:30 “Therefore
Acts17:31 because he has set a day when he is going to judge the world in righteousness by the man he has appointed. He has provided proof of this to everyone by raising him from the dead.”
Acts17:32 When they heard about the resurrection of the dead
Acts17:33 So Paul left their presence.

Acts20:27 because I did not avoid declaring to you the whole plan of God.

Acts20:29 I know that after my departure savage wolves will come in among you
Acts20:30 Men will rise up even from your own number and distort the truth to lure the disciples into following them.
Acts20:31 Therefore be on the alert
Acts20:32 “And now I commit you to God and to the word of his grace
Acts20:33 I have not coveted anyone’s silver or gold or clothing.
Acts20:34 You yourselves know that I worked with my own hands to support myself and those who are with me.
Acts20:35 In every way I’ve shown you that it is necessary to help the weak by laboring like this and to remember the words of the Lord Jesus

Acts27:26 But we have to run aground on some island.”

Acts27:28 They took soundings and found it to be a hundred and twenty feet deep; when they had sailed a little farther and sounded again
Acts27:29 Then
Acts27:30 Some sailors tried to escape from the ship; they had let down the skiff into the sea
Acts27:31 Paul said to the centurion and the soldiers
Acts27:32 Then the soldiers cut the ropes holding the skiff and let it drop away.
Acts27:33 When it was about daylight
Acts27:34 So I urge you to take some food. For this is for your survival
Acts27:35 After he said these things and had taken some bread
Acts27:36 They all were encouraged and took food themselves.
Acts27:37 In all there were 276 of us on the ship.
Acts27:38 When they had eaten enough
Acts27:39 When daylight came
Acts27:40 After cutting loose the anchors
Acts27:41 But they struck a sandbar and ran the ship aground. The bow jammed fast and remained immovable
Acts27:42 The soldiers’ plan was to kill the prisoners so that no one could swim away and escape.
Acts27:43 But the centurion kept them from carrying out their plan because he wanted to save Paul
Acts27:44 The rest were to follow
Acts28:1 Once safely ashore
Acts28:2 The local people showed us extraordinary kindness. They lit a fire and took us all in
Acts28:3 As Paul gathered a bundle of brushwood and put it on the fire
Acts28:4 When the local people saw the snake hanging from his hand
Acts28:5 But he shook the snake off into the fire and suffered no harm.
Acts28:6 They expected that he would begin to swell up or suddenly drop dead. After they waited a long time and saw nothing unusual happen to him
Acts28:7 Now in the area around that place was an estate belonging to the leading man of the island
Acts28:8 Publius’s father was in bed suffering from fever and dysentery. Paul went to him
Acts28:9 After this
Acts28:10 So they heaped many honors on us
Acts28:11 After three months we set sail in an Alexandrian ship that had wintered at the island
Acts28:12 Putting in at Syracuse
Acts28:13 From there
Acts28:14 There we found brothers and sisters and were invited to stay a week with them. And so we came to Rome.
Acts28:15 Now the brothers and sisters from there had heard the news about us and had come to meet us as far as the Forum of Appius and the Three Taverns. When Paul saw them

Romans1:22 Claiming to be wise

Romans1:25 They exchanged the truth of God for a lie
Romans1:26 For this reason God delivered them over to disgraceful passions. Their women exchanged natural sexual relations for unnatural ones.
Romans1:27 The men in the same way also left natural relations with women and were inflamed in their lust for one another. Men committed shameless acts with men and received in their own persons the appropriate penalty of their error.
Romans1:28 And because they did not think it worthwhile to acknowledge God
Romans1:29 They are filled with all unrighteousness
Romans1:30 slanderers
Romans1:31 senseless

Romans2:1 Therefore
Romans2:2 We know that God’s judgment on those who do such things is based on the truth.
Romans2:3 Do you really think — anyone of you who judges those who do such things yet do the same — that you will escape God’s judgment?
Romans2:4 Or do you despise the riches of his kindness
Romans2:5 Because of your hardened and unrepentant heart you are storing up wrath for yourself in the day of wrath

Romans2:7 eternal life to those who by persistence in doing good seek glory

Romans2:10 but glory

Romans2:14 So

Galatians1:2 and all the brothers who are with me: To the churches of Galatia.

Galatians1:7 not that there is another gospel
Galatians1:8 But even if we or an angel from heaven should preach to you a gospel contrary to what we have preached to you
Galatians1:9 As we have said before
Galatians1:10 For am I now trying to persuade people
Galatians1:11 For I want you to know
Galatians1:12 For I did not receive it from a human source and I was not taught it
Galatians1:13 For you have heard about my former way of life in Judaism: I intensely persecuted God’s church and tried to destroy it.
Galatians1:14 I advanced in Judaism beyond many contemporaries among my people
Galatians1:15 But when God
Galatians1:16 to reveal his Son in me
Galatians1:17 I did not go up to Jerusalem to those who had become apostles before me; instead I went to Arabia and came back to Damascus.
Galatians1:18 Then after three years I did go up to Jerusalem to get to know Cephas
Galatians1:19 But I didn’t see any of the other apostles except James
Galatians1:20 I declare in the sight of God: I am not lying in what I write to you.
Galatians1:21 Afterward
Galatians1:22 I remained personally unknown to the Judean churches that are in Christ.
Galatians1:23 They simply kept hearing: “He who formerly persecuted us now preaches the faith he once tried to destroy.”
Galatians1:24 And they glorified God because of me.

Galatians2:6 Now from those recognized as important (what they once were makes no difference to me; God does not show favoritism ) — they added nothing to me.
Galatians2:7 On the contrary
Galatians2:8 since the one at work in Peter for an apostleship to the circumcised was also at work in me for the Gentiles.
Galatians2:9 When James
Galatians2:10 They asked only that we would remember the poor

Galatians2:12 For he regularly ate with the Gentiles before certain men came from James. However

Galatians2:14 But when I saw that they were deviating from the truth of the gospel

Galatians3:5 So then
Galatians3:6 just like Abraham who believed God
Galatians3:7 You know

Galatians3:10 For all who rely on the works of the law are under a curse
Galatians3:11 Now it is clear that no one is justified before God by the law
Galatians3:12 But the law is not based on faith; instead

Galatians3:14 The purpose was that the blessing of Abraham would come to the Gentiles by Christ Jesus

Galatians3:16 Now the promises were spoken to Abraham and to his seed. He does not say “and to seeds
Galatians3:17 My point is this: The law
Galatians3:18 For if the inheritance is based on the law
Galatians3:19 Why then was the law given? It was added for the sake of transgressions until the Seed to whom the promise was made would come. The law was put into effect through angels by means of a mediator.
Galatians3:20 Now a mediator is not just for one person alone
Galatians3:21 Is the law therefore contrary to God’s promises? Absolutely not! For if the law had been granted with the ability to give life
Galatians3:22 But the Scripture imprisoned everything under sin’s power
Galatians3:23 Before this faith came
Galatians3:24 The law
Galatians3:25 But since that faith has come
Galatians3:26 for through faith you are all sons of God in Christ Jesus.
Galatians3:27 For those of you who were baptized into Christ have been clothed with Christ.
Galatians3:28 There is no Jew or Greek

Galatians4:14 You did not despise or reject me though my physical condition was a trial for you. On the contrary
Galatians4:15 Where
Galatians4:16 So then
Galatians4:17 They court you eagerly
Galatians4:18 But it is always good to be pursued in a good manner — and not just when I am with you.

Galatians4:20 I would like to be with you right now and change my tone of voice

Galatians4:23 But the one by the slave was born as a result of the flesh
Galatians4:24 These things are being taken figuratively
Galatians4:25 Now Hagar represents Mount Sinai in Arabia and corresponds to the present Jerusalem
Galatians4:26 But the Jerusalem above is free
Galatians4:27 For it is written

Galatians5:1 For freedom
Galatians5:2 Take note! I
Galatians5:3 Again I testify to every man who gets himself circumcised that he is obligated to do the entire law.
Galatians5:4 You who are trying to be justified by the law are alienated from Christ; you have fallen from grace.
Galatians5:5 For we eagerly await through the Spirit
Galatians5:6 For in Christ Jesus neither circumcision nor uncircumcision accomplishes anything; what matters is faith working through love.
Galatians5:7 You were running well. Who prevented you from being persuaded regarding the truth?
Galatians5:8 This persuasion does not come from the one who calls you.
Galatians5:9 A little leaven leavens the whole batch of dough.
Galatians5:10 I myself am persuaded in the Lord you will not accept any other view. But whoever it is that is confusing you will pay the penalty.
Galatians5:11 Now brothers and sisters
Galatians5:12 I wish those who are disturbing you might also let themselves be mutilated!
Galatians5:13 For you were called to be free
Galatians5:14 For the whole law is fulfilled in one statement: Love your neighbor as yourself.
Galatians5:15 But if you bite and devour one another
Galatians5:16 I say then
Galatians5:17 For the flesh desires what is against the Spirit
Galatians5:18 But if you are led by the Spirit
Galatians5:19 Now the works of the flesh are obvious: sexual immorality
Galatians5:20 idolatry
Galatians5:21 envy
Galatians5:22 But the fruit of the Spirit is love
Galatians5:23 gentleness
Galatians5:24 Now those who belong to Christ Jesus have crucified the flesh with its passions and desires.
Galatians5:25 If we live by the Spirit
Galatians5:26 Let us not become conceited

Galatians6:2 Carry one another’s burdens; in this way you will fulfill the law of Christ.
Galatians6:3 For if anyone considers himself to be something when he is nothing
Galatians6:4 Let each person examine his own work
Galatians6:5 For each person will have to carry his own load.
Galatians6:6 Let the one who is taught the word share all his good things with the teacher.
Galatians6:7 Don’t be deceived: God is not mocked. For whatever a person sows he will also reap
Galatians6:8 because the one who sows to his flesh will reap destruction from the flesh
Galatians6:9 Let us not get tired of doing good
Galatians6:10 Therefore
Galatians6:11 Look at what large letters I use as I write to you in my own handwriting.

Galatians6:14 But as for me

Ephesians1:10 as a plan for the right time  — to bring everything together in Christ

Hebrews1:3 The Son is the radiance of God’s glory and the exact expression of his nature
Hebrews1:4 So he became superior to the angels
Hebrews1:5 For to which of the angels did he ever say
Hebrews1:6 Again
Hebrews1:7 And about the angels he says: He makes his angels winds
Hebrews1:8 but to the Son: Your throne
Hebrews1:9 You have loved righteousness and hated lawlessness; this is why God
Hebrews1:10 And: In the beginning
Hebrews1:11 they will perish
Hebrews1:12 you will roll them up like a cloak
Hebrews1:13 Now to which of the angels has he ever said: Sit at my right hand until I make your enemies your footstool?
Hebrews1:14 Are they not all ministering spirits sent out to serve those who are going to inherit salvation?
Hebrews2:1 For this reason
Hebrews2:2 For if the message spoken through angels was legally binding and every transgression and disobedience received a just punishment
Hebrews2:3 how will we escape if we neglect such a great salvation? This salvation had its beginning when it was spoken of by the Lord
Hebrews2:4 At the same time
Hebrews2:5 For he has not subjected to angels the world to come that we are talking about.
Hebrews2:6 But someone somewhere has testified: What is man that you remember him
Hebrews2:7 You made him lower than the angels for a short time; you crowned him with glory and honor
Hebrews2:8 and subjected everything under his feet. For in subjecting everything to him

2Peter1:5 For this very reason

2Peter1:7 godliness with brotherly affection

1John3:2 Dear friends
1John3:3 And everyone who has this hope in him purifies himself just as he is pure.
1John3:4 Everyone who commits sin practices lawlessness; and sin is lawlessness.
1John3:5 You know that he was revealed so that he might take away sins
1John3:6 Everyone who remains in him does not sin; everyone who sins has not seen him or known him.
1John3:7 Children
1John3:8 The one who commits sin is of the devil
1John3:9 Everyone who has been born of God does not sin
1John3:10 This is how God’s children and the devil’s children become obvious. Whoever does not do what is right is not of God
1John3:11 For this is the message you have heard from the beginning: We should love one another
1John3:12 unlike Cain
1John3:13 Do not be surprised
1John3:14 We know that we have passed from death to life because we love our brothers and sisters. The one who does not love remains in death.
1John3:15 Everyone who hates his brother or sister is a murderer
1John3:16 This is how we have come to know love: He laid down his life for us. We should also lay down our lives for our brothers and sisters.
1John3:17 If anyone has this world’s goods and sees a fellow believer in need but withholds compassion from him — how does God’s love reside in him?
1John3:18 Little children
1John3:19 This is how we will know that we belong to the truth and will reassure our hearts before him
1John3:20 whenever our hearts condemn us; for God is greater than our hearts
